CHAPTER 716 .
AN ACT to allow the voters of Ballard county to vote on the question of
removing the county seatof Ballard county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That in making out the poll-books for the various
voting precinctsin Ballard county, for the purpose of
recording the votes of the county ,at the election to be

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 43
heldonthefirstMonday in August, 1880,the clerkshall
preparetwo columns in each and every poll-book ,for the
purposeof ascertainingthedesireof the people of said
countyin relationto removing the county seat of said
countyfrom Blandvilleto Wickliffein said county; the
firstcolumn shall be headed " For removal ," the second
"Againstremoval ;" and in taking the vote at said elec-
tion,thequestionshallbe distinctlyput to everyqualified
voter,"Are you for or against the removal of the county
seatfrom Blandville to Wickliffe ?" and the clerk of the
electionshall record the vote in accordance with the an-
swer .
§2.That the vote on saidquestionshallbe kept,re-
cordedand counted , and the resultmade known at the
sametime,and in the same manner ,and by the same
officers,as is now requiredby law in the electionof
countyofficers.
3.That if a majority of allthe votes cast be against
theremoval,then no furtherstepsshallbe taken in the
matter;but if a majorityof all the legalvotes cast at
saidelectionand on saidquestionbe forthe removal,
then,assoon as the necessary officesand a court-house
canbe builtat Wickliffe,the books and records of the
countyshallbe transferredthereto,and the various courts
ofthecountynow held at the county seatshallbe held
atWickliffe, and Wickliffe shall be thereafterthe county
seatofBallard county .
§4.That the county courtof saidcounty shallappoint
threepersons,who shallbe known as the Commissioners
ofPublicBuildingsfor the county,and who shallgive
bondforthe faithfulperformance of the dutieson them
imposed,in a sum not to exceeding thirtythousand dol-
lars,tobe approvedby the court; and thedutiesof the
commissionersin relationto said buildingsshallbe pre-
scribedby said court.
§5.That allacts and parts of acts inconsistentwith
theprovisionsof thisactareherebyrepealed.
6.This act shall take effect and be in force from and
afteritspassage.
Approved April 1, 1880 .

CHAPTER 717 .

rialdistrict,of Henry county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . Any person being the owner of stock, or having
the custody or control thereof, who shall sufferor permit
the same to run at large,or to be outside of his or her
own inclosure or premises under his or her charge , shall
be finedin any amount not lessthan one nor more than
twenty dollars,to be recovered by warrant in the name of
the Commonwealth before any justiceof the peace or
policejudge withinsaiddistrict: Provided, The owner of
such stock had notice that said stock was so at large .
§2 . Any person whose premises or property may be
injured by stock running at large with the knowledge or
permission of the owner , shall have a right of action
against any owner of such stock, notwithstandingthe
premises so injuredmay not be inclosedby a lawful
fence as now required by law ,for any damages he may
have sustained; and may also have a lien upon the stock
so trespassingto satisfyany judgment he may recover ;
and may alsohave executionleviedupon saidstock,and
the same sold in satisfactionthereof,notwithstanding it
may be exempt from saidlevy and saleunder the present
laws of the State .
3. Should any stock be sold as provided in the pre-
ceding section,the amount realizedtherefor shallfirstgo
to satisfythe amount of the execution, including costs,
and the remainder be paid over to the owner of such
stock .
§4 . Any person finding any stock on his premises , or
on premises under his control,shallhave the right to take
up the same , and hold them as estrays are now by law
taken up and held.
§5 . Whenever stock of any kind shallbe found run
ning at large on any of the public roads of said district,
or on any of the streetsor alleys of any of the towns
therein, or on the premises of another without his con

untiltheowner or owners of such stock shall have re-
deemedthemby the payment of allfees,costs,and proper
chargesincidentto such procedure .
§6.Thefeesto any of the above named officersforthe
servicesrequiredof him by the preceding section shallbe
fiftycentsfor each head of stock so taken up and im-
poundedbyhim ,and allreasonablecostsand chargesfor
keepingthem while they may remain so impounded ,all
ofwhichshallbe paid by the owner or claimant of said

withintwodays thereafter give notice to the owner of the

chargesdue; and if the same isnot reclaimedby the
ownerwithinfive days from said notice,the officerhav-
ingthecustodythereof shall advertise the same at two
ormorepublicplaces in said district,by posting notices;
andunlessthey shall be reclaimed by the owner ,within
fivedaysafterthe postingof said advertisement,itshall
bethedutyof such officerto expose them to saleat pub
licauction,atsome publicplacein the saiddistrictmost
convenientto the owner . Said sale shall be made on a
creditofthree months ; the officermaking the sale shall
takebond,with sufficientsurety, forthe price,payable to
himself,which shallhave the forceof a judgment ; and
afterhaving satisfiedall costs and charges incident to

itwas sold.

fail,orrefuseto perform any of the dutiesimposed upon

thepeacein said district,or any policejudge thereof,
shallbe finedin any sum not lessthanone dollarnor

more than ten dollarsfor each act of neglect or failureor

§ 9. The officersherein named ,and theirsureties,shall

tionsof duty imposed upon them by thisactin not pay-
ing over to owners of stock any and allmoneys to which
theymay be entitledby reason of any provisionor sec-

CHAPTER 719 .
AN ACT toincorporatetheKentucky and IndianaBridgeCompany .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Bennett H. Young , St.John Boyle, Bluford
Wilson , Charles Godshaw , E. F. Trabue ,and their suc-
cessorsand associates,are hereby constitutedand declared
a body -corporate forever, under the name of the Ken-
tucky and Indiana Bridge Company ; with power to sue
and be sued ,contract and be contracted with ,and with
allotherpowers ,rights,and privilegesincidentto corpo-
rations .
§2 . Said Kentucky and Indiana Bridge Company is
hereby empowered , and to locate, build, construct,and
maintain ,under the laws of the United States,a bridge
forrailway,wagon ,streetrailway,and allotherpurposes,
between the citiesof Louisville,Kentucky ,and New Al-
bany ,in the Stateof Indiana,from any convenient and

accessiblepointwithin the limitsof the cityof Louis-
ville,orwithin one mile thereof, to any point in the city
ofNew Albany , Indiana ,or within one mile thereof,and
may,ifdesired,crossover Sand Island,or any bar above
orbelowsaidisland; and saidcompany isherebyclothed
withallthe powers ,privileges,rights,and franchisesnec-
essaryforthecarryingout the purposes named herein;
andisempowered to purchase,lease,or condemn allthe
realestatethat may be necessary forthe purposes of said
corporation, whether for piers, approaches , tracks, toll-
houses, or approaches leading to the same , and shall
haveallrightsand powers for the condemnation of prop-
ertyasmay be necessaryand requisiteto securethe pur-
posesofthisact. Whenever said company shalldesire
tocondemn any property for the purposes named in this

shallbecarriedon,as near as may be,as actionsat law
byordinaryproceedings . Warning orders against non
residents,absentdefendants,or unknown owners ofprop-
erty,must be publishedthree times in one of the daily
paperspublishedin Louisville, the last publicationat
leasttendays before the trial. The owners of distinct
partsof one contiguous tractmay all be included in one
proceeding, or any one or more of them holding con
tiguoustractsmay be proceeded againstin a separate
action. These causes shallbe triedby juries, as other
casesare triedin said courts,and shallhave precedence
uponthe docket as soon as the parties are before the
courtsand the issues are made up . The jurors shall be
sworntrulyand impartiallyto ascertainand determine,
bytheirverdict,the amount of compensation each owner
willbe entitledto if hisland or propertydescribedin
thepetitionis condemned . The court in which these
proceedingsare brought shallhave power to assign a
dayforthetrialof the case as soon as the petitionis
filed; andupon thereturnof theverdict,the courtshall
enterjudgment,vestingtitleto thepropertydescribedin

52 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
said corporation,said judgment to take effectupon the
payment intocourt by the said corporation of the amount
of money named in the verdict. If the said amount so
named in the verdict shallnot be paid within thirtydays
from the rendering of said judginent , the said proceed-
ings shallbe dismissedwithout prejudiceto any subse-
quent proceeding .
§3 . Said corporationshallhave the power to laydown

poration, and itsofficersand employes .

ofthetime and place of meeting, publishedin some
Louisvillepaper,shallmeet and electa board of direct-
ors;and saidcorporationshallthen be entitledto com-

§9. Thisactishereby declareda publicact,and shall
takeeffectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 1, 1880.

CHAPTER 722 .
AN ACT forthebenefitof Isham Cottingham,of Hendersoncounty.
WHEREAS , The tax-books of Henderson county forthe
years 1873, 1874, and 1875 were placed in the hand of
Isham Cottingham under a settlement made August 1st,
1878,by and between D. Howard Smith , Auditor,James
B. McCreary ,Governor, and Thomas E. Moss ,Attorney
General , for the State of Kentucky ,and Isham Cotting-
ham ,of Henderson county . This settlement was made
in pursuance of an act of the Legislature, approved
March 29th , 1878 , entitled " An act to authorize the
Auditor , with the assent of the Attorney General and
Governor , to compromise with defaulting sheriffsand
collectorsof the public revenue and theirsureties." By
the terms of this settlement , all the taxes found in the
books above -mentioned were transferred and conveyed
to the said Cottingham , with the privilegeof collecting
the same , and appropriating to his own use not more
than five thousand dollars of the amount collected ; there-
fore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Isham Cottingham be authorized and em-
powered to appoint a collectorto collectthe uncollected
taxes of Henderson county for the years 1873, 1874, and
1875,and that such collectorshall, with sufficientsure .
ties, execute to the Commonwealth a bond for the faith-
fulperformance of his duty , thisbond to be taken in the
same manner ,and with the same penaltiesattached,as
is now provided in case of other collectorsof the public
revenue .
§2 . The collectorshall have the authority to appoint
as many deputies as may seem necessary for the collec-
tion of the taxes mentioned in section one of this act .
These deputies are required to give bond , with the same
penalties annexed , as now provided in case of other
deputy collectingofficersof the public revenue .
§3 . The collectorand hisdeputiesshallhave the same
authority and rights to enforce the collectionof these

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 63
taxes,and be subjectedto the same penaltiesas other
collectorsof the State revenue , and shall pay over to
Isham Cottingham the amounts collected every three
months,and shallreport to him every three months their
actsand deeds done under this act .
§4. Should any executive or collectingofficerof Hen-
dersoncountybe appointedcollectoras provided herein,
nothingin this act shall be construed to be inconsistent
with his duties as such officer .
§5. The collector shall be allowed not more than
twentyper cent. for his services; and this act to take
effectfrom and afterits passage .
Approved April 1, 1880,

CHAPTER 732 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof owners of sheep killedby dogs, and to tax the
owners and controllersof dogs fortheirbenefit,in Bath county.
WHEREAS , Large numbers of valuable sheep are annu-
allykilled in Bath county by ravenous dogs ,to the great
lossof theownersthereof; wherefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That in the county of Bath there shall be assessed
and collectedfrom the owner or controllerof any num-
ber of dogs above one an annual tax of one dollar upon
each dog ; and any person being a housekeeper or a
landlord shall be liable to such assessment and tax for all
dogs exceeding one for each family.
§2 . That itshallbe the duty of the assessorof Bath
county to make and return to the county court of said
county ,with his annual tax lists,the names of the per-
sons and the number of dogs so liablefor taxation.
§3 . That it shallbe the duty of the sheriffof Bath
county to collectthe said tax at the same time and in the
same manner as he is required by law to collect the
county levy,and he shallbe allowed the same compensa-
tion therefor, and shall hold and pay over said tax so
collectedas shallbe required of him by the court of claims
of said county.
§4 . That the court of claims of said county , at the
annual meeting thereof,shall ascertain and declare the
number and value of any sheep killedby dogs in said
county during the preceding year ,and the owners there-
of, and afterpaying to the assessora fee of fivecents for
each dog so listedfor taxation by him , shall distribute
the proceeds of said taxes pro rata to said persons so

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 7.5
declaredto have lostsheep by dog -killing,not to exceed
thefullvalue of such losses.
§5.That in case the assessorshallfailto listany per-
sonliablefor said dog tax, it shall be the duty of the
sheriffof saidcounty to listand collectsuch taxes, and
reportthe same to said court, in the same manner as
requiredforcounty levytax omittedby the assessor.
6.This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage; and all other acts imposing a tax upon dogs in
Bathcountyare hereby repealed .
Approved April 2, 1880 .

CHAPTER 739 .
AN ACT in relationto the county levy and ad valorem tax in Boyd county ,
and legalizingthe actionof the court of levy of January , 1880.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the action of the Boyd county court at court
of levy in January, 1880,fixingthe polland ad valorem
tax for 1880,ishereby legalized.
§2 . That the court of levy may levy an ad valorem
tax for the year 1881,to be collectedin the year 1881 as
other taxes, an ad valorem tax of not exceeding fifty
cents on each one hundred dollars of property liable to
taxation for State revenue . If said court shall levy a
sum exceeding fortycents on the one hundred dollars,the
poli-tax shallbe fixed at three dollars; if the tax shallbe
over thirtycents, and not over forty cents, the poll-tax
shall not be less than two dollars ; if the tax shall be less
than thirtycents,the poll-tax shall not be lessthan one
dollar .
§3 . This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 2, 1880.

CHAPTER 765 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the Campbell Turnpike Road Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the charterof the Campbell Turnpike Road
Company ,of Campbell county,Ky .,be,and the same is,
so amended as to authorize said company to construct,
operate,and maintain along the line of their turnpike
road a telephone line from Newport to Alexandria , in
saidcounty,and the said company is hereby so author-
ized and empowered .
§2 . The said company shallkeep at each of itstoll-
houses, and also at some publicplace in Newport and
Alexandria , telephone instruments connected with said
line, which said instruments shall at all reasonable times
be open to the public forthe transmission of messages ; and
the said company may charge forthe use of said linea sum
not toexceed fifteencentsa message : Provided, however,
That if more than ten minutes are required in sending a
message ,then the company may charge , in excess of said
fifteencents,a sum not exceeding five cents for each
additionalfiveminutes ,or fractionpart they require.
§3 . Said company may contract with any person or
corporation for the furnishing of them with telephonic
communication in connection with said line ; and for
thatpurpose may constructany branch lineswithin said
county .
§ 4. Said company may acquire rightsof way for such
branch linesin the same way provided by law for turn-
pike companies in acquiringrightsof way forturnpike
roads .
§5 . Said company may contractwith any other tele-
phone company for connectionor continuationof lines,
or for retransmission of messages .
§ 6. This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 2 , 1880.

CHAPTER 771 .
AN ACT to improve and keep in order the turnpikeroads of Logan county.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be the duty of the Logan county
court annually to appropriate so much of the poll-tax
levied by the county court of said county as may be nec .
essaryto put in good orderand keep in good repair the
turnpike roads now constructed in the said county : Pro-
vided, That it is not intended by thisact to release the
hands now belonging to the differentdistrictsalong the

districtsshall concur ,by a majority vote of said board ,

of the times and manner of such appropriations.

repealed, so far as such conflictmay exist,and no fur-
ther .

CHAPTER 772 .
AN ACT for the benefit of the owners of the grounds of the West Louisville
Cemetery .
WHEREAS ,It has been represented.to this General As-
sembly that the use of the grounds of the West Louisville
Cemetery , a corporation chartered by act of the General
Assembly,has long been discontinuedforburialpurposes,
and the rightsof lot-holderspurchased in by the orphan
ayslums , who are devisees of Cromie ,one of the original

corporatorsand grantors; and whereas,itisfurtherrep
resentedthat the bodies heretofore interred in said ceme-
terygroundshave been removed to othercemeteries,with
theexceptionof those interred in a single lot,which lot
has been,by decree of the Louisvillechancery court,set
apart,with an avenue of ingress and egress to the owner
thereofby inclosure, as appears in the record of the suit
filedby the said lot-owner ; and whereas ,the said ceme-
terypropertyis situate within the city limits of Louis-
ville, and it is desirable that the same be partitioned
amongthose entitled; now , therefore,
Beitenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
ofKentucky:

signeesof lots or certificatestherefor ,in the West Louis-
villeCemeteryCompany ,or any one or more of them ,to
filea petitionin the Louisvillechancery court for the

pany,and for a partitionof the lands thereof among the

claiminginterestor titleshallbe made partiesplaintiffs
ordefendants; and upon trialof the same ,the courtmay
adjudgediscontinuanceof the cemetery,and partitionof

chancerycourt,and her rights,asdefinedby saiddecree,
shallnotbe affectedby any judgment renderedunder
thisact.
§4.Ifitappearto the court, in theprogressof any
trialhadinaccordancewith thisact,thatany bodiesin-
terredinthelandssoughttobe partitionedhave notbeen
removedand re-interredin other cemeteries,the court
mayprovide,in itsjudgment ,forthe decent exhumation
andre-interment of such bodies.
Thisactshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 2, 1880

CHAPTER 809 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Mill Springs National Cemetery Decoration
Society, of Pulaski county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That W. F. Scott,J. W. F. Parker ,T. Z. Morrow ,
Solomon Weddle , Charles B. Porch , Joseph Sumpter ,G.
B. Buchanan , Perry Weddle , John Hall, H. G. Trimble ,
John Delacroy ,F. V. Logan , Henry Lemert , D. W. Rus-
sell,sr., and their successors, be , and they are hereby ,
created a body -politicand corporate,under the name and
styleof the " MillSpringsNationalCemetery Decoration
Society, of Pulaskicounty," with power to acquire, by
purchase,donation,or lease,a suitabletractor parcelof
land ,not to exceed ten acres,convenient to the national
cemetery ,to be used as a meeting ground for those who
attend saidcemetery on " decorationday." The society
may organizeat any time afterthe passage of thisact by
choosingone of theirnumber president. They may also
appoint a secretaryand treasurer. The duties of the
treasurershall be to receive allmoneys belonging to the
society, obtained by subscriptionor otherwise, and he
shallpay thesame out by orderof the society.
§2. The societyshallhave power to make and enforce
such by -laws and regulationsas willbe necessary to pre-
serve order on the grounds or about the cemetery on
decorationday, and shallhave power to appoint such
officersas will be necessary to enforce the observance of
good order : Provided,That no by -law or regulation shall
be adopted in contraventionto the Constitutionand laws
of the State .
§3 . It shallbe unlawful for any one to give, sell, or
loan any spirituous,vinous,or malt liquors, or a mixture

of either,within two miles of said cemetery on decora-
tionday. Any one violatingthissectionshallbe subject
to indictmentbefore a grand jury, and fined as persons
are forviolationof the local option law .

afteritspassage .
Approved April 6 , 1880.

CHAPTER 842 .
AN ACT toempower and authorizethemembersof PalmyraChristian
Church,inHenry county, tosell,disposeof, and conveysaidproperty.
WHEREAS, The members of the Palmyra Christian
Churchdesireto sellthe church property owned by
them,becauseitis fastgoing to decay, and they are

204 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
not able repair it and keep it in repair; and the church
issituatedin an inconvenient place ; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the members of the Palmyra Christian Church
be,and they are hereby,empowered to selland convey
the said lot and church property .
§2 . That L. M. Sanford and E. J. Tyler be,and they
are hereby,empowered to conveyand to holdthe pro-
ceeds thereof,subject to the conditions of the trust, until
the congregationof said church,by proper proceedings,
otherwise disposes of the same .
§3 . That this act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved April 8, 1880 .

CHAPTER 852 .
AN ACT forthebenefitof S. M. Peacock, of Garrardcounty.
WHEREAS , At a specialterm of the Garrard circuitcourt
for the trialof Grove C. Kennedy ,charged with murder ,
the sheriff, being a witnessand forotherreasons,would
not act,his honor,J. C. Wickliffe,appointed S. M. Pea-
cock to act as sheriff, and the said Peacock did act in
that capacityfor thirteendays, as is evidenced by an
order of said court allowing him three dollarsper day for

# LAWS OF KENTUCKY. 231

that length of time, and there being no law authorizing the Auditor to pay this claim; therefore,

*Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky:*

§ 1. That the Auditor of Public Accounts be, and he is hereby, authorized to draw his warrant on the Treasury for the sum of thirty-nine dollars for the benefit of said Peacock.

§ 2. This act shall take effect from its passage.

Approved April 8, 1880.

## CHAPTER 853.

AN ACT for the benefit of John W. White, late sheriff of McLean county.

*Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky:*

§ 1. That the Auditor of Public Accounts be, and he is hereby, directed to issue his warrant on the Treasury in favor of J. W. White, late sheriff of McLean county, for seventy-five dollars, or so much thereof as may be found due him for delinquent colored tithes and exonerations, upon a just settlement of his accounts as sheriff of McLean county with the Auditor, not exceeding the sum of seventy-five dollars.

§ 2. This act shall take effect from its passage.

Approved April 8, 1880.

---

## CHAPTER 854.

AN ACT to relieve the Bourbon County Agricultural and Mechanical Society from paying county or other local taxes.

*Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky:*

§ 1. That the real and personal property of the Bourbon County Agricultural Society be exempt from all taxation; either county or municipal. The said act to include taxes for the year 1879, and to be in force and effect from its passage.

Approved April 8, 1880.

CHAPTER 866 .
AN ACT authorizingthe Boyd county court to apply the localtax on Chat-
taroiRailway Company's road and property in payment of expenditures
forrightof way to a limitedamount .
WHEREAS , The people of Boyd county were deeply in-
terested in having the Chattaroi Railway built through
said county ; and itwas proposed to secure for the rail-
way company the rightof way ,free of cost; but it was
found that this could not be done by voluntary grant of
the land -owners ; a few refused to give the right of way ,
and in some instances the owners were infants ,and could
not. It was then proposed that the railway company
should procure the right of way by purchase and con-
demnation ,neither the people nor county court having a
right to condemn lands; that for the amount paid for
such right of way , including cost of condemnation and
interestthereon,the county court of said county should
issuebonds,to be paid out of thelocaltax to which the
road and company's property within the county might
become liable. This propositionwas accepted by the
Chattaroi Railway Company ; therefore,in order to ena-
ble the county court of Boyd county to carry out said
agreement ,
Be itenuctedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Boyd county is author-
ized to ascertainthe amount paid, and that may be paid,
by the ChattaroiRailway Company for the rightof way
for itsrailroadthrough the county of Boyd , including the
costofcondemnation,and therespectivedateswhen paid;
thatthe county court, at its court of claims, shall enter
upon itsorder-book the amount so ascertained,and that
the local tax upon the road and company's property

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 243
withinthecounty of Boyd ,when the same shallbecome
liableto local tax for county purposes ,shall be applied
tothe payment of said amounts and the interestthereon,
not,however ,exceeding in the aggregate ,including inter-
est,the sum of seven thousand dollars.
§2. That itshallbe the duty of the county court,when
theroad and company's propertybecomes liableto the
paymentof local taxes to have the same set apart and
appliedas above provided ,and no furtherobligationshall
be imposed upon the county than isherein declared.
§3. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 8, 1880.

CHAPTER 878 .
AN ACT to provide forthe reformationof a certainclassof prostitutesin
the cityof Louisville.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the judge of the citycourt of Louisvilleshall
have the jurisdictionand power ,when thereare present-
ed to him girlsbetween the ages of sixteen and eighteen
years, residentsof the cityof Louisville, charged with
being common bawds or prostitutes,to hear and deter-
mine their cases; and , upon satisfactoryproof that they
are such common bawds or prostitutes, and that, from
theirhabits and character, there is no probabilitythat
they will reform and lead virtuous lives,he shallmake an
order of court committing them to the care of the Sisters
of the Good Shepherd in their Instituteof St.Xavier for
the reformation of viciouswomen in said city; and said
bawds or prostitutesshallremain in said instituteuntil
they attain the age of twenty -one years .
§2 . Any girlso committed as providedin sectionone,
shall remain at said Institute of St. Xavier until she at-
tains the age of twenty -one years; and shall,during the
time of her commitment ,be subjectto the rulesand orders
governing said institution.
§3. The Mother Superior,or otherofficeror person in
charge of said institution,shall have the power to dis-
charge any girlcommitted to the same before she attains
the age of twenty -one years.

dollarsnor more than two hundred dollars,on warrant of
arrestbefore the citycourt of Louisville.
§ 5. This act shall take effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 8, 1880.

CHAPTER 888 .
AN ACT to incorporateScience HillAcademy , of Taylor county .
WHEREAS , The voters under the school law , and the
patrons residing in school districtNo. 4,in Taylor coun-
ty, desiringto elevate the standard of education in said
district, and realizing that the facilitiesoffered by the
present school system are inadequate to the accomplish-
ment of thispurpose ,have improved the old building and
erected a new one in conjunction therewith, at an ex-
pense of about a thousand dollars,on the lotof ground

268 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
that was donated for school purposes by J. Cowherd , and
have had in successfuloperation for the last few years a
schoolof high order, and are now desirousof perpetu-
ating the institutionand of putting it on a firm basis by
harmonizing any apparentlyconflictinginterest; there-
fore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That James W. Edwards ,Benj . O. Durritt, W. H.
Cowherd , their associates and successors , be , and are
hereby, constituteda body-politicand corporate, under
the name and styleof the Science Hill Academy ; and by
thatname they shallhave perpetualsuccession, with
power to adopt and use a common seal,and change the
same at pleasure; to make contractsforthe benefitof
said academy ; to sue and be sued,to plead and be im-
pleaded,in any of the courtsof thisCommonwealth ; to
acquire and hold, by gift, grant, devise, purchase, or
otherwise, any real or personal propertyfor the use of
said company , not to exceed five thousand dollars in
value .
§2 . That the capital stock of said academy be divided
into shares of twenty ($ 20) dollarseach ; and the trustees
may , from time to time,as necessitymay require, issue
shares additional to those already issued,but in no case
to exceed fivethousand dollars; and the amount of stock
subscribed shallbe entered of record in a book to be kept
by the secretary,and no stock shallbe transferablefrom
one person to another except the transferbe entered on
such book .
§3 . That the titlein and to the said house and lot,and
the entire control of the same ,be lodged in the hands of
said trustees,to be used forthe purpose of education ,and
none other ; that the above named trustees hold their office
tillthe firstSaturday in May ,1880,at which time,and on
the same day each succeeding year , an electionshall be
held. That at the firstelection there shallbe elected by
the stockholders,who shallbe allowed one vote for every
share of stock held by them , five trustees,one of whom ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 269
to be determinedby lot,shallhold his officefiveyears,
another four, another three, another two , and another
one ; and on the firstSaturday in May of each year there-
after the stockholders shall elect one trustee to fill the
place of him whose term expires on that day .
§ 4. That said board of trustees,at every election,shall
select out of their own number a chairman ,whose duties
itshallbe to preside at each meeting of the board , and
thathe have power to convene the board at any time he
may deem proper ,by giving reasonable notice; and that
the said board shall select a secretary annually ,whose
duty itshallbe tokeep a record of alltheproceedings; and
thatsaidboard have the power to employ a principal,
and make such arrangements as may seem best for the
success of the school,and that they be clothed with gen-
eral supervisory powers over the school; to hear and de-
termineallcomplaints,and to expelany scholarforgood
and sufficientreasons ; that should any vacancies occur
in the said board ,that they be filledby election by the
stockholders; and that itbe the duty of the chairman , in
addition to his other duties ,to make out a census each
year of the number of common school scholars in said
district,and to make such reportsof thatportionof the
schoolas may be requiredunder the schoollaw.
§ 5. That a uniform rate of tuition for the different
grades be charged and collected on each scholar that
attends: Provided,however, That those pupilswithinthe
district, and of age fixed by the school law , and who
attend the said school,shallreceivea creditby theirper
cupitaof the school fund .
§6 . That the trusteesshallhave power ,from time to
time , to establishsuch by laws ,rulesand regulations,not
inconsistent with this charter , the Constitution and laws
of the State,or of the United States,as they may deem
necessary for the supervision and government of said
academy . The propertyof said corporationshallbe ex-
empt from taxation.
§7 . That this act shall be in force from and after its
passage .
Approved April 8 , 1880.

CHAPTER 904 .
AN ACT to establishan additionalvoting and magisterialdistrictin Martin
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That there ishereby establishedan additional vot-
ing and magisterial districtin Martin county , bounded as
follows,viz : Beginning at the mouth of Wolf creek, on
Tugg river,on the lower side of said Wolf creek; thence
up the pointtothetopof the hillbetween Wolf creekand
Buck creek ; thence with the top of the ridge to the head
of LittleRockcastle fork of Wolf creek, so as to include
all the citizens on Little Rockcastle to its mouth ; thence
down main Wolf creek to the mouth of the Pigeon
Roost fork of said creek; thence up the point on the
lower sideof said Pigeon Roost fork to the top of the hill
between the said fork and the Emily of Wolf creek;
thence with the top of the hillto the county line at the
head of the Bent branch of Big creek; thence with the
county line to the mouth of Big creek; thence down the
riverto the beginning; and the voting place in saiddis-
trictishereby located at the mouth of the Long branch .
§3 . This act to take effectfrom and after itspassage .
Approved April 8 , 1880 .

CHAPTER 914 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Harp Turnpike Road Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That a company may be formed ,under the name
and styleof the Harp Turnpike Road Company , in Fay-
ette and Bourbon counties,for the purpose of construct-
ing a turnpikeroad from the pointwhere the Russell'sdirt
road intersectsthe Lexington and Cynthiana Turnpike;
thence along the line of said dirtroad to the Devore lane;
thence along line of said Devore lane to the Hurst place;
thence in an easterlydirection,through said Hurst farm,
to the line between the Brooks farm and the Bagg farm,
or in suchdirectionto the Lexington and MaysvilleTurn-
pike Road at such pointas may be agreed upon by the
board of directors. Said corporation,when formed ,shall
have allthe power and authorityincident to such corpo-
rations .
§2 . That the capital stock of said company shall not
exceed fifteenthousand dollars, and shall be divided into
shares of fiftydollarseach .
§3 . That Jas.C. Wallace,John J.Piper,Robt.Devore,
Lewis Harp ,George Harp , and Jas.Hurst are appointed
commissioners,whose duty itshallbe to procurea book
in which the subscribersof stockin said company shall
enter into the following obligation: We ,whose names
are hereto subscribed, do bind ourselvesto pay to the
Harp Turnpike Road the sums of money set opposite
ournames,in suchproportion,and atsuchtime,asshall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 319
be requiredby the presidentand directorsof said com-
pany .
§ 4. As soon as a sufficient amount of stock has been
subscribed or secured to build one half of said road , it
shallbe the duty of said commissioners to give notice of
a meeting of the stockholders of said company , at some
convenientpoint,forthe purpose of electingofficersfor
saidcompany ; and at said meeting at leasttwo of said
commissioners shallbe present and superintend the elec-
tion .
§5 . There shall be elected a president and fivedirect-
ors,who shallhold theirofficefor one year from the day
of election, and until their successors are duly elected
and qualified . The said board of directors shall elect a
treasurer, and such other officersas they may deem nec-
essary,who shall,beforeenteringupon hisor theirduties,
give such bond as said board may require.
§ 6. The board of directorsshallhave the power to fill
allvacancies that may occur in their body ; and the elec-
tion for president and directors, after the firstelection,
shallbe the firstSaturday in April of each year .
§7 . That said company may sue and be sued,plead
and be impleaded ,contract and be contracted with ,and
in alllitigationshallbe dealt with as a natural person .
§ 8. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 9, 1880.

ertybelonging to the company or corporation shallbe held
by the board of directorsin trustforthe use and benefit of

cersas theyshalldeem necessary, who shall hold their

CHAPTER 920 .

CHAPTER 932 .

of Kentucky:
§1 . That the board of trustees of common school dis-
trictNo. 1,in the town of Grayson ,Carter county,Ken-
tucky,are hereby authorized to issue the bonds of said
districtto any amount ,not exceeding five thousand dol-
lars,forthe purpose of erectingwithin saiddistrictsuch
buildingor buildings as may be deemed suitable for
schoolpurposes.

ingsixper cent. per annum , which interestshall be
paidsemi-annually,and shallbe issuedin such denom-
inationsas said board of trustees may deem proper , not
exceedingone thousand dollarseach ,running not longer
thanten years ,and shall be redeemable at the pleasure
of theboard of trusteesat any time aftertwo years.
§3 . That the fund hereby created shall vest in the
board of trusteesof said district; and they shall be re-
quiredto give a good and sufficientbond to the county
courtfor the faithfulperformance of their duties; and
theyshall have power , with the fund hereby created,
toacquire such real estate as they may deem necessary ,
and erect thereon a building or buildings suitable for
schoolpurposes; and may eithercash the bonds or pay
them , or such proportion of them as may be agreed upon ,
to such persons as they may purchase realestate from ,or
to the contractoror contractor'sworkmen who may erect
the buildingherein provided,or may use them in any
way deemed proper to carryout the provisionsofthisact.
And in erecting or contracting said building or buildings,
the trusteesof said districtare hereby prohibited from
being one of the contractors or workmen , eitherdirectly
or indirectly.
§4 . The board of trusteesshallannually,by some suit-
ablepersonappointed by them ,make an annual asssess-
ment of all the real,personal, and residuary estate and
propertyin said district; and are hereby authorizedto
levy,a tax to any amount not exceedingfiftycentsupon

352 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
each one hundred dollars'worth of such property. The
collection of the tax shall be had in the manner that
isprovidedby law . The money thus raisedshallbe ap-
plied exclusivelyto the payment of the interestupon
the bonds herein authorized to be issued , and to their
redemption at maturity,or at the optionof the board of
trustees: Provided, however, That only the property of
white persons shallbe taxed for the purpose of thisact.
§5 . The board of trusteesshallhave supervisionand
directionof all matters pertaining to this act ; and are
hereby authorized to appoint such agents as they may ,
from time to time ,deem necessary to carry out the pro-
visions of this act
§ 6. The trusteesmay acquireany property,forthispur-
pose herein set forth,by donation ,gift,or otherwise .
§ 7. This act and the provisionsthereof shallnot be in
forceuntilratifiedby a majorityof the patronsof school
districtNo. 1,of Carter county; and forthe purpose of
ascertainingthe willof the people of said district,the
trusteesof said districtare hereby required to cause an
election held at their school-house , on the firstMon-
day in May next,to take the vote upon the adoptionor
rejectionof thisact. If a majorityvoteforitsadoption,
then this act shall be in full force .
Approved April 9, 1880.

CHAPTER 935 .
AN ACT inrelationto the Louisville, New Albany and St. Louis Railway
Company .

of Kentucky:
§1.That the Louisville, New Albany and St. Louis
RailwayCompany , a corporationformed and existing
underthelaws of the States of Indiana and Illinois,be ,
anditishereby, made a corporate-body within this Com-
monwealth,with allthe rightsand privileges,and subject
toallthedutiesand liabilitiesconferred or imposed upon
otherrailroadcompanies incorporatedby the laws of this
Commonwealth .

andoperatea railroad in the county of Jefferson, and in
thecityof Louisville, from the terminus of any bridge
now or hereafter built across the Ohio river, or from any
railroadtransferlanding on the said riverto any station,
ortoa junctionwith any otherrailroadinthe saidcounty
orcity; and may purchase , lease, or make running ar
rangementswith the owners of any such railroad; and
saidcompany may carry and transferitslocomotives,
cars,freight, and passengers across the Ohio river; it
beingthe intentand purpose of thisact to enable and
authorizethesaidcompany to build,own ,and operatea
lineofrailwayfrom some convenient point in the said
cityof Louisvilleto any such bridge or transferlanding ;
and so as to connect the same with a line of railway in
theStateof Indiana by bridgeor rivertransfer; and the
saidcompany may establishand constructsuch depots,
sidings,turn-tables,shops,and officesas may be deemed
expedient; and it may purchase ,lease,or otherwise ac-
quirepropertyforthe saidrailroaddepots,sidings,shops,
offices,and otherpurposesofthesaidcompany ; andfor
theseobjectsmay condemn propertyby proceedingsin
theJeffersoncourtof common pleasor Louisvillechan-
cerycourt; and in such proceedings the owner or owners ,
or claimantsof the property sought to be condemned ,
shallbe made defendants ,and be summoned as in other
actions. The actionsshallbe triedby a jury,unless a

356 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
jurybe waived; and the proceedingsshallbe according
to thepracticein cases of a traverseof the returnupon
a writ of ad quod damnum .
§3 . The saidcompany may issueand sellitsnegotiable
bonds ,bearing a rate of interestnot exceeding seven per
centum per annum ,payable at such time and place as it
may determine ; and may secure the same or other obli-
gations by a mortgage upon the property, rights, and
franchisesof the said company within this State, either
separatelyor in connection with the property out of

§ 4. The saidcompany shallhave the rightto buildor

CHAPTER 936 .

WHEREAS , At the December term , 1879 , of the Frank-
lincriminal court, in a prosecution therein pending be-
tween the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,plaintiff,and F.
J. Wilson ,defendant ,charged with arson; and whereas ,

duly sworn ,and entered upon the discharge of his duties
and summoned a venireof (70) citizensand house-keep-
ersof the county of Franklin,and (19) nineteenwitnesses;
and whereas,a jury was acceptedand sworn to trysaid
issueof guiltyor not guilty, and the trialgone into,

manded into the immediate custody of the said J. W.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 357
Gaines,elisoras aforesaid,who ,duringthatperiod,con-

CHAPTER 938 .

Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Adolph Rentlinger,Ferdinand Voigt,Joseph
J. Fischer,Gotheb Layer , and Adam Schuster, their asso-
ciatesand successors, be ,and they are hereby ,created a
body-politicand corporate, under the name and styleof
the Voigt Patent Water -wheel Company ; and by that
name , and as such body-corporate, are empowered to
contract and be contracted with; to sue and be sued , to
have and use a common seal, and to alterand renew the
same ; to lease and purchase real estate to the extent
of fiftythousand dollars in value, and the same to hold ,
sell,exchange,and convey; to employ agents; to borrow
money as itsbusiness may require, and to secure the
same by pledge of its real or personal estate; to make ,
execute,and amend such by -laws as may be necessary or
convenient for its government , and not contrary to the
law of Kentucky or of the United States.
§2 . That said corporation is incorporated for the pur-
pose of purchasing the patent rightfrom the patentee of
the wheel known as the Voigt Patent Water -wheel , man-
ufacturingsaid water -wheel ,and sellingthe same ; selling
throughout the United States territorialrights to manu-
facture and sellthe same ; receiving royaltiesfor the
manufacture and use of said wheel .
§3 . That the capitalstockof said company shall be
$ 100.000 , divided into 500 shares of $ 200 each .
§ 4. That the incorporators hereinbefore named shall
constitute the firstboard of directors, who shall elect a
president,vice president,and secretary; at the firstmeet-
ing of the stockholdersthey shallelectfrom theirnumber
a directoryconsisting of five members , who shall elect
from theirnumber a president, vice president, and secre-
tary ; that said board of directors and said officers shall
remain in officeone year , or until their successors are
elected and enter upon their said office. In allelections
each share of stock shall entitle the holder to one vote ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 359
which may be cast in person or by written proxy . It
shallbe the duty of the directorsto manage and control
allthe affairsof the corporation,and to submit to the an
nual meeting of the stockholders a full report of the
management and condition of the company ; and they
shallhave the power to fillby appointment any vacancy
that may occur in theirboard .
§5 . The shares of stock shall be deemed personal es-
tate, and be transferableon the books of the company ,
and the stock shall be paid in such installments and at
such times as the board of directorsmay determine ; and
fordefault of payment ,the company may ,by itsby -laws ,
authorize the forfeiture of stock .
§6 That said corporation shall go into operation as
soon as the said incorporatorsshalldetermine a sufficient
amount of the capitalstockto have been subscribedand
paid.
§7 . That this act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 9 , 1880 .

CHAPTER 950 .

of Kentucky :
§1 .That the town of Caneyville,in Grayson county ,be ,

Joseph Oller'sand A. A. Kemper's; thence from Kemp-
er's a northwest course to Cicero Wilson's ; thence to G.
E. Porter's a northwest course ; thence north to S. W.
Bond's , on the north side of Caney creek; thence a north
courseto J.A. Whitton's; thencetoV. B. Roman's,the
beginning .

town shallbe vested in three trustees,who shallbe elected

town . No person shall be eligibleto the officeof trus-
tee except a resident and freeholderof said town . Said

wealthupon corporate bodies of a like character; may

pleasure. They shall be known by the name and style
oftheBoard of Trustees of the town of Caneyville.
§5. The said trustees, afterbeing sworn to discharge

chairmantopresideover theirdeliberations,and he shall

CHAPTER 951 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled" An act to incorporateClark'sRiver
Grange,No 228, Patronsof Husbandry, of Callowaycounty."
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That J. M. Smith , T. C. Edwards , W. F. Smith ,
H. M. Fartner,Z. T. Rule , H. B. Miller, D. Bean ,C. C.
Smith , W. H. Watson , S. J. Smith , M. C. Wilson , L. P.
Wilson,M. S. Smith,officersofClark'sRiverGrange ,No.
228 ,Patrons of Husbandry , and theirsuccessors,be ,and
they are hereby ,created a body -corporate, by the name
and styleof Clark'sRiver Grange ,No. 228, Patrons of
Husbandry .
§2 . And they and theirsuccessorsshallcontinueand
have perpetualsuccession,and by that name are made
capable in law as natural persons to sue and be sued ,
plead and be impleaded ,contract and be contracted with ,
answer and be answered in law and all courts of law and
equity in the Commonwealth or elsewhere ; to make ,
have ,and use a common seal,and alter or amend the
same at pleasure.
§3 . They may make and ordain regulations and by-
laws for their government : Provided, They be not in
contravention with the constitutionand by -laws of the
National Grange of Patrons of Husbandry ,nor in con-
travention of the Constitution and laws of this State or
United States .
§ 4. That saidcorporationshallhave power and author-
ityto acquire and hold realor personal estate,not exceed-
ing twenty thousand dollars in value at one time , and
from time to time ,if deemed expedient,to sell and con-
vey same ,or any partthereof,and re-investand disposeof
the proceeds .
§5 . No privatepropertyof any member shallbe held
responsibleforthe corporate debts.
§ 6. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 9, 1880 .

CHAPTER 954 .

county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
§1 . That the corporate limitsof the town of Versailles,
in Woodford county, Kentucky , be, and the same are
hereby , so changed and enlarged as to reach and extend
over that portion of Woodford county embraced in the
following boundaries , to-wit : Beginning at a point in

yards from the center of the square in Versailleson

a circleto a point in the Versaillesand McCoun's Ferry
Turnpike Road ,one thousandyardsfrom thecenterofsaid
square; thencein a circleto a pointintheVersaillesand
NicholasvilleTurnpike Road , one thousand yards from
the center of the square aforesaid; thence in a circleto
a point in the Frankfort, Lexington, and Versailles
Turnpike Road ,east of Versailles,eight hundred and forty
yards from the center of the above -named square ; thence
to a point in the same pike,on the north sideof Ver-
sailles, eighthundred and fortyyards from the center of
said square ; thence in a circleto the beginning .
§2 . That the board of trustees of the town of Ver-
saillesshallemploy the county surveyor of Woodford
county to run off,fix,and mark the corporatelimitsof
saidtown ,in accordance with the provisionsof the first
section of this act ; and when the limits and boundaries
fixedand marked by him shallhave been approved and
adopted by saidtrustees,itshallbe conclusiveproof that

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 385
theyarefixedin accordance with thisact; and saidtrus-
teesshallpay said surveyor for his services out of any
money in,or which may be in, the treasury of the town .
§3 . That all acts of the General Assembly heretofore
passed concerning all laws of this State applicable to,
and allpowers ,rights, and privilegesheretofore granted
tothetown of Versailles, or any of itsofficers, agents, or
servants, shallapply, be in force, and have the same

thetown limitsof saidtown ,that are used foragricultural,
horticultural,orpasturelands,shallbe taxedformunicipal
purposesuntilsaidpropertyshallbe laidoffby the own-
ers thereof into town lots .

Approved April 13, 1880,

CHAPTER 956 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Bagdad , Jacksonville, and Benson Turnpike
Company .
Be ilenactedby theGeneral Assemblyof theCommonwealth of
Kentucky :
§1 . That Thomas D. Kesler, W. S. Scroggin , J. P.
Pryor ,William Moore ,sr., and Samuel Miles, and their
associates and theirsuccessors,be, and they are hereby ,
createda body -politicand corporate and board of directors,
with perpetual succession,under the name and style of
" The Bagdad ,Jacksonville,and Benson ValleyTurnpike
Company ;" the objectbeingto buildand equip a macad-
amized turnpike road from the town of Bagdad ,in Shelby
county,by the way of Jacksonville,in the same county,
and Benson Valley, in Franklin county, to the city of
Frankfort,in the lattercounty ; and said board of direct-
ors shallhave power to contract and be contracted with ,
sue and be sued ,to acquire,hold, and transfer property ,
real and personal, for the purposes herein stated; said
propertynot to exceed the sum of twenty-fivethousand
dollars in value .
§2 . The board of directorsshall have power to open
stock books and obtain subscriptionsof stock for the pur-
pose of raisingfunds wherewith to build said turnpike
road, the shares of said stockto be twenty-fivedollars
each, payable at such times and places, and in such
amounts ,as said board may direct.
§ 3. The stockholdersof said company shallhold an
annual meeting for the election of a board of directors,
who shall hold their officefor the term of one year ,or
until their successors are elected and installed .
§ 4. The board of directorsshall organize by electing
from theirnumber a president,and from among the stock-
holdersa secretaryand treasurer, who shallhold their
officesforone year,or untiltheirsuccessorsare elected
and installed .
§ 5. Itshallbe the duty of the presidentto callthe
board togetherat such times and places as the affairsof
the company may demand ; presideover itsdeliberations,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 387
and do allthat he can to push the road through to com-
pletion; and he shallreceive as compensation an amount
sufficientto cover his actual expenses while engaged in
theserviceof the company ,said amount to be determined
and certifiedto by the board of directors.
§ 6. Itshallbe the duty of the secretary and treasurer
tokeepthebooks of subscriptionafterthestockshallhave
been subscribed; he shall also keep a record of all the
receiptsand disbursementsof the company ,with vouch-
ersforevery transaction,and be ready to make ,and shall
make , fullreports of the same at least once a year ,or
whenever the board of directorsor the stockholdersmay
callforthe same ; he shallalsokeep a fullrecord of the
proceedings of the board of directorsat theirregular and
calledmeetings ,and of those of the stockholders at their
annual meetings; and the compensation of said secretary
and treasurershallbe not lessthan fiftydollarsnor more
one hundred dollarsper annum - the exact amount to le
determined by the board of directers according to the
amount of actual service .
§7 . After two consecutive miles of said road shall have
been built, one toll-gate may be erected across it,and for
every additionalfive(5) miles of road finishedby the
company , they may erect an additional toll-gate across
said road ; but the rate of toll shall not exceed that fixed
by general law , and may be regulated by law .
§ 8. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved April 13,1880.

nue and county levy, and shall pay over allmoneys thus
collectedto the county treasurerof said county ,lessthe

CHAPTER 977 .
AN ACT to authorizethe votersof Ballard county to vote on the question
of theremovalof the county seat, and providing forthe buildinga court-
house,and repealingformer act on same subject.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
ofKentucky:
§1. That it shall be the duty of the Ballard county
courtto order the sheriffof said county to open a poll at
eachof the votingprecinctsof said county on Saturday,
the2d day of May ,1880,to take the sense of the qualified
votersof saidcounty on the questionof removal of the
countyseatfrom Blandville to Wickliffe,in said county .
And the said county court shall appoint the officersto
holdsaidelection; and in making said appointments,to
havedue regardto the generallaws on thatsubject.
§2. That in making out the poll-books for the various
votingplaces,for the purpose of taking the vote on said
question,the clerk shallprepare one column in which to
record the votes of those who are in favor of the removal
ofthecountyseattoWickliffe,and thepoll-books shallbe
deliveredby the sheriffto the several clerks of the elec-
tion. The clerkof saidelectionshallpropound to each
individualvoter voting at said election the question,
" Are you foror against the removal of the county seat
from Blandville to Wickliffe ? " And if said voter answers
affirmatively, then his vote shallbe so recorded;and ifhe
answers negatively,then no entry shallbe made of it on
saidpoll-book .
§3.That thepoll-book shallbe keptsealedby theclerk
ofsaidelectionas requiredof them by the general laws
on thesubjectof elections,shallbe deliveredto the clerk
ofthecountycourtby thesheriffofsaidelection,and the
officerswhosedutyitistocountand compare thevotein
LOC , L., VOL . 11-27

418 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
case of electionsshall,on Wednesday aftersaid election,
open saidpoll-books and countand compare thepolls,and
make known the result in the same manner as is now
required by law in the electionof county officers.
§ 4. That if a majority of all the qualified voters of
the county vote for the removal of the county seat to
Wickliffe, then,in that event, the court-house and public
officesshallbe erected at Wickliffe,and the books ,papers ,
and recordsof the county shallbe removed there, and
the various courts of the county shall be held there, and
it shall thereafterbe the county seat of said county ;
and in order to ascertainwhether a majority of all the
qualifiedvotersof the county are in favor of the removal ,
the officerscomparing the pollsshallreferto the assessors'
books ,and count thereon each individual assessed for the
purpose of county levyor poll-tax,and from that result
determine whether the majority have voted for the re-
moval .
§5 . That in case a majority of the legally qualified
voters of the county vote for the removal of the county
seat to Wickliffe,then the acts passed at thissession,en-
titled" An act to authorize the Ballard county court to
re-build court- house in Blandville,and levy an ad valorem
tax forthatpurpose," and " An actto authorizethe Bal-
lard county court to rebuild court-house in Blandville,
and issuebonds for that purpose," shallapply to and be
in effectfor the purpose of building the court-house at
Wickliffe .
§6 . That the act passed at this session, entitled" An
act to allow the voters of Ballard county to vote on the
question of removing the county seat of Ballard county ,"
be ,and the same is hereby ,repealed
§7. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.

CHAPTER 979 .
AN ACT authorizingthe improvement of the Fountain Ferry Road , in
Jefferson county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assemblyof theCommonwealth of
Kentucky:
§1. That the Fountain Ferry Road ,in Jeffersoncounty ,
commencingat the western lineof the cityof Louisville
and extending to the Ohio river,or as near thereto as the
commissionersnamed in this act may direct, shallbe im-
provedtwenty feetwide by macadamizing or graveling,
orboth,at the cost,expense , and charge of the owner or
owners of land within fifteen hundred feet each side of
saidroad authorizedto be improved by thisact. The
saidroad shallbe graded and shaped for a proper drain-
age,with all necessary culverts and bridges for public
travel, and when completed , the county court of said
countyshallhave controlover itand keep itin repairat
theexpense of said county .

f Kentucky :

pany .
§2 . The said corporators,or any one of them , are au-
thorized to open books for the subscriptionof stock in said
company at any point or place,and at any time during
the years of 1880, 1881, or 1882, in the said county of
Daviess . In said book or books the following obligation
shallbe written,viz : We , whose names are hereunto at-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY , 433
tachedand subscribed,do respectivelypromise and obli-
gateourselvesto pay unto the Owensboro and Buzzard
RoostGravel Road Company the sum of fiftydollars, for
eachshareof stockin saidcompany setoppositeour re-
spectivenames ,in such manner and proportion,and at
suchtime or times,as shallbe requiredby the president
and directorsof said company .
§3 . The book or books may be opened as soon as said
corporatorsmay think proper ,and remain open until a
sufficientnumber of shares of stock has been subscribed,
which,in the judgment of said corporators,or a majority
of them , willjustifythem to proceed to build said road
orany part thereof.

than fifteen hundred dollars, nor more than ten thousand
dollars, and shall be divided into shares of fiftydollars
each , and when the sum of fifteen hundred dollars has
been subscribed, the directors hereinafter provided for
may commence work upon said road ; but the books may
remain open until the amount of ten thousand dollars
have been subscribed in stock .
§5 . As soon as the said corporatorshave secured a sub-
scriptionof stocksufficienttojustifythem tobeginopera-
tionson said road ,they shallcalla meeting of said stock-
holders at some convenient point, after having given
reasonable notice thereof to each subscriber of stock,for

and said notice shallbe given by placing or causing to
be placed , in the hands of each stockholder,a writing,
statingthe amount of stock which has been subscribed,
by whom ithas been subscribed, and the day and place
of the meeting of the stockholders. At said meeting of

may presideand superintendthe organization; and said
stockholders shall proceed to elect five of their number
as directors,who shallbe known and styled: The Board
of Directorsof the Owensboro and Buzzard Roost Gravel
Road Company . The said fivedirectorsshallthen choose
one of their number to be president, another treasurer,

CHAPTER 1003 .
AN ACT forthebenefitofG. and C. Shindler,of Washingtoncounty.
WHEREAS , G. and C. Shindler, citizensof Washington
county,being the owners and proprietorsof a saw and
grist-mill in the town of Glenville,on the waters of the
Big Beech , in said county ,a long race is now a necessity,
and has ever been since the construction of said mills .
Said race was made over fiftyyears ago,and the several
roads loading out from the town of Glenville, in said
county,at which pointsaidmillislocated,have allbeen
surveyedsincethe erectionof said mill and the opening
of said race ; therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Washington county court shallbuild and
keep in repair the bridges across the race of G. and C.
Shindler, at the severalplaces where the county roads
cross said race,and that the surveyors of said roads shall
be requiredby saidcourttobuildand keep up saidbridges,
and shall be held liable in the same manner that survey-
ors are for keeping in repairthe public roads of said
county .
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 15, 1880.

CHAPTER 1013 .
AN ACT to prevent obstructionsin Littleand Big Greasy creeks, in Hop-
kins county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonweal h
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall hereafter be the duty of the several
owners of lands in Hopkins county ,bordering upon or
through which Littleand Big Greasy creeks may run , to
clean and remove ,or cause to be removed ,at least once
in each year, any and all timber, trees, logs, brush,or
other obstructions in the bed of said streams ; and where
said streams ,or eitherof them , shallbe the linebetween
separate tractsowned by differentpersons ,or where said
streams,or eitherof them ,shallrun between the tractsof
land owned and held by differentpersons,then the ob-
structions herein mentioned shall be cleaned and removed
at the joint expense of said owners ,so far as their lines
may extend .
§2 . Should any person failor refuse to clean out and
remove the obstructions herein mentioned from the bed
of said streams,running through or bordering upon his
or her lands,then it may be lawful for any person own-
ing lands upon said streams,at the expirationof sixty,
days afterdue notice and demand ,to clean out and re-
move such obstructions,at the expense of the person or
persons refusingto comply with the provisionsof this
act, and may recover the same in any court of competent
jurisdiction.
§3 . It shallbe unlawful for any person to place or
throw any timber , trees, logs, brush , or other obstruc-
tions in the bed or channel of the streams herein men-
tioned Any one so offending shall, on conviction, be

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 465
subject to a fineof not lessthan one nor more than five
dollars,to be recovered by warrant in the name of the
Commonwealth ,in any court of competent jurisdiction.
§ 4. This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 15 , 1880.

CHAPTER 1018 .
AN ACT to regulatevotingat primary electionsand prevent fraud therein,
for the benefitof Harrison, Bourbon , Campbell ,and Kenton counties.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That allelectionshereafterto be holden by any
voluntarypoliticalassociationor partyin thisState,for
any delegate or managing committee ,or for the nomina-
tion of candidates for public office,may be calledor or

470 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
dered,by publishednotice,upon the vote of a majorityof
the county ,city,district,or controllingcommittee of such
voluntarypoliticalassociationor party of the county,
city,or districtwhich may electto acceptthe provisions
of thisact,which shall state the purpose ,time ,manner ,
condition,togetherwith the place or places of holding
such election; also the authority by which the call or
notice ispublished; and the persons shallbe named who
shallact as judges at each pollof said election, and the
said person shall be a legal voter of the precinct, ward ,
or election district for which he is named . Said notice
shalllikewise declare the qualificationsof the persons to
vote at such elections: Provided, That such prescribed
qualificationsshall not be inconsistentwith the provis-
ions of this act .
§2 . That itshall be unlawful for any one to vote at a
primary election who would not, at the time of voting ,
be a legal voter,under the Constitution and laws of the
State , for the office for which the nomination is being
made , as if the regular electionwas being held on that
day; and in additionthereto,he must possessthe qualifi-
cations prescribedby the authorityof those who called or
ordered the primary election to be held,in the callthere-
for; and any one violating the provisions of thissection
shallbe deemed guiltyof a misdemeanor , and ,upon con-
viction, finedin a sum not less than twenty -fivedollars
nor exceeding one hundred dollars.
§3 . A primaryelection,withinthe meaning ofthisact,
is an election held under the order of the constituted au-
thorityof any politicalpartyin the State,at which a per-
son isto be voted for as the nominee of said party,for all
officesto be filledby an electionafterward to be held for
said office,by the voters of the State,county ,district, or
ward .
§4 . It shallbe the duty of all persons who act as
judges and clerks at said election to take an oath,before
some officerauthorized to administer oaths,that they will
faithfullydischargetheirdutiesas such,and to see that
an accurate poll of the vote cast is kept , and to refuse

thevotesof allpersons not entitledto vote,and to cast
up and certifythe poll-book,and send the same ,under
seal,by one of the officersof the election,to be selected
bp them , to the clerk of the county court,whose duty it
shallbe to keep the same on filefor two years thereafter,
or so long as any prosecutions may be pending in the
courts wherein such is used as evidence ; and said books
so made and kept shallbe evidenceof the factstherein
stated .
§5 . Any officerof said electionor otherperson,who
shallfalselycertifyto any fact in said poll-book , or who
shall alter, erase, change , or deface said book ,shall be
guilty of a misdemeanor , and , on conviction, shall be
punished by a fine not less than twenty -five dollarsnor
more than one hundred dollars .
§ 6. That itshallbe unlawful forany person to influ-
ence or to offerto influence the vote of any person at
such an election,or any delegate at a convention ,if such
delegate be elected under the provisionsof thisact,by the
use of money ,or property of any kind whatever ;and any
one so offending shall be guiltyof a misdemeanor ,and ,

twenty -five dollars nor more than one hundred dollars;
and iftheperson so offendingbe a candidatefornomina-
tion at said election,he shall, on conviction,be disqual-
ifiedfrom holding said officeduring the next ensuing
term thereof, or the vacancy , if a candidate to fill
vacancy .

§ 8. It shallbe the duty of the judges of the courts in

act .
§9. This actshalltakeeffectfrom and afteritspassage,
and no expense shall be incurred to the county or State

inthe conduct of electionsunder itsprovisions,and the

bon,Kenton ,and Campbell .

CHAPTER 1025 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe saleof intoxicatingliquorswithin one and a half
miles of Mason school-house, in Keysburgh district, Logan county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That it shall be unlawful ,after this act takes ef-
fect, for any person to sellany spirituous,vinous ,or malt
liquors,or any mixture thereof,within one and a half
miles of Mason's school-house , in Keysburgh district,
Logan county .
§ 2. That any person or persons guilty of sellingsuch
liquorsor mixtures,in violationof the firstsection of this
act, shallbe deemed guilty of keeping a tippling-house ,
and for the firstoffense, upon the indictment of a grand
jury ,shallbe fined fiftydollars,and for each subsequent
offense,shallbe fined one hundred dollars; the said fines
to be collected as other fines are now collected by law .

hereby repealed .
§4 . This act to take effect and be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved April 22 , 1880.

CHAPTER 1027 .
AN ACT to prohibit the sale of spirituous, vinous,or malt liquors,or a
mixture of either, in the town of Three Springs, in Hart county , or
within one mile of the corporate limits thereof .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallhereafterbe unlawful for any person
to sellor vend any spirituous,vinous, or malt liquors, or
any mixture of either,in the town of Three Springs ,in
Hart county,or within one mile of the corporatelimits
thereof .
§2 . That any person who shallsellor vend ,in any
manner whatever , any spirituous,vinous ,or malt liquors,
or a mixture of either,in said town , or within one mile
of the corporate limitsthereof, shallbe deemed guilty of
keeping a tippling-house ,and for each offense shall be
fined not lessthan twenty nor more than sixtydollars.
§3 . If any person shalltwice sell or vend any spiritu-
ous,vinous,or malt liquors,or a mixture of either,in said
town , or within one mile thereof , he shall be fined in a
sum of not less than sixtynor more than two hundred
dollars .
§ 4. Any person that shallkeep a tippling-house in said
town , or within one mile of the corporate limits thereof,

476 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
for three months at one time ,shall be fined in a sum of not
less than two hundred dollars nor more than four hundred
dollars .
§5 . Prosecutionsfor a violationof the provisionsof
thisact may be had in any court having jurisdictionto
try such offenses under the General Statutes .
§6 . Any fines collectedunder the provisionsof thisact
shall inure to the benefit of the common school fund of
the districtin which Three Springs is situated, and to be
paid over to the common school commissioner of Hart
county , and he shallbe liableon his officialbond for the
faithfulapplicationof the same to common school pur-
poses in said district.
§ 7. This act to take effectfrom and after its passage .
Approved April 22,1880 .

and Wolfe .

of Kentucky :

lowing boundary , to-wit : Beginning on Indian creek .
where the Menifee and Powell county lines crosses the

county line,and with same to the beginning .

urer,who shallbe elected at such times and places and in

CHAPTER 1046 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled"An act to establisha system of com-
mon schoolsin the town of Corydon, Henderson county," approved
March 25th , 1872 .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That " An act to establish a system of common
schools in the town of Corydon , Henderson county ,
Kentucky ," approved March 25th, 1872,be amended as
follows : That section eleven of said act be repealed ,
and the following substituted therefor : That all white
childrenbetween the ages of six and twenty years old
on the firstof July before the ensuing term ,whose pa-
rents or necessary legal guardians , and those who are
permanently separated, in good faith, from parents or
guardians , and those having no parents or guardian ,
and are residentsof said school district,shallhave equal
rights to the school free of charge , except as provided
in section one of amendment to said act, approved
March 6th, 1878 : Provided further, That persons having
children livingwith them from under the control of pa-
rents or guardians , as mentioned in this section, shall
appear in person before the board of trusteesand make
oath , and produce other satisfactoryevidence,ifrequired,
that the arrangement is permanent and in good faith.
§2 . That the treasurer of said school districtshall re-
ceiveand receiptforthe tax-book foreach year,and col-
lectand receiptfor alltaxes paid to the firstday of June
next ensuing the receipt of said tax-book ; he willdeliver
the saidtax-book ,or a certifiedstatement therefrom,show-
ing the names of those who have not paid ,and the amount
due from each ,to the collectorof said district,who shall
proceed to collect said unpaid taxes, and ,in addition
thereto,a penalty of ten per cent.; and alllaws conflict-
ing with thissection are hereby repealed.
§3 . All partiesmoving into saiddistrict(with intention
to become permanent residents), afterthe annual assess-
ment has been taken ,shall be entitledonly to the first
five months ' session of the ensuing term .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 493
4. The collector of said school district shall have
power to collectthe taxes,or any moneys due the district,
by levy,distress,or sale, anywhere in the county of Hen-
derson .
§5 . The board of trusteesshall,on the firstMonday in
August next ,at theirregular electionfor trustees,cause a
pollto be opened for the ratificationor rejectionof this
amended act . This act is not to be in force and effect
unlessa majority of the qualifiedwhite voters voting at
the election provided for in this section cast theirvotes
in favor of this act . Printed or written notices shall be
posted in not lessthan six publicplacesin saiddistrict,
not lessthan fifteendays priorto the election.
§ 6. No one in said districtshall become the guardian
of a non -residentchildfor the sole purpose of itsenjoying
the benefit of the school .
§ 7. This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage.
Approved April 22, 1880 .

ARTICLE I.

stonemarked " M ," one hundred poles and eleven links

ship two ,range four east; thence south one hundred and
thirteenpoles,one foot,and four inches to a stone; thence
east one hundred and thirteenpoles,one foot,and four
inches to a stone ; thence north one hundred and thirteen
poles,one foot,and four inches to a stone; thence west
one hundred and thirteenpoles,one foot,and fourinches
to the beginning ,containing eighty acres.
§2 . That the inhabitants of said town are hereby cre-
ateda body-politic,with perpetualsuccession,withpower
to sue and be sued ,contract and be contracted with ,plead
and be impleaded,defend and be defended,by the name
of the town of Murray .
ARTICLE II.
Officersand Elections.

i
firstregularmeeting of the board oftrustees,be by them

Police Court .

General Powers and Duties of the Board of Trustees.
§1 . That the board of trusteesshall hold stated meet-
ings,and the chairman may callmeetings,by noticeto

or written; but ifwritten,such notice must be served per

sonallyor leftat their usual place of abode . A major-
ity of the persons electedas trusteesshallconstitutea

real,personal, and mixed ,belonging to the corporation ;
and shall have power to contract and to be contracted
with, for and on behalf of said town ; and shall have
power to provide for the erection and repair of all need-
fulpublic buildings for the town ; and to provide for in-
closing and improving allpublic grounds belonging to the

tion of said town ,by by -law or ordinance-
Ist. To restrainand prohibitalldescriptionsof gaming
and fraudulent devices, and all playing of dice, cards,
and other games of chance ,whereat anything of value is
bet, won , or lost.
2d . To regulate and tax the sellingof any ardent spir-
itsor otherintoxicating,fermented,vinous,ormalt liquors
by any hotel-keeper , tavern-keeper ,innkeeper ,saloon or
coffee-house keeper ,druggist or merchant in said town :
Provided , That , in addition to the present State tax, the
tax (under this act) upon licensedhotel, tavern, inn,
saloon ,or coffee-house keepers shall be not lessthan two
hundred nor more threehundred dollars,and thetax upon
such licensed druggist, merchant or merchants , shall be
not less than ten nor more than twenty -five dollars, in
addition to the amount now required by law.
3d . To prohibitthe sellingor giving away of any spir-
its, or other intoxicatingliquors,to any minor or appren-
tice,without the writtenconsentof his or her parent or
guardian , master or mistress.

billiardtables,pigeon hole,jenny lind,and allsimilarta-
bles,or contrivances, pin-alleys, nine or ten-pin alleys,
ball alleys,and shooting galleries.
5th. To tax, license, regulate auctioneers, distillers,
brewers , and pawnbrokers ,and tax or lay duties on the
sale of goods at auction.

ARTICLE VI .
Taxation .
§1 . That the board of trustees of Murray shall have
power ,subject to the exceptions hereinafter mentioned ,
to annually levy and collecttaxes, not exceeding fifty
cents on each one hundred dollars ' worth of all the real
estate,and upon allpersonalestatein saidtown; and a
headorpoll-tax upon every male inhabitantofsaidtown
overtwenty one years of age ,of not exceeding one dol-
larand fiftycents.
§2. There shallbe exempt from taxation under thisact,
or forthe purposes of said town , all public property be-
longingto said town ,the county of Calloway,the State
ofKentucky,the United States,any church,college,pub-
licseminaryoflearning,allgrounds attachedto any such
