ARTICLE VII .
Collectionsof Taxes and Assessments .
§1 . That the board of trusteesshallhave power , by
ordinance,to prescribethe form of assessment rolls,and
prescribe the duties and define the powers of assessors;
and to make such rules,and give such directionsin rela-
tion to attending,revising,or adding to the rolls,as they
may deem itproper and expedient .
§2 . That the annual assessment rolls shall be returned
by the assessor,on or beforethe firstday of May in each
year ; but the time may be extended by order of the board
of trustees. On the return thereof , the board of trustees
shall fix a day for hearing objections thereto, and the
clerk shall give notice of the time and place of such
hearing ; and any person feelingaggrieved by the assess-
ment of his property, may appear at the time specified
and make his objections. The board of trusteesshallhave
power to supply omissionsin said assessmentrolls; and
for the purpose of equalizing the same ,to alter, add to,
take from , or otherwise correct and revise the same .
§3 . That when the assessment roll shall have been cor-
rected and revised,the same shall be filed,and an order
confirmingthe same shallbe enteredby the clerk. The
board of trusteesshallthereupon ,by an ordinance or res-
olution,levy such sum or sums of money as they may
deem proper ,not exceeding the amounts authorized by
this act , and direct the collector to collect the same .
§4 . That alltaxes and assessments,general or special,
levied or assessedby the board of trusteesunder this act,
shallbe a lienupon the propertyupon which the same
may be imposed,voted,or assessed,untilsuch tax shall
be paid ,and no sale or transfershallaffectthe lienthere-
on; and no propertyshallbe exempt from sale for the
payment of such taxes or assessments,except such as is
by thisact exempted from taxation.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 521
§5. That the clerk of the board of trustees shall, im-
mediatelyafterthe assessment roll shall have been con-
firmed,make out a true and perfectcopy thereof,which
shallbe examined and signed by the chairman , attested
bytheclerk, and deliveredto the collector, who shall
proceedto collectthe taxes according to said assessment ,
asdirectedby the board of trustees,and give his receipts
therefor. If not otherwise paid ,the board of trusteesof
Murray shallhave power to collectsaid taxes and costs,
by suitin the name of the board of trusteesof Murray ,
orby distressand saleof the propertyas aforesaid,after
a demand and refusal to pay the same . The assessor's
rollshall, in all cases, be evidence on the part of the
boardof trusteesof Murray; and noticepublishedby the
collector, by stickingup written or printed notices at four
publicplaces in said town for ten days ,shall be deemed
a demand ,and a neglectto pay for twenty days there-
aftershallbe deemed a refusalto pay such tax or assess-
ments .
6. That the collectorshall pay all taxes and assess-
ments as fastas collectedinto the town treasury; and his
powers,duties, liabilities, in cases of a default or mis-
conduct,shallbe prescribed by the board of trusteesby
ordinance .
§7 . That in case of non -payment of any taxes due the
town of Murray ,so much of any personal or real estate,
belongingtothe person from whom the said tax is due,
asissufficientto pay the same ,shallbe soldby the col-
lectortothehighestbidder,at publicauction,for cash in
hand ,afterthe same shall have been duly advertised, as
thelaw requiresof similarpropertysoldunder execution,
at any time within two years after the confirmation of

shallgive tothe purchaserof any realestatesoldby him
a certificateof purchase,similarin allrespectsto certifi-
catesgiven by the sheriffin sales of land fortaxes due the
State, which certificatesshall have the same force and

effectas those given by the sheriffin salesfor taxes, and
shallbe recovered by the county court clerk of Calloway
county in the book kept by him to record certificatesof
real estatesoldfor taxes. When any real estate sold for
taxesdue the town of Murray shallremain unredeemed
at the expiration of the time in which the same may be
redeemed as hereinafter provided, the collectorthen in
officeshallmake a deed of conveyance to the purchaser ,
which shall have the same force and effect as deeds made
by the sheriffin similar cases. The collectorshall be

§ 8. That the right of redemption ,in allcases of sales
of real estatefor taxes or assessments,shall existto the

on the amount for which the same was sold,and alltaxes

§ 9. If, at any sale of real or personal estatefor taxes

and shall be vested with the same rights as other pur-
chasers at such sales, and for the benefit of the town ;
and the collectorshall execute to the purchaser a certifi-
cate of sale for all real estate sold for tax .
§ 10. Any deed , or a duly authenticated copy of the
record thereof, made for real estate sold for taxes or
assessments,shallbe prima facieevidence of the power
and authorityto sellthe same , that allproceedings in
reference to such sale,and levying the taxes or assess-
ments for which the same was sold, were regular.

ARTICLE VIII .
Grading,Paving,and ImprovingStreetsand Alleys.
§1. That the board of trustees shall have power to
grade,pave , improve , protect, and ornament any public
squareor ground now or hereafterlaid out.
§2. That the owners in front of or upon whose prem-
isesthe board of trustees shall order and direct curbing
and sidewalks to be constructed,repaired,or re-laid,shall
make,repair, or re-lay such curbingor sidewalksat their
own costsand expense,and in the manner ,withthemate-
rial,and in the time prescribedby saidboard oftrustees,

ner,with the materials,and within the time so prescribed,

structed, repaired, or re-laid, and assess the expenses
therefor,by an order to be entered in the record of their
proceedings ,upon such lotsrespectively,and collectsame
by warrant and sale of the premises, as in the case of
taxes; a suit may also be maintained by the board of
trusteesof Murray against such owner or owners for the

§3 . That all the provisions of the preceding section
shallapply with equal force to allpersons whose duty it
isto remove or abate any nuisance ,and to allproperty
chargeable with the expense of abating or removing

shall be required to make a sidewalk in front of his or
her premises until after the streetin front of such shall
have been curbed for the sidewalks thereof at the expense

ordered to be made .

ingsuch improvements as may seem necessary,theboard

bonds of said town of Murray ,due in one year from date ,

CHAPTER 1061 .
AN ACT เอ amend the charter of the Irvine and Miller'sCreek Turnpike
Road and Bridge Company , and to legalizethe transferof the stock of
the Irvineand Miller'screek districtsthereinto Estillcounty .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the charter of the Irvine and Miller'sCreek
Turnpike Road and Bridge Company ,and the act to
amend the same , approved March 27,1872,be,and the
same are,amended as follows: The transferof the stock,
rights, and franchisesowned in said company by the
Irvine and Miller'screek districts, made by the judge of
Estillcounty and the justicesof said districts,on the 18th

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 527
day of February, 1880,to Estillcounty,islegalizedand
made valid .
§2 . In the absence of an election of officersof said
company ,as provided for,the county judge of said county
shallbe the presidentof said company ,and the clerkof
the county court of said county the treasurer, ex-officio;
and said clerk shallbe responsibleon his officialbond for
allmoneys received from said road .
§3 . the electionof officersof said company shallhere-
afterbe held annually at the court of claims of said coun-
ty,and the stockof saidcounty voted by the judge and
justicesthereof: Provided, That at the next regular May
election provided for, the vote of said county may be
castby a person appointed by said judge and justices.
§4 . That any money expended by any of the stock-
holders in rebuilding the Cow creek bridge on said road ,
or extending the road under the charter,shallbe additional
stock in the road .
§5 . This act to be in force from and after its passage .
Approved April 22 , 1880.

CHAPTER 1064 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Grand Lodge of the Ancient Order of United
Workmen of Color of Kentucky , and provide foritsorganization.
WHEREAS , Certain colored persons, citizensof Ken-
tucky, are desirous of forming a corporation to promote
and advance scientificand mechanical pursuits in said
State and elsewhere ; therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 That S. M. Bell,sr.,Henry Harris, Warren How-
ard,Joseph Smith, Robert Hawkins , G. W. Shelton, J.
Allen Ross ,and S. M. Bell,jr., and their successors, be ,
and they are hereby ,created a body -politicand corpo-
rateby the name ,style,and titleof the Grand Lodge of
the Ancient Order of United Workmen of Color of Ken-
tucky,and by such name and titleshallhave perpetual
succession, and be capable in law of suing and being
sued,pleading and being impleaded , and of purchasing ,
holding,and receiving in its corporate name property,
real,personal,and mixed ,and of institutingsuch subor-
dinatelodgesas itmay see fit,under such rules,by-laws,
and regulations as the corporation may establish,not in
conflict with the Constitution and laws of the Common-
wealth or of the United States .
§2 . The object of the corporation shallbe to improve
the moral ,mental , and social condition of the members
of the lodgeunder itsjurisdiction,and toprevent strikes
among allclasses by exhausting all honorable means in
itspower forsuch end .
§ 3. The said corporation shallhave a common sealfor
the making and deliveringof all legal acts and proceed-
ings,and itshallhave power to alterthe same at pleasure.
§4 . It shallbe lawful for this corporation to accumu-
late,hold, manage , and disburse a beneficiary fund for
the reliefof the members ,and theirfamilies,of the lodges
establishedby this corporation,or of sisterlodges estab-
lishedby othergrand lodgesworking under and subordi-
nate to a supreme lodge, under such regulations as may
be adopted by thiscorporationor by a supreme lodge.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 633
§5 . Such beneficiaryfund as thecorporationmay deem
suitableand propermay be set apart and providedtobe
paid over to the families of deceased members , or the
heirsof such deceased members ,or to such person or per-
sons as such deceased members may ,whilst living,direct.
The collecting, management , and disbursement of the
same , as well as the person or persons to whom the same
shallbe paid on the death of a deceased member ,shallbe
controlledand regulated by the rules and by -laws of the
corporation; and any such fund ,so provided and setapart,
shallbe exempt from execution,and shall,under no cir-
cumstances ,be liableto be seized,taken , or appropriated
by any legalor equitableprocesstopay any debt of such
deceased member .
§6. The said corporation may make and constitute
forthe same such officersas itmay deem necessary and
proper,whose term of officeshall expire on the second
Monday in May in each year .
§7 . A supreme lodge may be establishedby thisgrand
lodge in conjunction with other grand lodges, and when
so established the officers thereof and their successors
in perpetuity shallbecome a body -politicand corporate,
under the name and styleof the Supreme Lodge of the
Ancient Order of United Workmen of Color of the United
States; and ,on accepting thischarter,shallbe entitledto
alltherights,privilegesand immunitiesthereincontained
with power to establish other grand lodges within the
United States,with likepowers,privileges,and immuni-
ties,but subordinate to the said supreme lodge .
§8 . The said grand lodge or any of its subordinates Mayissuebonds.
may ,for enabling them to erect any building in which
they shallhave a hall for the use or their lodges,issue
theirbonds ,not to exceed five thousand dollars($ 5,000),
and bearing interest not to exceed six per cent per an-
num . They may also issuetheirbonds not to exceed two
thousand dollars($ 2,000), for the purpose of temporarily
relievingthem in raisingmoney to pay insuranceon their
deceased members . But at no time shall any lodge

534 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
therein incur an indebtedness to exceed seven thousand
dollars .
§9 . This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 22, 1880 .

CHAPTER 1066 .
AN ACT to createa specialroad law for the countiesof Lee and Rockcastie.
Be itenactedbytheGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
ofKentucky:
1.That the sheriff'sof Lee ,and Estill, Laurel,and Rock-
castlecountiesshallcause a poll to be opened at the

540 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
several voting places in said counties,at the electionto
be held on the firstMonday in August , 1880, and thatto
each voter thisquestion shallbe put by the officerscon-
ducting the election: " Are you in favor of having a tax
of two dollarsimposed on allpersonsnow designatedby
law to work on roads in this county , or are you against
such tax ? " Those voting for the tax shall have their
votes placed in one column ,and those voting againstit
in another ; and the resultshallbe returned to the county
courtclerk'sofficeofsaidcounty,and thesame compared
and reported in the same way and by the same officers
who ,under existinglaws ,examine and adjudge the poll-
books in elections .
§ 2. Should a majorityof the qualifiedvotersofeither
county vote forthe tax as above stated,then itshallbe the
duty of thecounty courtof saidcounty to levy a tax,not
exceedingtwo dollarsper head,on allthepersonsin said
county designated now by law to work on roads ,which
shallbe collectedas hereinafterprovided .
§3 . That thecountyjudge of saidcounty shallappoint,
at the firstcounty court after the ratificationof thisact
by the voters of said county ,one road commissioner in
each magisterialdistrictin said county .
§ 4. Beforeactingas commissioners,theyshallfirsttake
an oath,beforethe countyjudge forsaidcounty,to faith-
fullydischarge theirduties, and shallbe required to exe-
cutebond to saidcounty court,with good security,forthe
faithtuldischargeof the same ; saidbond to be approved
by the courtof saidcounty and filedin the officeof said
county court .
§5 That said commissioners shallcollectallthe road
taxes in their respectivedistrictsin the same manner as
othertaxesare now requiredto be collectedby sheriffs,
and shallhave the power that sheriffshave in collecting
the State revenue and county levy, and shall be subject
tothe same liabilities,and shallreceivethe same commis.
sion thereon as now allowed sheriffsby law .
§ 6. The commissioner of tax forsaid county shallkeep
a separatebook for each magisterialdistrict,and shall
have recordedin saidbook the names of personsbetween

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 541
the age of sixteen and fiftyyears . Said book shall be
furnished him by the county clerk of said county ,and to
be returned with the other tax books at the same time ;
the clerk of the county court shall make out a certified
copy of each of said books ,and give to each road com-
missioner before the firstday of June in each year . The
county court clerk shall have one cent for each name so
copied and certified, to be paid out of the county levy
fund . The commissioner of tax shall have one cent for
each name so taken,to be paid out of the county levy
fund .
§7 . That said road commissioners shall proceed , in
such manner as best to secure the permanent improve-
ment of the roads in said districts, as far as the same can
be done by a judicious expenditure of the funds so re-
ceived by them . Said commissioners are required to
make ,to the county court of said county ,an annual re-
port and settlementon the firstMonday in November of
each year ,showing the amount of moneys collectedby
them , and the disbursements of the same ; and oftener
than once a year ,if so required by said court; and for
any misapplication of the road funds so received by them ,
may be proceeded against before said county court,by
motion , and , upon convictionthereof, shallforfeitand
pay to the county , for the use of said district, the full
amount of the principalso misapplied and thirtyper
cent . damage thereon; the proceeding before said court
to be conducted by the county attorney.
§ 8. Fullpower and authorityisherebygranted to said
commissioners to do whatever may be ,in theirjudgment ,
necessaryto carryout the purposes of thisact,such as
hiringhands to work the road,with tools,wagons ,carts,
teams ,and to make all necessary contractstouching the
same .
§ 9. That all delinquent capitation tax-payers of Lee
county shallbe requiredto work out theirtaxes,at such
times and on such roads as the commissioner of the dis-
trictwhere they live may require; for which work such
delinquentsshallhave creditat the rateof fiftycents for

542 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
each fullworking day performed by him . The commis .
sioners for the purpose above set forth, is invested with
the same authority that surveyors of roads under the gen-
eral law now have ; and forfailingor refusing to comply
with the orders of said commissioners ,when notified as
requiredby law,allsuch delinquentsare made liableto
allthepenaltiesnow imposed by law for personsfailing
or refusing to work on roads. Each commissioner is
authorized and empowered to collectthe taxes due from
such delinquents, if they shall elect to pay the same
ratherthan work on the roads,and the same shallbe held
and appliedas partof the road fund. Any person shall
have the right to work the road , if he so elect,rather
than to pay the two dollars tax imposed by thisact,and
will be credited at the rate of fiftycents for each full
day's work .
§ 10. If it is ascertained that there is an insufficient
amount of taxes in any districtin said counties to put the
roads therein in good order,then the commissioner shall
have power towarn the hands within such districtto work
on the roads therein,and put the same in good condition;
not exceeding ,however , two days in each year .
§ 11. The commissioners shall be allowed a reasonable
compensation of the sums received by them for their ser-
vices .
§ 12. The general law of this state is hereby adopted
and made part of this act,except when itcomes in con-
flictwith the provisions of thisact.
§ 13. The said commissioners ,afterthe approval of this
actby the votersof said county,shallproceed to assess
theirrespectivedistrictsforthe year 1880,and collectthe
taxes, and apply the same for road purposes; but the
commissioner of tax for said county ,afterthe year 1880,
shallcomply with sectionsixof thisact.
§14 . This act shall take effect and be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved April 22 , 1880.

CHAPTER 1067 .
AN ACT toincorporatetheMurray and WadesboroGravelRoad Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That J.M. Hutchens,L. C. Linn, Wm . H. Curd ,
Ben .C. Keys ,James Hendrick,W. C. Clement,and A. J.
Holland be ,and they are hereby ,created a body -politic
and corporate, by the name of the Murray and Wades-
boro Gravel Road Company ; and by that name may con-
tractand be contracted with ,sue and be sued ,plead and
be impleaded,defend and be defended,in allthe courtsof
thisCommonwealth . They may have a common seal,
which they may change or alterat pleasure.
§2. Said company shallhave power ,and are author-
ized, to construct a gravel road , beginning at Murray ,
thence north to the north boundary of Calloway county ,
at or near Wadesboro .

CHAPTER 1069 .
AN ACT to empower the county court of Henderson county to take stock
in turnpike or gravel roads in Henderson county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Henderson county court,composed of the
county judge and the justicesof said county ,or a major-
ityof them ,be ,and they are hereby , authorized and re-
quired to subscribestock for said county in any company
organized forthe purpose of building turnpike or gravel
roads in said county ,to an amount not lessthan ten nor
more than fifteenthousand dollars to each company or
road; and the said county shall have all the rights and
privilegesof a stockholder, and the said court shall ap-
point one or more persons to represent the county in all
meetings of the stockholdersin any such company .
§2 . That the saidcourtshallhave the power and au-
thority,and they are required to issue the bonds of the
county ,forsuch amount or amounts as may be subscribed
under section one of this act . Said bonds shall be of
such denomination,and shallbe made payable at such
time and place,as the saidcourtmay determine,but with
the privilege to redeem same at the expiration of five
years from theirdate,and shallbear such rateof interest,
not exceeding six per cent. per annum , payable semi-
annually , as said court may deem best.
§3 . That said bonds,when issued,shallbe signed by
the countyjudge and countersignedby the county clerk,
with the county seal attached,and the coupons for the
semi-annual interestshallbe signed by the county clerk;
and thesaidbonds,to the amount thatmay be subscribed
to any company ,shallbe delivered to the company ,or the
bonds may be sold and the money paid to the company
by the said court as they may deem best,at the times and
inthe manner providedin the articlesof associationof
said company ; and the said county shallreceivefrom
such company a certificateof stock for the amount thus
subscribed and paid in.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 547
§ 4. That in ne event shallthe subscriptionabove pro-
vided forbe made , or the said bonds be issued, untilthere
has been subscribed to any such company or road by good
and solventpersons,at leastthe sum of ten thousand dol-
lars of stock in such road , and when that amount shall
have been subscribed by said parties,itshallbe the duty
of the county judge of said county,upon the requestof
said subscribersor any of them ,to convene the saidjus-
ticesforthepurpose of carryingout the provisionsofthis
act; and a registerof the bonds issued under thisact shall
be kept by the county clerk.
§5 . That when the bonds as hereinbefore provided for
are issued,it shall be the duty of the said court to an-
nually levy and cause to be collected, as other county
revenues are now collected, an ad valorem tax of not
exceeding twenty -fivecents on the one hundred dollars'
worth of all the property subject to taxation for county
purposes,which said amount so collected,togetherwith
the dividendsfrom such road or roads as may be built,
shallbe used for the purpose of paying offthe interest
and principal of said bonds .
§ 6. It shallbe the duty of the judge of the Henderson
county court to cause the sense of the qualifiedvoters of
Henderson county to be taken upon this act at the next
general electionheldin saidcounty,and forthatpurpose
there shallbe opened upon the poll-books , at each voting
place,a column headed " For subscriptionto turnpike
or gravel roads," and another column headed "Against
subscriptiontoturnpikeor gravelroads;" and should
majorityof thosevoting at said electionfor and against
such subscriptionbe in favorof suchsubscription,then
the subscriptionshereinprovidedforshallbe made and
said bonds be issued .
H
§7. The saidjudge shall,in likemanner,submitthis
act to the qualifiedvotersof said county at any subse-
quent general election,when theretorequested,in writing,
by as many as twenty residentvoters and tax -payers of
said county , should not a majority of votes be cast there-
for in the first submission .

54S LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§8 . The provisionsof thisact shallnot apply to the
territorylying north of Green river in said county ,nor to
the citizens therein .
§ 9. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 22 , 1880.
CHAPTER 1070 .
AN ACT incorporatingthe White Sulphur and Fisher'sMill Turnpike
Road Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That any five or more persons may associate
themselves together for the purpose of constructing a
turnpikeroad from White Sulphur,in Scott county,to
Edward Waite's , in Woodford county , by the way of
Fisher'sMill. Said persons shallbe incorporatedby the
name and styleof the White Sulphur and Fisher'sMill
Turnpike Road Company ; by which name they shallsue
and be sued ,plead and be impleaded ,in any court in this
Commonwealth , and shallhave perpetual succession.
§2 . That B. J. Laughlin , James Gallahue , P. O. Мс-
Manus , and Gilbert Thompson , of Scott county , and
Jesse McConnell , Louis Fisher, and H. O. Davis , of
Woodford county,are hereby appointed commissioners,
who ,or any one of them ,are hereby authorized to receive
subscriptionsto the capitalstock of said company ,in
shares of twenty -five dollars. When one thousand five
hundred dollarsissubscribed to the capital stock of said
company ,the stockholdersare authorizedto electa pres-
ident and three directors, who shallelecta treasurer and
secretary, allof whom shallbe stockholders Said board
shallhold theirofficeone year ,or untiltheirsuccessorsare
elected and qualified.
§3 . That the county courts of Scott and Woodford are
hereby authorized to take stock in said road , not to ex-
ceed the amount per mile which said county court has
the authorityto subscribeby law to turnpikeroads; and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 541
said county courts are hereby authorized and empowered
to levy a tax for the amount of stock which they may
subscribe, and shall be collected according to the way
provided by existinglaws of thisState.
§4 . Said corporation shallhave power to condemn the
right of way ,the necessary rock, gravel, dirt, wood , or
other necessary materialforthe construction or repairsof
said road ,as provided by chapter one hundred and three
of the Revised Statutes and amendments thereto .
§5 . When said road iscompleted,saidcompany shall
have power to establish a toll-gate , and to collectany
rate of toll authorized to be collected by the General
Statutes .
§6 . This act shallbe in force from itspassage .
Approved April 22, 1880.
CHAPTER 1071 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Dutch Fish Trap Bridge Company , and te
facilitatecommunication between the counties of Hopkins and McLean .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Samuel Croombaker ,the owner of each bank
of the water-course known as Pond river, at a point
known as Dutch Fish Trap , and such other persons as he
may associatewith him ,be,and they are hereby,consti-
tuted a body-politicand corporate,under the name and
styleof the Dutch Fish Trap Bridge Company ; and by
that name they shall have perpetual succession, with
power to make contracts,to sue and be sued ,and have
and exercise all the rights,privileges, and immunities of
a corporation.
§2 . The said corporation shall have the privilege,and
are incorporated for the specialpurpose ,of constructing
a bridge acrossthe saidPond riverat the point known
as Dutch Fish Trap ; said bridge to be constructedof such
materialsas the said corporation may deem proper ,and
being builtupon trestle-work or stone abutments ,at the
option of said corporation : Provided, however, That the

550 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
said bridge shall be so constructed as not to be an obstruc-
tion to navigation of the said Pond river.
§3 . The said corporationshallhave such officers,and
adopt such by-laws fortheirgovernment ,not inconsistent
with the laws of this Commonwealth ,as to the stock-
holders in said corporation may seem proper .
§ 4. The said corporation shall have the privilegeand
rightto charge tollsfor crossing said bridge at rates not
exceeding ten cents for one horse and buggy ; for two
horses and buggy , twenty cents; for four horses and
wagon ,thirtyfive cents; for horse and rider, five cents;
and at the same rates for every character of conveyance
and travelers,except that persons on foot shallpass free.
§5 If the said corporation shall not commence the
constructionof the said bridge within two years from the
passage of thisact,then the county courtsof Hopkins
and McLean countiesmay , afteracquiring,by giftor
purchase , the right of way , eitherjointlyor separately,
appropriate any moneys that may be necessary for the
building of said bridge; in which event allthe rightsand
privilegesheretoforegranted by thisact to the saidcor-
porationshallbe transferredto the saidcounty courtor
courts; and the said corporationmay also,at any time
before the expirationof the said two years, surrender
theirprivileges as aforesaid to the said county court or
courts .
§ 6. This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 22 , 1880 .

Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth

ing itor them to the land -owner so refusing,forpayment ;

isdamaged by having to constructsaid ditchor ditches,

corded in a book to be kept by them ,and alsorecorded
in the clerk'sofficeof the McLean county court,and may

day in May ,by the legallyqualifiedvoters,citizensof
saidtown ,fivetrusteesforsaidtown ,who shallbe styled
Trustees of the Town of Beech Grove , with perpetual
succession; who ,in theircorporatename aforesaid,shall

be impleaded ,grant and receive,by theircorporate name ,
and do allother acts as natural persons may do ; and as

forthe government and regulation of said town ,not in-
consistent with the Constitution or laws of the State or
of the United States; and they may do and perform all

joritymay act.

and alleys in said town .

day in May ,by the legallyqualifiedvoters,citizensof

Police Judge of Beech Grove and Town Marshal of Beech

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 561
Grove. The policejudge to have the same jurisdiction,
power ,and capacity, entitledto the same fees and emol-
uments,and subjectto the same liabilitiesand responsi-

The town marshal to have the same power and capacity,
entitledto the same fees,and subject to the same liabil-
ities, duties, and responsibilitiesas a constable for Mc-
Leancounty. The policejudge to be commissioned by
theGovernor of this State ; the town marshal to execute
bond in the McLean county court, with security,to be
approvedby the court,with same conditionsand penal-
ties as the bond of a constable .
§5 . All the officersherein mentioned shallbe firstduly
sworn,by some court or justice of the peace , that they

theyproceed to act; allresignationsof the same shallbe
to the McLean county court, and all vacancies in the

tinueto act untiltheirsuccessorsare qualified. All elec-
tionsshallbe held by a clerk, to be appointed by the
board,who shallcertifythe same to the McLean county
court; and saidcourt,as to the policejudge,shallcertify
to the Governor of this State .

untiltheirsuccessors shall qualify,the following shallbe
theofficersof saidtown :James Smith,policejudge;Guss
Rudy ,W. H. McDaniels ,C. B. Travis,John Wesley ,and
MarshallAshby ,as trustees; William Rudy ,town marshal
for said town .
§7 . This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspassage ;
butthe Legislaturereservesthe rightto change,amend ,
or repealthe same at any time ; but in case of itsrepeal,
or the forfeitureof this charter, all debts due to or from
thecorporationshallbe extinguished, and allproperty
boughtby or donated tosaidcorporation,not disposedof,
shallreverttothe originaldonor or vendor.
Approved April 22 , 1880.

forsaid county , for the year 1880, on or before the first

since, on the ninth day of February , 1880, fullyexecuted
and completed said bonds , with good and sufficientsurety,
dulyacceptedand approvedby thejudgeofthe county
court for said county ; and there being some doubt as to

CHAPTER 1091 .
AN ACT to renew and amend the charter of the Camdenville and Van
Buren Turnpike Road .
WHEREAS , Under order of the Anderson county court,
enteredJune 24, 1867, the people of Anderson county
voted one thousand dollars per mile to the construction
of a turnpike road from the terminus of the Lawrence-
burg and Camdenville via to Van Buren ; now ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Anderson county are
hereby authorizedto carryout said vote by subscribing
one thousand dollarsper mile to each mile of road tobe
constructedby the company hereby created: Provided,
however, That said subscriptionshallnot be made nor tax
levied until the company show to the county court that,
with the subscriptionof one thousand dollarsper mile
aforesaid,it has sufficientmeans to build and complete
at least five miles of said road , and when that is done ,
then said subscriptionshall be made ,and a levy laidon
all the taxable property of said county subject to taxa-
tion for State revenue purposes, sufficientto pay said
subscription: Provided, however, That the said county
court may provide for the payment of said subscription
in one or more years . The following commissioners are
hereby empowered to open books for the subscriptionof
stock,at such times and places as they may direct,and
alsosuperintendthe constructionof saidroad,and outof
theirnumber selecta presidentand board of directors
in the manner prescribed in their charter, viz : Lewis
Franklin, James Stennett, Alex. Goodnight, W. C. Ash,
J. H. Yowel .
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 22, 1880.

CHAPTER 1096 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Home of the Innocents."
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That James T. Helm , John N. Norton , Sam'l D.
Tompkins ,John B. Smith,and William Babb ,theirasso-
ciatesand successors,be , and they are hereby , created a
body -politic and corporate, under the name and style
of the " Home of the Innocents;" and by that name may
contract and be contracted with , may sue and be sued,
and may do any and allotheractspertainingto the pur-
poses of the organization; may have and use a common
seal,and the same to alter at pleasure; and may make
such rules,regulations, and by -laws as may be necessary
to carry out the purposes of thisorganization.
§2 . The objectof thisorganization isto provide for the
Theobjectofthecomfort and proper care of young childrenand infants
whose parentsare too poor,helpless,or worthlessto take
care of them ; also to furnish a home for the children of
such mothers as are compelled to labor out,and also to
affordshelterand protectionto destitutemothers with
infantstoo young to be separated. Said institutionshall
be located in Louisville,or in Jeffersoncounty ,Kentucky ;
and may acquire and hold,by purchase or otherwise,real
estatefor the purposes of said institution, to the amount
of ten thousand dollars,and may selland convey the same
as a natural person . Said institutionshall be under the
management and control of a board of five directors,
composed ofthepersonsnamed in the firstsectionof this
act; a majority of said board shall constitute a quorum ,
and theirterm of service shallbe for life, unless they re-
sign or their place be declared vacant . If any member

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 575
of the board fails to attend for two successive regular
meetingsof said board , after being duly notifiedto at-
tend,hisplacemay ,by the other members of the board,
bedeclaredvacant ,and they may select some one to fill
the vacancy .
§3. The said board may receive and hold, for the ben-
fitofsaid institution, allgifts,bequests, and conveyances
ofpropertyof every kind, and apply the same ,or the pro-
ceedsthereof, to the uses of said institution, in such man-
neras may be prescribedby said board. Said board of
directorsshall electfrom their number a president and
vicepresident,and may also selecta secretary and treas-
urereitherfrom theirown number or from outside. They
shallhave power to appoint all agents, assistants, and
servantsnecessary to conduct the affairsof said institu-
tionproperly, and to discharge them ,or any of them , as
theymay deem necessary .
§4. The corporationhereby createdshallbe supported
initswork entirelyby the charitableofferingsofthe com-
munity in which the institutionis located, or by any
means appointed in the providence of God ; and the prop-
ertyand effectsof said corporation are exempted from all
tax .
§5 . This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 23 , 1880.

CHAPTER 1103 .
AN ACT to amend an act to amend and reduce intoone the severalactsin
relationto the cityof Frankfort, approved March 16 , 1869.
WHEREAS , Differencesand disputeshave existedbe-
tween the cityof Frankfortand thatportionof saidcity

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 583
on the south side of Kentucky river, known as South
Frankfort,and for settlement, the same were ,by public
meetingsof the citizensof saidcity,and by the board of
councilmen of the city of Frankfort, referred to a com-
mission,and said commission has made a reportof set-
tlement to, and the same has been ratifiedand confirmed
by,saidboard of councilmen,who now ask the necessary
legislationto carry out the same ; now ,to that end ,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
ofKentucky :
1. That allaccounts ,claims,and indebtedness of every
characterand descriptionbetween the cityof Frankfort
and that part of said cityon the south sideof the Ken-
tuckyriverknown as South Frankfort,be,and the same
arehereby declared to be,fullycanceled and relinquished.
And allthe property,rights,franchises,and immunities,of
everykind and description,belonging or appertainingto
the city of Frankfort , or North or South Frankfort, are
herebymade and declaredto be the common property,
rights,franchises,and immunitiesofthe cityofFrankfort,
includingSouth Frankfort; and allthe debts or liabilities
incurred,or to be incurred,by the city of Frankfort ,are
hereby made and declared to be the debts and liabilities
of the city of Frankfort,as embracing both North and
South Frankfort .
§2. The cityof Frankfortis herebylaidoffintothree
wards as follows, to-wit : That part of said city south
of Kentucky river shall constitutethe firstward ; that
parton the north side of Kentucky river and east of the
center of St. Clair street, shall constitute the second
ward ; and that part on the north side of Kentucky river
and west of the center of St. Clair street,shall constitute
the third ward .
§3 . That the board of councilmen of the cityof Frank-
fort, to be elected at the next regular and each succeed-
ing election, shall consist of nine councilmen , three of
whom shallbe residents of the firstward ,three of the
second ward , and three of the third ward ,each of whom
shallbe the owner of real estate in the ward for which

584 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
he was elected , and shall have been a resident of said
ward one year next preceding his election. If a council-
man removes from his ward ,he shallthereby vacate and
forfeit his office .
§4 . That the qualificationsof voters at said elections
shallbe as now prescribedby law . Each voter shallvote
in the districtin which he resides,and shall have the
right at such voting place to vote for nine councilmen ;
but it shall be unlawful for any such voter to vote for
more than three persons who are residentsof the same
ward . The three persons receiving the highest number
of votes for each of said wards shall be declared to be
elected .
§5 . That thisact take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 24 , 1880 .

CHAPTER 1108 .
AN ACT to amend an act incorporatingthe town of London , authorizing
the county judge to act as chairman of the board of trustees.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county judge may act as chairman of the
board of trustees .
§2 . That the county judge may try all offensesagainst
the town laws , and all offenses committed within the
town , and the proceeds of said fines to go for the benefit
of said town .
§3 . That the office of chairman of the board of trus-
tees and county judge ,to that extent meant in the fore-
going section,shallnot be incompatible .
§4 . This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 24, 1880.

of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 1110 .
AN ACT to authorizethe Green and Barren River Navigation Company to
constructand maintain a lineof telephone or telegraphfrom Bowling
Green to Evansville, Indiana.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Green and Barren River Navigation Com-
pany be , and they are hereby , authorized and empow-
ered to construct and maintain a line of telephone or tel-
egraph along or near the banks of Green and Barren
rivers,from the city of Bowling Green , Kentucky , to
Evansville,Indiana,or to such points along saidlineas
they may desire, and to charge and receive pay for mes-
sages and dispatches over said linefrom and to itsvarious
points,at rates not exceeding those charged by the West-
ern Union Telegraphic Company for similarmessages
over those telegraphiclines.
§2 . That any person or personswho shallin any man-
ner mutilate,pull down ,injure, or destroy any part of the
saidlineof telephoneor itsbuildings,shallbe subjectto
thesame finesand penaltiesprescribedby statuteagainst
thosewho inany manner injurea lineof telegraph.
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 24 , 1880.

CHAPTER 1112
AN ACT to incorporatethe Drennon Turnpike Company , of Henry county .
WHEREAS . John Martensy ,John Harding ,J. W. Downs ,
II.C. Barclay ,James Kephart ,Thomas W. Rowland ,and
E.W. Eeasley,under and by virtueofthespecialturnpike
law for Henry county,approved March 9,1869,built(2)
two milesand (16) sixteenrods of turnpikeroad in Henry
county,beginning at the western terminus of the New
Castle,Pleasureville, and Sweet Home Turnpike Road ,
one mile west of the town of Pleasureville,and running
westwardly,and intersectingwith theSulphur,Eminence ,
and Kentucky River Turnpike Road ,which was received
and accepted by the turnpike commissioner for said
countyas being builtaccordingto law and the require.
ments of said act; and whereas , said turnpike is being
used and traveledas a publichighway ; and whereas,
tollscannot be collected for travel thereon , because said
road islessthan two and one half miles in length; there-
fore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky:
§1.That a company withpowers ofperpetualsuccession
LOC . L., VOL . 1-39

602 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ishereby created,under the name of the Drennon Turn-
pike Company ; and by thatname itmay contractand be
contracted with,sue and be sued,collecttolls; and itshall
have the powers ,rights,and privilegesincident to such
corporations.
§2 . The capitalstock of said company shallnot ex-
ceed five thousand dollars. The stock issued shall be
divided into shares of twenty -five dollars, and every
stockholder shall be entitled to one vote for each share of
stock owned by him .
§3 . Said company shall organize by the election of a
president, two directors, a secretary,and a treasurer,who
shallhold officeuntiltheirsuccessorsare elected and qual-
ified. The treasurer shallgive bond with security, to be
approved by the president and directors.
§4 . The company may erect one tollgate at any point
on saidroad which may be selectedby a majorityof the
stockholders, or the president and directorsmay author-
izethe gate-keeper eastof and nearestEminence ,of the
Sulphur,Eminence ,Kentucky River Turnpike Company ,
or the gate-keeper at Pleasureville,of the New Castle,
Pleasureville,and Sweet Home Turnpike Company ,either
one gate-keeper ,to collecttollsat his gate fortravelupon
said two miles and sixteen rods of turnpike; and when so
authorized,said gate -keeper shall have the same power
to collecttollsfor the Drennon Turnpike Company that
he now has forhis said respectivecompany .
§5 . The provisions of chapter one hundred and ten,
entitled " Turnpike , Gravel , and Plank Road ," of the
General Statutes,not inconsistentwith thisact,are made
parts hereof.
§ 6. This act to be in force from itspassage .
Approved April 24, 1880.

chased ,fencing the same , and such other acts necessary
to secure the public buildings of the county from danger
of fire .
§3 . That the bonds which ,by the firstsection of this
act,are authorized to be issued, may be in such denomi-
nations as the court may direct,and the bonds so issued
shallbe indorsed by the county judge and clerk in their
officialcapacity,and when so indorsedand sold,shallbe
binding and valid upon said county .
§4 . That to enable the county court to pay off the
principaland interestof the bonds authorized by thisact,
they are empowered to make a levy on the taxable prop-
erty of said county a tax sufficientto pay off the princi-
pal and interestof the bonds issued by them under this
act. Such levy shallbe made at the time the county
court meets to make the county levy for other purposes ,
and when a majority of the justicesare present and vot-
ing therefor .
§5 . That said bonds shallbe made payable in ten
years,or at an earliertime ,in the discretionof the court,
Said bonds shall have interest coupons attached to the
margin of each bond for each year that said bonds may
have to run .
§6 . That the intereston said bonds shall be payable
semi-annually on the firstday of January and July of
each year at the National Bank of Stanford , until said
bonds are paid off.
§7 . That thisact shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 24, 1880 .

§1 . That it shall be unlawful for any person ,in any
quantity whatever , to sell to another any spirituous,
vinous , or malt liquors, or a mixture of either, within
the limits of common school district No. 25 , in Graves
county .
§2 . Any personfound guiltyof a violationof thisact
shall,upon conviction, be fined in a sum not less than
fiftynor more than one hundred dollars,recoverable upon
either warrant or indictment .
§3 . This act shall take effect and be in force from its
passage .
§ 4. It shall be the duty of the trustees of common
school, in said district25,to take the vote of the qual-
ifiedvoters in said district, two of whom shall act as
judges,theotherthe sheriffof saidelection,and one of
the judges shallact as clerk; and in case only two of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 619
saidtrusteesattend, they shallhave power to appoint
some person to act as sheriff. Said election shall be
holden at the common school -house in said district on
theSaturday ,29th day of May , 1880. And said trustees
shallgive at least fifteendays ' notice of the time and
place and purpose of holding said election, and the
question shall be asked of each individualvoter voting ,
"Are you for or against the sale of spirituous,vinous , or
malt liquorsin school districtNo. 25?" and the clerk shall
record the vote in accordance with the answer . The re-
turnsshallbe made to the clerk of the county court,who
shall,with the assistanceof the county court,count and
compare the vote,and make known the resultas in elec-
tionsfor county officers. If a majorityof allthe votes
cast be for the sale of spirituousvinous liquors in said
district,then this act shall cease and be of no effect and
force .
Approved April 24, 1880.

CHAPTER 1140 .
AN ACT to authorizethe citycollectorof Lexington to sellrealestatefor
the payment of citytaxes.
Be it enactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the collectorof taxes of the city of Lexing-
ton shallbe,and he is hereby, authorizedto levy upon
any realestatein the cityof Lexington of a person ow-
ing taxesto said city,for the amount of taxesunpaid of
such person , and in the hands of the collectorfor collec-
tion, and proceed to selland convey the same , or so
much thereof as is necessary to pay such taxes,in the
same manner that sheriffs are authorized to sell lands
under executions of fi.fa.;but with the right of redemp-
tion for the same time ,and upon the same terms, that is
given by the charter of the cityof Lexington upon sales
of land for taxes assessedupon such land .
§2 . The lien on the real estate of the tax-payer for
taxes shallcommence on the tenth of January in the
year in which the taxes are assessed,and may be enforced
by the levy and sale provided in the firstsection of this
act .
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 24, 1880.

CHAPTER 1151 .

CHAPTER 1176 .
An act forthe benefitof John P. Raymond , of Robertson county .
WHEREAS , John P. Raymond , a citizen of Robertson
county,isblindand badly crippled,and has been so ever
since he was an infant , and that he has no estate , either
realor personal,and isentirelyunable to perform manual
labor ; therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That it shall be lawful for John P. Raymond to
peddle goods,wares, merchandise, notions,& c., but not
spirituous,vinous ,or malt liquors,or any mixture thereof,
in the counties of Nicholas , Harrison , and Robertson ,
without being requiredto take out licenseforsaid priv-
ilege of peddling , or paying any tax therefor.
§ 2. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 26, 1880 .

CHAPTER 1178 .
AN ACT for the benefit of the trustees of Hartford .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . It shall be lawful for the county judge of Ohio
county, when directed to do so by order of the county
court,made and entered of record upon the order-book
of said court,to convey to the board of trusteesof the
town of Hartford all the right, title, and interest of said
county in and to the one acre lot in said town , and all
the buildingsand improvements thereon,known as the
old jail-house and lot.
§ 2. It shall be lawful for the board of trusteesof the
town of Hartford ,when conveyance shallhave been made
in pursuance to the provision of section one ,to sell and
convey or lease said lot and buildings and improvements ,
upon such terms and time as they may deem advisable,
and apply the sums so arisingto the building, improving ,
and maintaining a graded school in said town .
§3 . This act shallbe in force from itspassage .
Approved April 26, 1880.

672 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1180 .
AN ACT to amend an act approved February 21st, 1880,and entitled"An
act regulatingthe citycourtof Louisville, and the remuneration of its
officers ."
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the city court of Louisville shall have the
same jurisdiction, within the city of Louisville,as iscon-
ferredon city and policecourts by section13 of the
Criminal Code of Practice. It shallhave exclusivejuris-
dictionas an examining courtof alloffensescommitted
within the city of Louisville, and itspowers in such
cases,and the effectof itsacts,shallbe the same as those
of a county judge in other counties.
§ 2. In allcaseswhere persons are presented to said
courtcharged with an offensewithin itsjurisdiction,the
punishment of which is limitedto a fineof twenty dol-
larsor less,the judge may hear and determine the case,
and impose such finewithoutthe interventionof a jury.
In allother cases he shall cause a jury to be impaneled ,
unlesswaived by the party charged; and in allcasesof
drunkenness or disorderlyconduct ,in addition to impos-
ing a fine,he may hold the offender to bail,in any sum
not exceeding one thousand dollars,to keep the peace
and be of good behavior ,for any length of time not ex-
ceeding one year,or he may impose the fine without
holding to bail; but in no case shallhe hold to bail with-
out imposing a fine; and thereupon the offender shallbe
forthwithcommitted to the citywork -house, and shall
there be kept in custody until the bail be given ,or the
time for which it was requiredshallhave expired,and
untilthe fineand costsare paid or replevied,or satisfied
by labor as provided by law .
§3 . In all preliminary examinations before said court,
when the offensecharged isnot capital,the accused shall
be admitted to bail to answer from day to day ,until the
case shallbe disposed of ; and no person who is charged
with an offense not capital in the cityof Louisville,and
who tenders proper and sufficientbail,shallbe committed
tojailor a station-house unless intoxicatedor violent at

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 673
the time ,or arrested afteror before the regular business
hours of the day , or at night,or on Sunday ; but shall be
taken forthwith before said citycourt,if in session, or,if
not in session,before the clerk of said court,or before the
judge of said court,ifthe clerk cannot be found ,and bail
shall be accepted by said court or clerk or judge , as the
case may be . All such bail bonds or recognizances , if
forfeited,shallbe prosecuted to judgment in said court,in
the same manner as provided by law for similar cases in
the circuitcourts; and for that purpose the said court shall
hold monthly terms , to commence on the firstMonday of
each month .
§ 4. Policemen shallhave power to execute warrants of
arrest, and subpœnas and attachments for witnesses,
whether the same be directed to them or not .
§5 . That section eighteen is amended by strikingout
the words " as now provided by law ," after the words
" remitting any fine,penalty ,or costs."
§6 . That section five of said act, entitled" An act
regulating the city court of Louisvilleand the remunera-
tion of itsofficers," be , and itis amended by adding after
the words " supported by the affidavitsof two credible
housekeepers of the city of Louisville," the following
words : When the judge of said court is compelled to
vacate the bench because of the filingof said affidavits,
the judge pro tem. who triesthe case shallnot receive any
pay for hisservices,unlessthe trialof the case occupies
more than one day ,and then he shallbe paid only the
sum of four dollarsper day ; and said sum , on the written
requestof saidjudge pro tem.,shallbe paid by the cityof
Louisville, and shall be deducted from the salary of the
regular judge .
§7 . That section nine of said act be ,and itis,amended
by adding the following words : In case of a vacancy of
said marshal's office,the judge of said court shall fillsaid
office,if the unexpired term be lessthan one year ; ifitbe
more ,he shallappoint some one to fillthe officetillthe
next general electionafterthe vacancy ,at which election
a new marshal shallbe regularlyelectedas in the firstin
stance .

674 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 8. The prosecutingattorney,subjectto theprovisions,
limitations, and conditions of section eight and section
sixteen in said act,entitled" An act regulating the city
court of Louisville,and the remuneration of itsofficers,"
shall receive as compensation for his services thirtyper
cent. of allfinesand forfeituresimposed and adjudged
by saidcourt; but no taxed fees shallbe allowed in any
prosecution or action.
§ 9. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 26, 1880 .

pany may be made , conducted, and carriedon without

committee or otherwise as the board may authorize,and

thisact, or such committee as they shallappoint,shall
receive subscriptionsto the capital stock of thiscompany ,
at such pointsas they may designate,upon two weeks'
notice,published in one or more newspapers publishedin
the cityof Louisville, which books shallcontinue open
from day to day for sixtydays , unless the whole amount
of the capitalstock shallbe sooner subscribed; and shall
receiveallmoneys paid at the time of subscribingtosaid
stock, and hold the same until the election of the first
board of directors,when they shalldispose of itas the
board may direct.
§ 14. That the investmentsof thiscompany shallbe
made in accordance with the provisionsof "An actfor
the incorporationand regulationof fire,marine,health,
accident,livestock, and allother except lifeinsurance
companies ," approved March 12, 1870 ,and the acts which
have heretoforebeen,or may hereafterbe,passed amend-
atorythereto; and thiscompany shall,upon itsincorpo-
rationand enteringupon business,be amenable toand
regulatedby the general insurance laws of thisCom-
monwealth as other stock fireinsurance companies now

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 709
doing business in this State are; both those heretofore
charteredby the General Assembly,and thosewhich have
become incorporatedunder the provisionsof the general
insurance laws of thisState.
§ 15. That thisact shallbe in force from and after its
passage , and shallcontinue in force for the space of thir-
ty years .
Approved April 26 , 1880 .

ration .

ified .

electionheld ,

ors firstchosen shallarrange ,as they may deem best,who

urer, and perform such other duties as may be requiredby

ciation may need .
§ 8. The vice presidentshallpossessthe powers and

ings,transactions,and business of the board and the asso-
ciation,conduct itscorrespondence ,receive allthe money
due theassociation,receiptforthe same ,and pay itover
to the treasurerat the close of each week ,taking his re-
ceipt therefor; he shallprepare allcertificatesof member-
ship, noticesof death,and registerallmemberships and
deaths in a well-bound book ; also the amount paid to the
representativeof any deceased member ; he shall,within

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 711
fortydays afternoticehas been servedupon the members ,
make a record of all those who have paid and have not
paid. He shallsemi -annually , or as often as required by
theboard ,present a written statement of the condition of

in the name of the associationin some good bank , to be
designated by the board ,and shall semi annually ,or as
oftenas required by the board , present a written state-
ment of the financial condition of the association .
§11 . Both treasurer and secretaryshall take an oath
to faithfullyperform their duties, and give bond in such
sum , with good surety,as may be required by the board ,
to account for and pay over allmoney or other property
receivedby them to the partiesentitledto the same ; and
such bonds shall be increased or renewed as often as de-
manded by the board .
§ 12. The board of directors shall have charge and
supervisionof the affairsof the association, and for this
purpose they may enact by -laws not repugnant to the
provisionsof thischarter, nor inconsistentwith the laws
of this Commonwealth . A majority of the board shall
constitutea quorum forthe transactionof business; and ,
before enteringupon theirduties,shalltake an oath to
faithfullyand to the best of theirabilityperform the duty
of director .
§ 13. The qualificationsformembership in thisassocia-
tion shallbe that the applicant shallbe a member of the
Knights of Honor ,and furnisha physician'scertificate,
showing that he is in good health, sound in mind and
body ,and that he is under fifty-fiveyears of age .
§14 . The board shalldetermine in theirby -laws how
applicationsfor membership shallbe made ,furnishthe
necessary form of application; also fix the schedule of
pricestobe assessedagainstitsmembers ,the dues to be
paid,theage at which personsshallbe eligibletobecome
members ,who shallbe members ,and feesto be paid by
the differentmembers ,and timesof meeting ofsaidboard,

712 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
how meetings shallbe called,& c., & c., and such rulesas
are necessary for theirmanagement .
§ 15. The applicantshall,at the time of his applica-
tion, designate who shall be the beneficiary; and ifit
shallbe forany otherperson than the applicant,thesaid
benefitshallnot be liablefor any debt of the applicant.
§ 16. Should a member ,at any time,desireto change
his certificate,he shall surrender the firstcertificate,or
give satisfactoryreasons,in the form of an affidavit,why
he cannot surrender the firstcertificate, and send said
affidavitto the secretary,with a feeof one dollarto pay
for new certificate, who shall submit same to the board
at theirnext meeting ; and should the board be satisfied,
a new certificate shall be issued .
§ 17. Upon the death of any member of thisassocia-
tion, a certificate of death shall be forwarded to the
secretary; and ifsatisfactoryto the president, he shall
instruct the secretary to draw an order on the treasurer
for the amount due beneficiaryon the decease of said
member .
§ 18. Three thousand dollarsshallbe the highest amount
paid by thisassociationon the death of a member ,and
thissum shallbe paid on the death of every member
whenever the membership of said association has so in-
creased as to realize the sum of three thousand dollars
from one assessment; and untilthattime,thebenefitpaid
shall not exceed the amount of one assessment on each
member . After the receipt of satisfactoryevidence of
the death of a member ,the secretaryshall immediately,
by mail,notifyeach member to pay theiradvanced as
sessment within thirtydays from the date of said notice;
and ifsaidassessmentisnotpaidwithinthirtydays,the
member shallbe reportedto the president, and by his
order suspended from allrights and benefitsof thisasso-
ciation .
§19 . Each member changing hispost-officeaddressis
required to notifythe secretary of said change .
§20. As earlyas practicableafterthe suspensionofa
member ,the secretaryshall,by mail,send a notice,noti-
fyingthemember of thedateof hissuspension; andsaid

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 713
suspendedbrothermay , within sixtydays from date of
hissuspension, forward the assessmentsfor all deaths
accrued previous to his suspension,and a fee of twen-
ty-fivecents for the additional trouble; and should he
remain suspended for a longer period than sixty days
withoutmaking application,then he shallbe re-examined
by the physician,and pay in allrespectsas though his
applicationwas for the firsttime .
§21. The privateproperty of members shallnot be
liable for the debts of this association .
§22 . This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved April26, 1880.

CHAPTER 1223 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the Hopewell and Bethlehem Turnpike
Road Company .

of Kentucky :
§1 . That the charterof the Hopewell and Bethlehem
Turnpike Road ,approved February 27,1867,be,and the
same is hereby , amended as follows,to-wit : Said com-
pany is authorizedto grade and macadamize that por-
tion of the Old Limestone dirt road , which liesbetween
and leads from the Hopewell and Bethlehem Turnpike to
the Antioch and Houston Turnpike Road , a distance of
about one and three fourth miles : Provided , That in
making the road,the company may deviatefrom the old
road by beginning the extension at the toll-house on the
Hopewell and Bethlehem Turnpike, and terminatingat
the toll-house on the Antioch and Houston Turnpike ,fol-
lowing the most direct,practicable, and cheap route, as
theirjudgment may deem best.
§2 . The road shall not be less than thirtyfeet wide ,
grade not lessthan eighteen feet,and metal not lessthan
fifteen feet wide .
§3 . When completed,the road shallbe consolidated
with , and become a part of,the Hopewell and Bethlehem
Turnpike Road ,and the company may erecta gateacross,
and collecttollat said gate on alltravelpassing over the
road, at the same rates,for the distance traveled, as are
allowed by the general laws of the State on turnpike,
gravel, and plank roads .
§4 . The officersof the company , for the time being ,
may receive subscriptionsto the extension herein author-
ized, and may enforce collectionof same by law ; and for
each sum of fiftydollarswhich may be subscribedand
paid toward building the extension,shall entitlethe sub-
scriber to a certificate of one share of stock in the whole
consolidated road , afterthe same is completed .
§5 . The company shall be entitledto allthe remedies
forprocuring rightof way , quarries,and material to build

thisextension,that are provided in the charterto which
this is an amendment .

ston,E. W. Royalty,Wm .Yates,Thomas J.Trent,C. C.

whom are willing to act,be ,and are hereby , appointed

scribed, they shall give ten days' notice for a meeting

ed and qualified.

director who is not the owner of one or more shares of
stock in said road ; and in the election of said officers,
each stockholder shall be entitled to one vote for each
share of stock so owned ,and may be cast in person or
by written proxy .

cers or agents which may be deemed necessary to effect

otherofficersand agents appointed by them ,to execute

bent on them as such .

a book or books ,and the subscribersto the stock of said
company shallenter intothe following obligation in said
book ,viz : We , whose names are hereto subscribed, do
promise to pay to the presidentand directorsof theDun-
cansvilleand Chaplin Turnpike Road Company the sum
of twenty -five dollars for each and every share of stock
in saidcompany setoppositeour names ,in such manner

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 733
and proportion,and at such times as shallbe by them re-
quiredunder the law incorporatingthe company ,to be
collectedas otherdebts. Witness our hands this- day
of , 1880. "
7. That said road shall not be lessthan twenty feet
grade,and the metal (sound rock broken),not lessthan
fourteen feet wide , and graded to an elevation of not
more than four degrees . The depth of broken metal to
be not less than ten inches; the top dressing of four
inches to be broken to pass through a three-inch ring.
That whenever two miles of said road is completed , a
toll-gate may be erected thereon, and tollscollectedthere-
at,at the rate now allowed by the General Statutes regu-
latingsame .

land over which the same may run ,not exceeding thirty
feetin width ,and for toll-gate sites,as also material from

road .

dollarsper mile.

§3 . No person shallrob or destroy the nest or nests or
eggs of any of the birds mentioned in the firstand sec-
ond sectionsof this act at any time whatsoever , and for
every offense mentioned in thissection shallbe subject to
a fine of three dollars for each offense .
§ 4. That the unlawful killing,catching,or possession
of each and every one of the birdsintended to be pro-
tected by this act shall constitutea separate and distinct
offense,and shallbe punishable accordingly,and two or
more offensesmay be joined in the same warrant or in-
dictment therefor, and the person so offending, if con-
victed , shall be fined for each offense .
§5 . All prosecutions under this act may be instituted
before any justiceof the peace ; and ,upon conviction, if
any person found guiltyof the violationof thisactshall
failto pay the fine assessed against him ,such person may
be confinedin the county jailone day foreach dollarof
the fineimposed .
§ 6. This act shall only apply to the counties of War-
ren,Hickman ,and Fulton , and shalltake effectfrom and
afterSeptember 1st, 1880.
Approved April 27, 1880 .
CHAPTER 1253 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Deering Camp Ground Association, in Nicholas
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That T. S. Parks,T. J.Glenn ,Thomas A. Dorsey ,
R. M. Parks, Moses A. Glenn , Horace M. Taylor, and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 741
W. P. Ross,and the followinglaymen ,viz: Rev. Morris
Evans , W. T. Pointer, and H. P. Walker , and their suc-
cessors, be , and are hereby , created a body -corporate,
under the name and styleof the Deering Camp Ground
Association ,situated on Licking river,and on the Ken-
tucky Central Railroad , and near the railroad bridge
which spans saidriverin Nicholas county; to have per-
petual succession, with rightto obtain, hold, sell, and
convey ,and dispose of every class of property necessary
for the uses of thiscompany , not to exceed in value two
thousand dollars; and may contract and be contracted
with, sue and be sued, plead and be impleaded,in all
the courts in the Commonwealth , and transact all busi-
ness in its corporate name for its corporate purposes ;
and may make by-laws foritsgovernment ,not inconsist-
ent with the laws of the land .
§2 . Said company having erected,at considerableex-
pense , a large amphitheatre and chapel for religious
services, a dining-hall,a large number of nice cottages,
ice-house ,cisterns, stables,and a number of other build-
ings,& c., and the grounds inclosed with a neat fence,it
shallbe the objectand businessof saidcompany tokeep
up and maintain said grounds ,with allthe appurtenances
thereunto belonging , and to furnish good boarding or
singlemeals forallpersonswho may desireit,and sta-
bles and provender for horses, and all other necessary
accommodations for the public during religiousservices
on said camp ground ,at fairand reasonable rates.
§3 . The company shallhave authorityto forbidall
transientpersonsfrom erectingbooths and sellingcon-
fectioneries, fruits and melons , tobacco and cigars, or
any kind of diet or provender for horses, at any place
within one mile of said camp ground, without firstob-
tainingthe writtenpermissionto do so of saidcompany .
§4 . If any person or persons violatesectionthree of
thisact,he or they,upon conviction,shallbe finedin the
sum of not lessthan ten dollarsnor more than twenty
dollars,for each offense,to be recoveredby warrant be-
fore any court of competent jurisdiction.

742 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§5 . The company shallhave allthepowers ,rightsand
privileges, and may do allthe acts and things necessary
to keep up said buildings, grounds , & c., and to police
said grounds ,that good order may be maintained during
the religiousservices; and to provide for the accommo-
dation and comfort of the people during religiousser-
vices,and at all other times from the beginning of any
protracted meeting untilitsclose.
§6 . It shallbe the duty of said company to postat
least twenty printed copies of theirrules and regulations
at the most publicplaces on said camp grounds at the
beginning of each protracted meeting .
§ 7. Nothing in the foregoing act shall be so construed
as to prevent or restrictany person or persons attending
services on said camp ground from bringing theirown
diet and eating it on said ground ,renting cottages and
boarding themselves ,& c.
§ 8. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 27, 1880.

CHAPTER 1255 .
AN ACT toincorporatethePerryvilleMale and FemaleSeminary,inBoyle
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That an institutionof learning,previouslyknown
as Harmonia College,locatedin the town of Perryville,
Boyle county, Kentucky , now owned and controlledby
the Kentucky Presbyteryof the Cumberland Presbyterian
Church,shallhereafterbe known as PerryvilleMale and
Female Seminary .
§ 2. That thefollowingpersons appointed as a board
of trusteesby said Presbytery, viz: Rev. J. C. Gilliam,
Nelson Wingate ,W. W. Brinton, Dr. J. W. Reno ,S.C.
S. Vanarsdall, J.C. Barkley, J. B. H. Latimer,Dr. J.B.
Bolling, and W. L. Caldwell, and theirsuccessors,shall
be a body -corporate,by the name and styleof the Board
of Trustees of PerryvilleSeminary .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 743
§3 . The board of trusteesshallelectfrom theirnumber
a president,and such other officersas may be necessary
to do business; fivemembers constitutinga quorum .
§ 4. The saidboard oftrusteesshallhave power to elect
members to fillvacancies that may occur by death , resig-
nation,or other causes,the same to be confirmed by the
Presbytery .
§ 5. That said board of trustees, with the consent of
the principal of said seminary ,shallhave power to direct
the course of study,to appoint assistantteachers as may
be necessaryto complete the course of study prescribed
by said board,and to make allby-laws and regulations
necessary for the management and control of said semi-
nary .
§6 . That the board of trustees shall, in case of the
death or resignationof any principalor teacherthey may
employ or may discharge, appoint his or her successor;
shallvisitthe seminary ,attend the annual examinations ,
and give such counsel to the principal as the interestof
the seminary may require.
§7 . That the principal and teachers,with the consent
of the board of trustees, grant diplomas signed by the
principal, teachers, and board of trustees, together with
the seal of the seminary , which shallbe as valid as those
conferred by any other seminary or college in thisState :
Provided,That no such honor be conferredupon any of
the students but those who shall have completed the pre-
scribed course of studies, passed a satisfactoryexamina-
tion,and shallhave sustained a good moral character.
§8 . That should Kentucky Presbytery at any time
remove or dismissthe present board of trustees,or their
successors,and appointa new board,the new board shall
have and exercise all the rights and powers conferred
upon the present board of trustees.
§9 That the trusteesshallreport annuallythe wants ,
condition,and prospectsoftheseminary,the conditionof
the property,& c.,to the Kentucky Presbytery.
§ 10. This act to take effectupon itspassage .
Approved April 27, 1880.

CHAPTER 1262 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof W. C. Kennedy .
WHEREAS , Certain improved real estatein the cityof
Louisville,Jeffersoncounty,Kentucky ,listedin the name
of Andrew McKinley , was returned as delinquent for
State taxes to the Auditor ,who caused a sale to be made
of said propertyon the 6th day of April, 1874, to raise
theamount returnedas due on said property,and W. C.
Kennedy became the purchaser thereofat said salefor
the sum of eight hundred and thirty-four dollarsand
thirty-six cents, the amount of said delinquent tax and
costs,which sum he paid,and the same was receivedby
the Auditor and covered into the Treasury of the State;
and whereas , thereafter, in an action in the Louisville
chancery court, No. 27572 , McKinley , & c., versus Mc-
Kinley,& c.,the legalityof saidtax was litigatedby the
McKinleys and defendedby saidKennedy ,who was rep-
resentedby ableand experiencedcounsel,yet,afterdue
investigation,itwas considered by the court that the tax
so levied and paid into the Treasury by said Kennedy ,
and forwhich saidpropertywas sold,was inpartillegally
assessed, and that Kennedy acquiredno interestin the
land,nor had he or the State any claim on McKinley or
the property forthe said taxes because of the illegalas-
sessment and sale to the extent of ($550) fivehundred
and fiftydollarsof the amount so paid into the Treasury

758 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
by said Kennedy ; and whereas , said Kennedy , priorto
the said litigationconcerning the legalityof the assess-
ment ,had received from a fund in court of said McKinley
the fullamount paid by him at said sale; but after said
decree, was ,by rule, compelled to pay back to the said
McKinley said sum of five hundred and fiftydollars,
which he did on the28th of March , 1878; and whereas,
the Treasury of the State has said money so illegally
collectedand received from said Kennedy ; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGentralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
ef Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor be , and he is hereby ,directedto
draw hiswarrantupon the Treasurerof thisStateinfavor
of W. C. Kennedy , of Louisville, Jeffersoncounty ,Ken-
tucky,forthe sum of($ 550) fivehundred and fiftydollars,
being fullamount of saidillegaltax paid intothe Treas-
ury by said Kennedy and received by the State, with
interest; the acceptanceby saidKennedy ofsaidwarrant
shall be in full discharge to said State because of any
claim he may have against the State arising from the
payment by him set forthin the preamble of this act, by
providingthatthe proper officersof the State shallre-
assesssaid property for the years 1865 to 1871,inclusive.
§2 . This act shall take effectand be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved April 27 , 1880.

CHAPTER 1281 .
AN ACT to directthe Auditor to sellthe P. A. Cline property in Piketon,
Pike county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth
ofKentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be , and he is
hereby,authorizedto sellto the highestbidder,at public
auction,at the court-house door in Piketon,Pike county,
Kentucky , the house and lot of ground in said town of
Piketon , said lot containing between one half and one
quarterof an acre, which was purchased by the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky as the propertyof P. A. Cline.
The sale willbe made afterdue advertisement, upon the
following terms : One third cash ,one third on a creditof
one year ,and the remainder on a creditof two years from
the day of sale,and will require the purchaser to give
bonds,with good security,payable to the Commonwealth
of Kentucky , for the deferredpayments . The Auditor
willconvey thepropertyto the purchaser,retaininga lien
thereontosecuretheunpaid purchase-money . The Aud-
itorwillcollectthe purchase-money , and pay intothe
Treasury .
§2. The Auditor is allowedtwenty-fivedollarsforhis
services herein .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 778
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage : Pro-
vided, All bids of less sum than eight hundred dollars
shallbe rejectedby the Auditor , who is authorized to re-
advertiseand offerforsalesaidproperty,inhisdiscretion,
untilsold. The Auditorshallonly give a quit-claim deed
to the purchaser thereof.
Approved April 28 , 1880 .

CHAPTER 1287 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Camargo and Stanton Turnpike Road Company .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That a company shallbe formed,under the name
and styleof the Camargo and Stanton Turnpike Road
Company ,for the purpose of making a turnpikeor gravel
road from Camargo ,in Montgomery county , to Stanton ,
in Powellcounty,passingatornear the PoplarTavern ,on
Lulbegrud creek,and that the capitalstockof said com-
pany shallbe as many sharesof fiftydollarseach as will
make said road , not exceeding the sum of twenty -five
thousand dollars .
§2 . That books for the subscriptionof stock in said
company shallbe opened on the firstSaturday in June
next,or at any time thereafterthatmay be deemed expe-
dient,in Camargo ,under the directionof William Ficklin,
James H. Hall, William F. Harton , John Jay Anderson ,
and George Douglass ,of Montgomery county ,in Stanton ,
under the direction of John W. Williams , John Rose ,
Judge J.R. Hardwick ,- Wallers,and Thomas B. Hall,
of Powell county , or some two or more of them at each
place,who are appointed commissioners . The subscribers
to said company shallenter into the following obligation,
substantially: We , whose names are hereunto subscribed,
severallypromise to pay to the Camargo and Stanton
Turnpike Road Company thesum of fiftydollarsin cash,
labor,or materials,as the president and directorsmay re-
quire,foreach and every share of stockset oppositeour
names respectively,and agreeablyto an act of the Gen-
eralAssembly incorporatingsaidcompany . Notice shall
be given of the opening of books forthe subscriptionof
stock in said company .
§3 . Whenever the commissioners ,or any three of them ,
may be of opinion thata sufficientamount of stock has
been subscribedtojustifycommencing the making of said
road,they may calla meeting of the stockholdersforthe
purpose of electinga presidentand fivedirectors,who
shallhold theirofficeforone year ,and untiltheirsuccess-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 791
ors shall be elected and qualified. A treasurer shall be
appointed ahnually by the presidentand directors,who
shall give a bond , with good security, in an adequate
penalty, payable to said company , conditionedthat he
will faithfullydischarge the duties of treasurer, that he
willperformthe dutiesrequiredof him by the by-laws of
saidcompany ,and thathe willpay the amount of moneys
in his hands to the president and directors,or to their
order: Provided, That the said president, directors, and
treasurer, before entering upon theirofficesrespectively,
shall take an oath before some judge or justice of the
peace thathe willfaithfullyperform thedutiesof hisoffice
without favor or affection,and to the best of his judg-
ment .
§4 . That upon the election and qualificationof the
presidentand directors,theyshallbe a body-politicand
corporate,in deed and in law,by the name and styleof
the Camargo and Stanton Turnpike Road Company ;and
by said name to have perpetualsuccession,and allthe
privilegesand franchisesincident to a corporation; and
shall be capable of taking and holding theircapitalstock,
and the increaseand profitsthereof; and of purchasing,

signs,and of selling,transferring,and conveying allsuch
lands,tenements ,and real and personal estate,as much
as shallbe necessaryto them in theprosecutionof their
