votes in the electionof a president and directors,and in
allother elections,as he may hold shares of stock in said
company : Provided, That if the county court of Mont-
gomery and Powell counties shalltake stock in said com-
pany ,theyshallbe entitledto onlyone vote forevery ten
sharesof stockin said company . The stockholdersmay
vote in person or by proxy . The electionshalltake place
annually on the firstSaturday in May ,unlessthe time

shallbe changed by the by -laws of the company ; and at
each annual meeting of the stockholders, the president
shalllay before them an expose of the situation of said

company shallhave power to pass by -laws for theirgov-
ernment , and to fix days and places for their annual
meetings and elections ; and certificatesof stock shall be

the company ,in person or by attorney,and proper certifi-

ized to do .

thirtydays on the amount of callon each share of stock,
and of the time of payment ; and should any stockholder
failto pay each callas may be made within thirtydays
after it fallsdue ,he shall pay one per cent. per month
for such failureuntil the call shallbe fullypaid; and for
failureto pay the call and interestfor six months , the
president and directorsmay forfeitto the corporation the
shares so unpaid ,and the amount which may have been
paid on such shares, and such forfeitedshares may be
soldby thepresidentat publicauctionforwhat theymay
bring, provided they bring the balance due thereon, or
the presidentmay forcepayment from such stockholder
by law .
§ 7. The road shallbe graded as deemed most advisable
by the president and directors; the width of the grade of

the road shall not be greater than thirty feet, and the
portion covered with metal or macadamized stone shall
not exceed sixteen feet; and the president and directors,
or a majority of them ,may fixthe route over which said
road shallpass; and by themselves ,servants,and agents
they arehereby authorizedto enterin and upon theland
and inclosures through and over which said intended
road may be thought proper to pass, and to examine and

pletion and repair of the road ; and in case the corpora-

Statutes .

of Montgomery and Powell,shallcertifyto the fact,toll-
gates may be put across said road ,not less than five
miles apart,or in that proportion for the whole length of
the said road ,and the corporation may collect tolls as
now provided by law .
§9. The presidentand directorsmay , from time to
time, appoint allnecessarytoll-gatherers,and to collect
tollfrom everyperson using saidroad.
§ 10. If any person liableto pay tollson said road
shall,with intentto defraud the company ,pass through

lessenor evade the payment of toll,shallpracticeany

ing,for each and every offense,shallforfeitand pay to
said company ,over and above the tolls,a sum not less
than one dollar nor more than five dollars,recoverable
beforea justiceof the peace in an actionof debt,in the
name of the corporation, and for itssole use .
§11 . Whenever the county court of eitherof said coun-
ties shall enter an order that the said road is so far out of
orderin itscounty thattollsshouldnot be paid,then the

§ 12. That the president and directorsshallkeep a fair
account of all the receiptsand expenditures of the com-
pany , which shall at all times be open for inspection of
any stockholder,and be laid before the stockholdersat

the presidentand directorsshall,at every annual meet-

the shareholders: Provided,however, That ifthe net pro-
ceeds of the tolls,at the rates herein allowed ,shall not
produce a dividend of net profitsof six per cent. per
annum on the cost of said road , the sum may be in-
creased so as to produce six per cent.per annum ; and if
the said rates for one year should produce more than
twelve per cent.per annum ,they shallbe reduced so as
not to produce a greater than twelve per cent.per annum .
§ 13. All gate-keepers shallgive a bond ,when required
by the company , for the faithfulperformance of their
duty ; and all persons guilty of tearing down or in any-
wise defacing any superstructure on said road belonging

to the company shallbe finable,before a justiceof the
peace ,not lessthan two nor more than ten dollars,in the
name and to the use of said company .
§ 14. All wheel-carriagespassing on said road other
wheel -carriagesshallkeep on the righthand side,leav-
ing at leastone half of the track on the other side free
and clear for other carriages to pass and re-pass,except
when overtaking or passing a carriage of slow draught ,
when they may pass on either side of the slow carriage;
and any driver offending herein shall be finable in the
sum of five dollars,to be recoverable by the company
before a justiceof the peace ,besides being liableto the
person injuredin alldamages which he may sustain.
§ 15.The presidentand directorsmay purchase, or
otherwise have condemned ,in the same way as land for
the road,a sufficientquantityof ground to erect a toll-
house and other buildings and fixtures, not exceeding one
acre ; but such building shallnot include any dwelling or
other house of the owner without his consent , nor his
garden ,yard ,or orchard; nor shall it prevent him from
having free access to the road from his farm or residence.
§ 16. That nothing in this act contained shall be so
construed as to impose toll-fees upon the owners of lands
through which the road shall pass,theirhorses,cattle,or
other stockpassing on said road from one partof their
farm to another,or to impose toll-feesupon personspass-
ing to or from any funeral,or upon voters going to or
returning from any general electionin said counties; nor
upon personson the Sabbath going to or returningfrom
any publicworship,or upon personsgoing to or return-
ing from any grist-mill,whose business at said mill is to
have grinding done .
§ 17. The presidentand directorsshallhave power to
reduce the rates of toll in cases of persons or families
having frequent occasions to pass through the gate ,and
instead thereof may charge a reasonable annual tollor
compensation for such person or family; allpersons mak-
ing these annual contractsshallbe reported by the treas
urer .

and Powell counties,be , and the same are hereby , au-

§ 22. That no corporation subscribing stock in said
company shall be entitled to more than one vote for
every ten shares of stock so subscribed.
Approved April 28, 1880 .

CHAPTER 1292 .

State and county taxes,salesunder decreesor judgments in Lewis county,
and to regulatethe compensation therefor.

of Kentucky :

and special commissioners making sales of real estate
under execution, or by virtueof judgments , where the
execution or judgment is for two hundred dollars and
over, shalladvertisethe time and place of salein one
newspaper ,if published within the county ,fortwo weeks ,
by weekly insertions, prior to the time of making the
sale .
§2 . That sheriffsshalladvertisethe time and place of
sales of land for State and county taxes in one newspa-
per , if published within the county , for four weeks , by
weekly insertions,prior to the time of sale.
§3 . That the publisherof such newspaper shallbe al-
lowed eighty cents per linealinch of the usual type of such
newspaper for the firstinsertion, and fortycents for the
subsequent insertionof such advertisements as are pro-
vided forin firstsectionof thisact,to be taxed as costsin
the action or proceedings ,and twenty -fivecents for each
listof taxable property,as requiredby second section,to
be taxed up as costs of sale.
§ 4. That alllaws or partsof laws in conflictherewith
are hereby repealed.
§ 5. This actshallapply only to Lewis county.
§ 6. That thisact shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 29, 1880.

CHAPTER 1294 .
AN ACT relatingto the Vanceburg , Dry Run , and Kinniconick Turnpike
Road Company .

of Kentucky :
§1 . That any person maliciouslyor willfullyinjuring
theVanceburg ,Dry Run ,and KinniconickTurnpike Road
by the use of dead -locks, or in any other manner , shall
be guilty of a misdemeanor ,and ,on conviction by a jus-
ticeof the peace of Lewis county ,or other officerhaving
competent jurisdiction,shallbe finednot lessthan two
nor more than ten dollars for each offense .
§2 . That allfines and forfeituresfor violation of any
laws relating to the Vanceburg , Dry Run , and Kinnico-
nick Turnpike Road Company , shallbe paid into the
treasuryof saidcompany and used fortheirbenefit.

CHAPTER 1296 .
AN ACT to arrange the businessof the Union circuitcourt, and directing
the clerk to docket the cases in accordance therewith .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . Hereafter the clerk of the Union circuit court is
empowered and directed,in docketing criminal and penal
cases in said court,to set them for trialin the firsttwo
weeks of the term , and no such case shall be set for trial
during the thirdweek of the term ,but some of such cases
may be set forthe fourth week of the term .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 803
§2 . In docketing ordinary actions in said court, the
clerk shallplace them allfor the second day of the term ,
on which day saidcases shallbe called,and judgments
rendered when no defense is made ,unless time be given
untilthe fifthday of the term to make defense.
§3 . When said ordinarydocket shallhave once been
called, as herein provided, itshallbe the duty of the
court, on the fifthday of the term ,to call the cases left
open , and either to render judgment , if no defense be
made , or to set such cases for trialon and after the thir-
teenth day of the term , as he may deem advisable. On
the second day,the court may give time for the comple-
tion of the pleadings.
§ 4. In setting cases for trial,as provided in the third
section,the court shallascertainwhether a jury trialwill
be demanded ; and ,unlessa jury trialshallbe then de-
manded by one of the parties,and noticeof record,the
rightto a jury trialshall be deemed waived ; and in set-
ting cases, the court shall, as far as practicable, place
the jury cases together consecutively.
§5 . By the thirteenthday of each term the clerkshall
make a docket of the cases as set by the court. One
such docket shallbe placed at an acceptableplace in the
court-room for publicinspection,and one shallbe pro-
vided by the clerk for the use of the court.
§ 6. The cases shall be called for trial in the order set
as herein provided, and may be postponed to another
day of the term ,or continued as by existinglaw .
§7 . Witnesses in ordinaryactionsshallbe subpœnaed
for the thirteenthday of each term .
§ 8. All laws in conflictwith thislaw are repealed; and
thisact shalltake effectfrom June 1st, 1880.
Approved April 29, 1880 .

CHAPTER 1300 .
AN ACT to authorizethe constructionof temporary driftsor booms in Sta-
tionCamp creek, in Estillcounty, and to prescribea penalty fora failure
to remove the same .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shallbe lawful for any person or persons
engaged in driving or rafting logs and timber in Station

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 805
Camp creek,a navigable stream of thisCommonwealth ,
to construct,from time to time,temporary,such driftsor
booms across said creek,at or near its mouth , in Estill
county ,as may be necessary for the securing of logs and
timber so driven or rafted; and each driftor boom may
remain in said creek for a period not exceeding ten days

driftor boom therein fora longer time than authorized by

CHAPTER 1304 .
AN ACT authorizingthe Lewis county court to build a bridge across Salt
Lick creek where the Vanceburg ,Salt Lick , Tollsboro, and Maysville
Turnpike Road crossesthe saidcreek.

of Kentucky :
§1 . The Lewis county court is authorized to provide
LOC . L., VOL . II- 52

810 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
The
for the construction of a bridge across Salt Lick creek
where the Vanceburg ,Salt Lick,Tollsboro,and Maysville
Turnpike Road crossessaid creek,at the Caines place.
To raisethe money to pay for said bridge, the Lewis
county court,at any regular term of said court,shallhave
the power to levy a tax not exceeding ten cents on each
one hundred dollars' worth of taxable property in the
county , to be collectedby the sheriffin the same man-
ner as the county tax iscollected,and paid over as the
court may direct. When the bridge isfinishedthe county
shallhave stockin the saidturnpike to the extentof the
costof said bridge. The county court may issuebonds
of the county sufficientto pay for said bridge, which
bonds shalldraw six per cent.interest; and the tax levied
shallbe pledged to pay said bonds and interest.
bonds shallbe issued payable in one, two , and three
years,and the tax leviedshallbe sufficientto pay the
same and interest; and when the bonds and interestare
paid ,then the tax shall cease . The tax shall be levied
forthe year 1880,and no bonds shallbe issued to antici-
pate the tax for 1880,but the same shallbe paid over,
when collected, on the order of the court The bonds
issued shallbe sold at theirface,and no discount shallbe
allowed in the sale. The bonds shallbe signed by the
county judge and attestedby the county clerk. The
bonds shall be issued in sums of not less than one hun-
dred dollars nor more than one thousand dollars . The
contractorsmay take the bonds in payment forbuilding
the bridge,but shalltake the same at theirface,and with-
outdiscount. The county courtshallappointa commis-
sioner to let out the contract for the building of said
bridge, which contract shall not be binding until the
same is confirmed by the county judge,indorsed on the
contract,and signed by him .
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 29, 1880 .

CHAPTER 1313 .
AN ACT to charterthe North Middletown and PrescottTurnpike Company .
WHEREAS , The North Middletown and Owingsville Old
Dirt Road , in Bourbon county ,is so rough and muddy ,
and so worn and neglected,as to have become almost im-
passable and uselessas a highway , and the effortsto re-
pair ithave become a great burden to those persons living
on and near to it,and who are dependent upon itto get
to the churches,schools,mills,and otherplacesof busi-
ness ; for remedy whereof ,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That J. W. Prescott and Mary E. Prescott are
hereby authorized to grade and macadamize one mile of
the said road at theirown expense ,beginning at the ter-
minus at North Middletown,and extendingto and along
the lineof the farm of said Mary E. Prescott,situatedon
said road .
§2 . They are granted the rightto occupy and usethe
material on said mile of road -bed ,and remove the same
from one place to another in grading and constructinga
turnpike, which shallnot be lessthan thirtyfeetwide,
graded not less than twenty -seven feet,metaled not less
than sixteen feet in width , with rock well broken ,and
spread so as to measure eight inches deep on the sides
and thirteeninchesin the middle of the road,with grad-
ual slope to the sides; the grade of the road not to exceed
two and a half degrees of elevation.
§ 3. They shallfollow the existingroad -bed , unlessfor
the purpose of straighteninga crook or shorteningthe
distance: Provided, The owner of the land adjoiningthe
road at the placeof the proposed diversionshallconsent
to a change .
§ 4. They shallconstructsubstantialculvertsor bridges
across ravines when needed ; and when the work of turn-
piking authorizedshallbe completed,they may erecta
gate acrossthe said turnpike,and collecttollfrom the
passersover the road at saidgate at the ratesestablished
in theGeneral Statutes,underthe title" Turnpike,Gravel.
and Plank Roads ."

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 829
§5 . It shallbe known as the North Middletown and
PrescottTurnpike Road ; and the saidJ. W. Prescottand
Mary E. Prescott, their heirs and assigns, shall have
the benefit of all remedies for the collection of tolls and
protectionof the turnpike, and shallbe subjectto like
penalties and responsibiltiesfor overcharges or failureto
keep the road in proper repairthatdevolve upon officers
and managers of incorporatedturnpike road companies
under the general laws of this Commonwealth .
§6. This act shalltake effectfrom date of itsapproval .
Approved April 29, 1880 .
CHAPTER 1314 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled "An act to incorporate the North Middle-
town and Cane Ridge Turnpike Road Company ," approved March 9,1867.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the act,entitled"An act to incorporate the
North Middletown and Cane Ridge Turnpike Road Com-
pany ," approved March 9,1867,be,and the same ishere-
by , amended as follows, to-wit: The said company is
empowered to make and construct, in extension of the
road as projected in said act,a road on the McAdam's
plan ,from Bramblett'sshop (a point near to the present
terminus of said road on the Paris and Flat Rock Turn-
pike Road ) tothe Parisand Jackstown Turnpike Road ,at
or near Bayliss'sCross Roads , in Bourbon county , and
thence to the ford on Hinkston creek, at Young's old
mill . Such extension of said road shall be located , as
far as is practicable,on the line or bed of the old road
between Bramblett'sshop and Bayliss'sCrossRoads ; but
wherever the grade ,or any material saving of distance
may require a departure from the bed of said old road ,
saidcompany shallhave the rightto acquireland neces
sary for the construction and completion of said road , in
the manner and on the terms prescribedby the act to
which this is an amendment .
§2 . All the rights,powers,duties,and responsibilities
conferredand imposed on thepresident,directors,officers,

830 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and company of saidNorth Middletown and Cane Ridge
Turnpike Road Company , by the said act of March 9,
1867, relativeto the construction,operation,and manage-
ment of the said road as originallyprojected, are con-
ferredand made obligatoryon them with referenceto the
construction,operation,and management of the road as
extended .
§3 . The Bourbon county court is authorized to sub-
scribe stock to aid in the construction of such extension
of said road ,on the same terms and in the same manner ,
and with likepowers to provide for the payment of such
subscription,as is provided in the seventh(7) sectionof
the said act of March 9 , 1867 .
§4 . Said company shall have the right to receive and
collectsubscriptionsto the capitalstock of said company
for the purpose of raisingmoney with which to construct
and complete such extension of said road , and for every
fiftydollarsso subscribedby any individual,company ,or
corporation,whenever the same shall be paid, a certifi-
cate of stock shallbe issued to such individual,company ,
or corporation by said turnpike road company ; and every
such share-holder shall be entitledto all the privileges,
and shallbe subjectto allthe responsibilitiesof allother
share -holders .
§5 . This act shallbe in force from itspassage .
Approved April 29, 1880.

CHAPTER 1330 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe United StatesMutual Aid Association.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the following-named gentlemen : Henry
Gunkel ,T. B. Youtsey, H. M. Heally, John W. Schnei-
der, L. R. Hawthorne , John Todd , Robt . Dodsworth ,T.
A. Widrig ,R. W. Nelson , and R. A. Dameron , be ,and
they are hereby,incorporated,under the name and style
of the United States Mutual Aid Association .
§2 . The principalofficeof saidassociationshallbe in
thecityof Newport ,in the county of Campbell,and State
of Kentucky .
§3 . The object and manner of carrying on the busi-
ness of said associationshallbe as follows : The purpose
of the associationshallbe for the mutual protection and
reliefof its members , and for the payment of stipulated
sums of money to the members , or the heirs of deceased
members ,of the association. Any person not lessthan
eighteen nor more than sixtyyears of age , of sound mind
and in good health, may become a member of said asso-
ciation, by signing an application, according to the pre-
scribed form , answering satisfactorilyallquestions as to
applicant'shealth, habits, and family historycontained
in said application, and the approval, by the proper
authoritiesof the association, of the applicationand
payment by the applicantof ten dollars. Thereupon a
certificateof membership shallbe issued to the appli-
cant,signed by the president and secretary, and having
affixedtheretothe corporateseal. The saidapplication
and certificateshall constitute the contract between the
member and the association; and each shallspecifythe
amount tobe paid and when - whether at thedeath ofthe
member or at a particular time , if the member shall so
long live. The fee of admission to membership and
assessments shall be the same to all members , and the
benefitsshallbe apportioned according to the ages of
members at the time of admission .
§4 . When a certificateof membership becomes due,and
the same has been proven,noticeby mail shallbe given

LAWS ' OF KENTUCKY . 861
to each member , addressed to his or her respectivepost-
office,requiringeach topay one dollarwithinthirtydays.
Of the amount so collected, eighty per cent. shallbe paid
to the person or persons entitledthereto within sixtydays
afterproof of claim : Provided, That in no case shall
more be paid than the amount named in the certificate.
§5 . Any member failingto pay his or her assessment ,
as requiredin the preceding section,shallforfeithis or
her membership and allmoneys paid to the association;
but such membership may be restoredwithinone year by
paying allback dues,and producing satisfactoryproof of
good health.
§ 6. No assessment shall be made for expenses ,but the
same shallbe paid out of any surplusmoney in the treas-
ury . The officersof said associationshallbe a president,
vicepresident,secretary,treasurer,generalagent,medical
director,attorney, not lessthan five directors, and such
other officersas the association may deem proper . The
association shall be organized and itsofficerselected on
the firstMonday in May , 1880, and the officerselected
annually thereafter.
§7. This associationshallhave perpetualsuccession;
may sue and be sued by itscorporatename ; may have
and use a common seal,and alter the same at pleasure,
and may make allnecessaryrules,by -laws,and regula-
tions for the management and carrying on of itsaffairsas
to the directorsmay seem right and proper ,not inconsist-
ent with the Constitution and laws of this State or of the
United States .
§ 8. Whenever said associationhas issued(3,000) three
thousand certificates,the partiesholding the same may
have said certificatereduced to one half of the original
amount by surrendering the certificatethen held, and
making application in writing to the association, and
paying pro rata assessments thereafter,and one dollar
for the certificate .
§9 . The associationmay acquire and hold real and
personal estatenot exceeding ($ 50,000) fiftythousand dol-
lars; and the privatepropertyof officersand members
shall not be liable for the debts of the association .

862 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 10.No part of the interestof any member of the
associationshallbe subjectto any debt,liability,or legal
or equitableprocessagainstthem ,orwhich shallalienate
the benefitsfrom theirlegal heirs.
§ 11. This act shall be in force from and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 29, 1880.

ley,James D. Smith,Joseph N. Driskell,John C. Spring-
gate, Thos . Edwards , and Benjamin Sanders , jr., or a

exceed twenty thousand dollars,and shallbe divided into
shares of twenty -fivedollarseach .

book providedby them forthatpurpose,receivesubscrip-
tions of stock to said road company to the effectfollow-
ing, to-wit : " We ,the subscribers,do respectivelypromise
topay to the Eldorado and Dugansville Turnpike Road
Company the sum of twenty -five dollars for each share
of stocksetoppositeour names ,in such proportionsand
at such times as shallbe determined on by saidcorpora-
tion: Provided,however, That said callsshall not be over
the one fifthpart of the stock subscribed, and sixtydays
to intervene .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 863
§4 . That when one thousand dollars of said stock is
subscribed,itshallbe the duty of saidcommissioners,or
such of them as will act,to call a meeting of said stock-
holdersby posting noticeat three or more places along
the lineof said road,at leastten days beforethe time
therein specifiedto meet ,appointing the time and place
of such meeting . Such of said commissioners as may
be presentat saidmeeting shallproceed to organizesaid
company by electingfivedirectors,one of whom shallbe
by them chosen as president. In the electionof a board
of directorseach stockholdershallbe eligibleto vote,and
shall be entitled to one vote for each share of stock sub-
scribedby him or her,and may cast the same in person
or by writtenproxy. The directorsshallappointa treas-
urerand such otherofficersas they may deem necessary;
and said treasurer shall,before entering upon the duties
of his office,take an oath to faithfullydischarge the du-
ties of his said office,and enter into such bond as the
directorsmay deem proper.
§5 . Any person or persons making subscriptionsto the
capital stock of said turnpike road company may pay
the same in property,personal or real,or in labor, the
same being indicated in writing at the time of making
such subscription. And it is hereby furtherprovided,
that the price for such labor shall be at the rate of
customary for such work ,and may be agreed upon by
the company and such subscriber; but in the event of
disagreement , the matter shall be determined by three
disinteresteddiscreet men , citizensof that community ,
one of whom shallbe chosen by the parties respectively;
the umpire to be chosen by the said referees; and in like
manner shallthe value of any propertyso subscribedbe
determined .
§6 . Upon the electionand qualificationof said presi-
dent and directors,they shallbe a body -politicand cor-
porate, by the name and styleaforesaid; and by that
name may sue and be sued, plead and be impleaded,
contract and be contracted with,answer and be answered ,
in any of the courtsin thisCommonwealth ; may hold

and acquireand convey allreal and personalproperty
for the purpose of constructingsaid road and keeping
the same in repair,and have allother privilegesand fran-
chisesincidenttoa corporation. Saidcompany may also
go upon the lands or inclosurescontiguous or near to their
road to examine any land , quarries of stone, or other
material necessary for the construction or use of said
road ; and if the directors and owner or owners of said
land or other materialcannot agree as to priceof land
or other material,the officersof said company may ap-
ply to the Mercer county court for a writ of ad quod
damnum to assess the damages to the land or value of
material to be used ; and after a tender of the amount
of the damages to such owner ,the company may , with
any and every appliance necessary in the construction of
said road ,enter upon such lands and make said road .
§7 . The whole width of said road shall not exceed
thirtyfeet,and thatpartcovered with metal not lessthan
fourteen feet. The depth of the broken metal on said
road,the sizethereof,and the grade of said road , as also
the elevation,shallbe determined by the directors,a ma-
jority of whom shall constitute a quorum to transact
business .
§ 8. When , in the judgment of the directors, a suffi-
cient amount of stock is subscribed to construct said
road , or as much as two and one half miles thereof,they
may proceed in such manner as they may deem best to
contract for the constructionof said road or portion
thereof ; and when as much as two miles of said road
shallhave been completed, they may erect a toll-gate
thereon ,and collecttollsin the manner and at rates not
exceeding those now regulated by General Statutes.
§9 . Said commissioners shall locate said road on the
most practicableroute,and for that purpose may ,with
theirengineers,chain-carriers,& c.,enterupon the lands
and highways along said route, and lay off and locate
the same .
§ 10. The president and board of directorsof said com-
pany shallhold officefor one year,and untiltheirsuc

cessorsareelectedand qualified; and an electionforthat
purpose shallbe held annuallyat such time and place as
shallbe determined by said board .

sage .
Approved May 1, 1880 .

veyed to it in payment or satisfactionof any debt or

§3 . Before this act shall go in force or take effectit
must be approved and accepted by a majorityin interest
of itsstockholders,at a meeting ordered or called for that

this act shall be considered and held to be in full force
and effect .
Approved May 1 , 1880.
CHAPTER 1339 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof the Eminence and Franklinton Turnpike Com-
pany , in Henry county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the board of directors of that part of the
road next to Franklinton ,of the Eminence and Franklin-
ton Turnpike Company , are authorized to establishan
additional toll-gate on that end of said turnpike road , to
be erected within one mile of Point Pleasant meeting-
house .
§2 . That at the additional toll-gate hereby granted
there shall not be collected a greater rate of toll than
that provided for in the General Statutes,the rate col-
lectedto be in proportionto the distancetraveled,there
having already been established a toll-gate in one and
one fourth mile of the town of Franklinton .
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 1, 1880 .

§1 . That an act,entitled" An act to amend the charter
of the Columbia and BurksvilleTurnpike Road Company ,
establishinga one -half toll-gate in less than one mile of
the town of Burksville," approved February 18, 1870, be,
and the same ishereby ,repealed .
§ 2. No tollscollectedon the part of the road in the
county of Adair shall be used in constructingor repair-
ing the road in the county of Cumberland .
Approved May 1, 1880.
CHAPTER 1341 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof the policejudge of Lebanon , in Marion county.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That thejudge of the police court of Lebanon ,in
Marion county,Kentucky ,shallreceiveas salarythe sum
of two hundred and fiftydollarsper annum in addition
to the fees now allowed him by law . The said salary
shallbe paid by the board of trusteesof the town of
Lebanon , out of the ordinaryrevenuesof said town of
Lebanon . This sum shallnot be paid by said board
unlessso orderedby a majorityof the board of trustees.
§2 . This to take effectand be in force from and after
itspassage .
Approved May 1 , 1880.
CHAPTER 1342 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof F. B. Huston , sheriffof Union county.
WHEREAS , F. B. Huston qualifiedas collectorof the
revenue for Union county for the year 1878, the office
of sheriffbeing vacant, with the understandingthathe
shouldreceiveten per cent.on allrevenue collectedby

him and paid into the Treasury, and no one could be
induced to make said collectionfor less; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
ofKentucky :

ment with said Huston ,ishereby authorized and directed
to allow said Huston ten per cent. on all revenue col-
lectedby him and paid into the Treasury for the year
1878 .
§2 . This act to take effectfrom itspassage .

CHAPTER 1343 .
AN ACT for the benefit of J. A. B. Huddleston and Wm .A. Walthall .
WHEREAS , The Government of the United States has
been paying the State of Kentucky the money expend-
ed by the Stateforthe purpose of arming,clothing,and
feeding troops who were enlistedeither for the national
orStateservicein the latewar ,and isstillpaying, and
willingto pay,the money expended as aforesaid; there-
fore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That J. A. B. Huddleston be , and he is hereby ,
allowed the sum of seven hundred and fiftydollars for
services rendered as First Lieutenant of Company D ,
South Cumberland Battalion,from the 17th of December ,
1864,untilthe 13th day of May ,1865,conditioned that the
same be firstpaid to the State by the General Govern-
ment; and the Auditorof the State ishereby directedto
draw his warrant in favor of said Huddleston , on the
Treasurerof the State,for the amount above specified,
whenever he shall ascertain that said amount for said
serviceshas been paid by the General Government to the
State ..
§2 . That upon the same conditions, and under the
same restrictionsas specifiedin the foregoing,the Aud-
itorshallalso draw hiswarrant on the Treasurer ,in favor

road ,or their necessary works or buildings,the president
and directors,or their agents,or those contracting with
them for constructing or repairing the same ,may imme-
diately take and use the same ,they having firstcaused
the propertywanted to be viewed by a jury formed as
hereinbeforeprescribed; and itshallnot be necessary,
aftersuch view ,in order to the occupation of the same ,
to wait the issue of the proceedings upon such inquest ;
and the inquest of the jury , afterthe payment or tender
of such valuation,shallbe a bar to all actions for taking
and using such property, whether before or after such
confirmation or payment of such valuation.
§ 10. The rates for freightand passengers shallnot ex-
ceed the ratesnow charged by roadsoperatedunder the
laws of thisState, and shallbe subject to such legislation
as may be equal to other roads in the Commonwealth .
§ 11. That the General Assembly reserves the rightto
alter, amend ,or repeal thisact, as provided in an act,
entitled " An act reserving power to amend or repeal
chartersand other laws," approved February 14th, 1856.
§ 12. That said railway company shallsurvey and locate
the route of said lineof railway,and begin activelyto
constructthe same ,within the period of three years , and
complete the same within fiveyears from the passage of
this act .
§ 13. This act shallbe in force from and after its pas-
sage .
Approved May 1 , 1880.

898 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1346 .
AN ACT to incorporatethePeople'sCityRailwayCompany .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That R. S. Veech ,Samuel Look ,T. N. Hays ,their
associatesand successors,be,and theyare hereby,created
a body-corporateand politic,by the name of the People's
CityRailway Company ,of Louisville,with allthe pow-
ers and authority incident to corporators, for the pur-
poses hereinaftermentioned .
§2 . The corporation is hereby authorized and em-
powered to construct,maintain ,and operate a singleor
double track railway , with allnecessary and convenient
tracks for turn-outs, side-tracks, and appendages ,in the
city of Louisville,in, on ,over , and along such street or
streets,highway or highways ,alleyor alleys,within the
present or future limitsof the cityof Louisville, as the
general council of said cityshall,in accordance with sec-
tion fifteenof the citycharter, authorize said corporation
so to do ,in such manner and upon such terms and condi-
tions,and with such rightsand privileges,as the general
council may ,by contract or otherwise with said corpora-
tionor any of them ,prescribe; but saidcorporationshall
not be liablefor any baggage carried on said railway
kept in and under the care of its owner ,his servant,or
agent .
§3 . Said corporation isalso hereby authorized to lease
or purchase other railroadsor parts of railroads,itsrights
and franchises,and allproperty appertaining thereto,up-
on such terms as may be agreed upon between the par-
ties .
§ 4. The capitalstock of said company shallnot exceed
three hundred thousand dollars, and shall be divided into
shares of one hundred dollars each , and be issued and
transferredin such manner and upon such conditions as
the board of directorsof said corporation may direct.
§5 . The affairsof said corporationshallbe managed
by five directors,one of whom shallbe president- all of
whom shallbe stockholdersin said corporation. The

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 899
first board of directors shall consist of the within named
incorporators,who shallcontinuein officeuntiltheirsuc-
cessorsshallbe electedand qualified. They may adopt
such by -laws ,rules and regulations,for the government
of said corporation and the management of its affairs
and business,as they may deem proper,not inconsistent
with the laws of this State .
§ 6. The said corporators, or any of them , may open
books of subscription to the capital stock of said com-
pany herein incorporated; and such books of subscription
may be opened and subscriptionreceivedat such times
and places,and upon such notice thereof, as any three of
saidcorporatorsmay deem rightand proper.
§7 . Said corporation isalso authorized to extend such
railways as they may be authorizedto constructunder
this act within three miles of the limits of the city of
Louisville; and to enable the said corporation to construct
any railway authorizedby thisact,the saidcorporation
is invested with the rightto acquire such land or right of
way as may be necessary to operate said railroad.
§ 8. The said corporation may borrow money to an
amount not to exceed the capital stock of said compa-
ny ,and issue bonds of one hundred dollars, fivehundred
dollars, or one thousand dollars, and may pledge and
mortgage the property and franchises of said corpora-
tion, including the track, fixtures, rolling-stock, appur-
tenances ,and real estate belonging to the corporation,to
secure payment of the same .
§ 9. The rate of fare shall not exceed five cents for
each passenger ,except when the cars or carriages shall
be chartered for specificpurposes .
§ 10.The cars or carriagesused upon said railway
shallbe operated by animal power , and shallbe run
under such regulationsas the municipal authoritiesof
the city of Louisvillemay prescribeby ordinancesor
agreement .
§ 11. Nothing in this act is to be construed so as to
limitor affectthe rightsand powers of the cityof Lou-
isvillenow vested in said city or the general council
thereof,and is not to affectthe legal rightsof other com-
1

900 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
panies , or the rights or power of the city to agree or
contract with other persons or companies to build street
railroadsin said city.
§ 12. The railway authorized to be constructed under
this act shall, in good faith, be commenced within two
years after the passage of this act,otherwise all rights
and franchises granted hereunder shallcease and deter-
mine .
§ 13. This act shall in nowise be construed as to au-
thorizethiscorporationto run over the track of any
other railroad a greater distance than has already been
granted or determined by existing contracts with the
cityof Louisville: Provided,however, That such pro rata
compensation be made as shall be agreed upon with
such company or companies for the construction, use,
and maintaining such part of track so used or utilized.
§ 14. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 1 , 1880 .
CHAPTER 1348 .
AN ACT for the benefitof the Union county court, authorizingsaid court
to appoint a collectorof county revenue.
Be itenuctedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . When the sheriffof Union county failsto give
bond for the collectionof the revenue ,or when there is
a vacancy in the officeof sheriff, eitherfrom a failureto
elector qualify,the county court of Union county ,a ma-
jorityof thejusticesof thepeace being presentand con-
curringtherein, shallappoint a collectorof the county
revenue for that year , and agree upon a compensation
therefor,and shalltake bond , with good security, for the
faithfulperformance of the dutiesrequiredby law . Such
collectorshall be governed by the laws regulating the
duties of sheriffsin the collectionof the revenue , except
as to compensation ; and he and his sureties shall be
jointly and severally liable to the Commonwealth , and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 901
allothers, in the same manner that the sheriffand his
sureties would have been .
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 1,1880 .
CHAPTER 1350 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof the Cynthiana , Paddy's Run , and Lair'sStation
Turnpike Road Company , in Harrison county .
WHEREAS , The Cynthiana , Paddy's Run , and Lair's
StationTurnpike Road Company , of Harrison county,
owes an indebtedness of about fourteen hundred dollars,
contractedin the originalconstructionof said turnpike
road; and whereas , some of the stockholders of said
company subscribed and paid one thousand nine hundred
and sixty-fivedollarsto pay offother indebtednessof
said company contracted in the originalconstruction of
said turnpike road,upon the faithof a mortgage executed
by the president and board of directorsof said company ;
and whereas ,since the passage of the act of the General
Assembly reducingthe tollsupon turnpikeroads in this
Commonwealth , the tollscollectedupon said turnpike
road have not been sufficientto keep the same in proper
repair,and the aforesaid indebtedness has been continu-
ally increasing since the passage of said act.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That T. J. Megibben , John Lair,John M. Lair,J.
A. McKee ,and W. D. Urmston ,the presidentand direct-
ors of the Cynthiana , Paddy's Run , and Lair's Station
Turnpike Road Company ,be, and they are hereby, au-
thorizedand empowered tosellthe turnpikeroad,and all
other propertyand thefranchisesof saidcompany ,forthe
payment of the indebtednessnamed in the preamble to
this act .
§2 . That said sale shall be made after advertisement
of the time,place,and terms of sale,by postingwritten
or printed notices at the court-house door in Cynthiana ,
and at threeor more publicplacesalong the lineof said
turnpikeroad,and by publishingthe same in one news

902 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
paper publishedin Cynthiana for one month before the
day of sale,at publicauction,to the highestbidder.
§3 . That the proceeds of said sale shallbe applied,
first, to the payment of the indebtedness firstnamed in
the preamble of thisact; and if said proceeds should be
more than sufficientto pay said indebtedness , then the
balance shall be applied pro rata to the payment of the
subscriptionssecured by mortgage as stated in the pre-
amble to this act; and if said balance should be more
than sufficientto pay said subscriptionsin full,then the
excess shallbe divided pro rata among the general stock-
holders of the company .
§4 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 1, 1880.
CHAPTER 1351 .
AN ACT to amend an act , entitled " An act to amend an act to amend
an act to amend and reduce into one the severalacts concerning the
management of the Wilderness Turnpike Road ," & c.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . The toll-gates now on said road are hereby abol-
ished,and no tollsshall be collectedat said gates after
the expirationof the presentleases; and such gates as
are not leased shall be removed immediately , by order of
the county court, in each county where they are located.
§ 2. This act shallapply to the Madison branch of said
road .
§3 . It shallbe the duty of the county courtsof Bell,
Knox , Laurel,Rockcastle, and Jackson countiesto ap-
point overseers and allot hands to work said roads, as
are allottedon othercounty roadsin thisCommonwealth .
§ 4. This act shallbe in force from itspassage .
Approved May 1, 1880 .

CHAPTER 1353 .
AN ACT toamend an act,entitled" An acttoincorporatethe Elizabechtown,
Lexington , and Big Sandy RailroadCompany ," approved January 29th,
1869, and the variousactsamending the same .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Elizabethtown ,Lexington ,and Big Sandy
Railroad Company may ,and itis hereby,authorizedto
borrow any sum of money ,in addition to the amount
heretoforeauthorized,which in the judgment of itsdirect-
ors may be requisiteto build, complete , and equip any
branches which it is now authorized to build to its line
of railroad, and to issue its bonds to such amount as
said directors may determine ,and to secure the same by
mortgage or trustdeed upon such of itsproperty and
franchisesas said directorsmay deem proper: Provided,
That the authorityhereby given to borrow money to com-
plete and equip any branches ,and to issuebonds therefor,
and to secure the same by mortgage or trust deed ,shall
not be exercised by said Elizabethtown ,Lexington , and
Big Sandy RailroadCompany untilsaidroad iscompleted
from Lexington to the Big Sandy river,in Kentucky ,and
trains shallbe running over the line of said road,together
with the road controlledby same from Lexington to the
Big Sandy river,in Kentucky ; and no debt contracted by
or under the authorityof thisact shallhave priorityover
any existingdebt ; nor shallthe authorityhereby given be
used to impair the value of any existing debt or liability,
or of any stockin said road; nor shallanything herein
be construed to impair the value of any contract entered
into by the New York board of directorsof said road with
the counties along its line.
§2 . This act shall take effectand be in force from its
passage .
Approved May 1, 1880 .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 925
CHAPTER 1363 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe saleof spirituous, vinous,or malt liquorsin the
town of Blandville, Ballard county, and to provide for taking a vote on
that question .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1. That itshallbe unlawfulforany personto sell
within the town of Blandville, Ballard county ,any spir-
ituous, vinous, or malt liquors,or any mixture thereof.
§2 Whoever shall be guilty of a violationof this act
shall,upon conviction thereof, be fined for each offense
not lessthan fiftydollarsand not more than one hundred
dollars .
§3. That in orderto take the senseof the qualified
votersof said town as to whether they are for or against
the provisions of thisact,itshallbe the duty of the board
of trusteesof said town to appoint three persons to take
the vote in said town , two of whom shall be named as
judges and the other the clerk of said election. The mar ..
shal of said town shall act as sheriff of said election .
§4 . That the clerk of the board of trustees shall make
out and deliver to the clerk of the election a poll-book
containing two columns . One column shall be ," For the
sale of whisky and other liquors;" the other column shall
be," Against the sale of whisky and other liquors;" and
on the firstSaturdayin June,1880,afterpostingnotices
of saidelectionin three placesin said town ,the officers
named shall,at some place in said town to be selected
by them ,hold said election, at which every qualifiedvo-
ter of said town shallbe entitled to vote; and of each
and every votervotingat saidelection,the questionshall
be distinctlyasked : " Are you for or against the sale of
whisky or other liquorsin the town of Blandville?" and
the clerk shall record the vote according to the answer .
§5 . The poll-book shallbe sealed and delivered by the
town marshal to the clerk of board of trustees of said
town , who shallsafelykeep same untilthe meeting of the
board ,which shall be on Tuesday following, when the
clerk shalldeliver the sealed poll-book to the chairman
of the board ,and he shallopen same ,and with the assist-

926 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ance of the other members of the board , count and com-
pare the vote; and if itisascertainedthata majorityof
those voting at said electionare in favor of the provisions
of thisact,or have voted against the sale of whisky and
other liquorsin said town , then the said board shall
order the clerkto statethe facts upon the record-book of
said town , and then the provisions of this bill shall
become the law,and be in forcefrom and afterthe 15th
day of June , 1880 .
Approved May 3 , 1880 .

CHAPTER 1381 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof A. E. Adams , of Pike county .
WHEREAS , A. E. Adams on January 30th,1862,received
from the Treasury of the State of Kentucky the sum of
two hundred and eighty-five dollars and twelve cents,
which was paid said Adams for expenses and servicesin
organizing a company of State Guards under the exist

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 915
ing law ; and some time priorto the 20th of January , 1863 ,
in obedience to orders,said Adams ,with his company ,
reported to the Federal commandant and was mustered
into the regular service of the United States Govern-
ment ; and on the said 20th of January , 1863,said Adams
was required to refund the money so received as afore-
said to the Treasury ,and did do so,as shown by the books
in the Adjutant General's office; and whereas ,the United
States Government recognizesitsliabilityfortheexpenses
aforesaid , and has manifested its readiness and willing-
ness to pay claims such as this,but that said claims must
be allowed by Congress to the States and by the States
paid to the partiesentitled; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That A. E. Adams be ,and he is hereby ,allowed
the sum of two hundred and eighty-five dollars and
twelve cents for services rendered and expenses incurred
in organizing a company of State Guards in the year
1862 ; conditioned that the General Government first
make the payment of said amount to the State upon the
account aforesaid; in which event the Auditor is directed
to draw hiswarrant upon the Treasurerin favorof said
A. E. Adams for the amount above specified.
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 4 , 1880 .

sage .

CHAPTER 1395 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Farmers' Mutual Aid Association, of Bath
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth
ofKentucky :
§1 . That George Wilson,president; John T. Marshall,
secretary; Wm . D. Boyd ,D. B. Emmons ,Ephraim Cald-
well,supervisors,and allotherpersonswho shallbecome
members thereof in the manner herein prescribed,be ,and
theyare hereby,incorporatedand made a body-politic,by
the name of the Farmers ' Home Mutual Aid Association,
of Bath county, Kentucky , for the purpose of insuring
their respective dwelling-houses ,barns, and other build-
ings in the county of Bath against lossby fire,lightning,
or wind ; and by that name may sue and be sued ,plead
and be impleaded,appear,prosecute,and defend in any
courts of record or other place whatsoever ; may have
and use a common seal; may make , establish, and put
into execution such by -laws , ordinances,and resolutions,
not being contrary to the laws of this State or of the
United States, as may seem necessary for their govern-
ment , and for the management of their affairs, and to
carry into fulleffectthe purposes and provisions of this
charter .
§2 . Allpersonswho shallhereafterinsurewith the said
corporation, and also their heirs, executors, administra-
tors, and assigns continuing to be insured in said cor-
poration as is hereafterprovided, shallhereby become
members thereof during the period they may remain in-
sured by said corporation, and no longer .
§3 . The affairsof said association shallbe managed
by a board of directors, consistingof five members , a
president,secretary,and three supervisors,as hereinafter
provided. All vacancies happening in said board may
be filledby the remaining directorsfor the remainder of
the year for which they were elected: Provided, That
said board shall not be reduced to a less number than
three,inwhich event the members of theassociationshall
proceed by electionto fillallvacanciesuntilthe regular

962 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
election . Should such vacancies occur more than three
months priorto said regularor annual election,a major-
ity of the board shall constitutea quorum for the trans-
action of business .
§ 4. The personsfirstnamed in thisact shallbe first
directorsof saidcorporation, and shallcontinue in office
for one year after the passage of this act,and until the
firstelectionby the corporationof othersin theirstead,
and which board of directors shall hereafter be elected in
each year at such time and place in the county of Bath
asthecorporationintheirby-laws shallappoint,of which
electiondue noticeshallbe givenby thedirectors,by post-
ersorotherwise,at leasttwenty days immediatelypreced-
ing said election; and such election shallbe made by the
members ,or theirproxies,by a pluralityof votes,allowing
to each member one for every one hundred dollarsinsured
by said association. No person shallbe an officerin the
association who is not a member thereof ; and the board
of directors, at their firstmeeting after the passage of
thisact, shallelectfrom among theirnumber a president,
secretary, and treasurer,who shall hold their officeone
year , or untilothers are elected.
§5 . This association shall not insure any dwelling or
other building in the aggregate with insurance taken in
other companies for more than two thirds of its cash
value; and in case of fire,accidentby lightningor wind ,
the associationwill not pay more two thirdsof the cash
value thereof at the time of such loss ; nor shall the asso-
ciationhave the rightto assessorcollectany money from
any member of the association,unless there has been a
lossin said association: Provided,however,They shallhave
the right to assess and collect, annually , not exceeding
one fifthof one per cent.upon the amount insured by
each and everymember to pay contingentexpenses.
§6 Upon the applicationof any persons to become a
member of the association and paying to treasurer two
dollars,any three,or more if necessary, of the fivedirect-
orsshall go to his dwelling or other house to be insured,
and make a survey , fix a valuation,and the amount of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 963
risk that the association will take; and if the parties
agree, the directorsshallreport to the president, who
shall issue his certificateof insurance ,countersigned by
the secretary,bearing date at what time the riskcom-
mences running,a copy of which shallbe entered by the
secretary,and countersignedby the presidentin a book
kept for the purpose .
§7 . In case of loss or damage by fire, lightning, or
wind ,happening to any member ,upon propertyinsured
in and with said association,the said member shall give
noticethereofto the presidentor secretarywithinthirty
days from the time such loss or damage happened ; and
any three of the directors,upon a view of the same ,or in
such othermanner as may be necessarytoinsurea just
and correct estimate thereof, shall ascertain and deter-
mine the amount of such loss or damage ; and if the
party sufferingis not satisfiedwith the determination of
the directors,the question may be submitted to referees,
or the said partymay bring an actionagainst saidasso-
ciation for said lossor damage ,the next court held in and
for circuitof Bath county , and not afterwards, unless
said court shall be holden within sixtydays after said
determination ; but ifnot holden within that time ,then at
the next court holden in said county thereafter; and if,on
the trialof such action a greater sum be recovered than
the amount determined upon by the said directors, the
party sufferingshallhave judgment thereforagainstsaid
association , with interest thereon from the time of such
loss or damage and the cost of suit ; but if no more shall
be recovered than the amount aforesaid, the said party
shall become non -suited ,and the association shall recover
cost: Provided, however, That the judgment last men-
tioned shall in nowise affectthe claim of said suffering
party to the amount of lossor damage as determined by
the directorsaforesaid; and further, that execution shall
not issue or lien be enforced on any judgment against
said associationuntilafterthe expiration of three months
from the rendition thereof, and then only to be levied
upon the property of such persons as shall have failedto
pay theirpro rata of such lossor damage and costs; and

the production of a receiptfrom the treasurerof said
associationforsuch pro rataor the payment thereof,the

release the person to whom such receipt was given or

ment or execution .
§ 8. The directorsshall, afterreceiving notice of loss or
damage by any member ,and ascertainingthe sum of, after
the renditionof any judgment aforesaidagainst said asso-
ciation for loss or damage ,settleand determine the sum
to be paid by the several members thereof as herein pro-
vided, as their respective portion of said loss,and give
each of the members notice in such manner as the by -laws
shall have prescribed; and the sum to be paid by each
member shallalways be in proportionto theircertificates
of insurance ,to be ascertained by the proportion that the

of the property insured,and shallbe paid to the treasurer
within thirtydays next aftersaid notice; and ifany mem-
ber shall, forthe space of thirtydays afterreceiving such
notice, neglect or refuse to pay the sum assessed upon

directors may sue for and collectthe amount assessed,
with cost and ten per cent. damages .
§9 . The aggregate valuation of the property insured
by said association shall not be less than one hundred
thousand dollars , nor shall it exceed five hundred thou-
sand dollars,nor shallsaid associationtake any riskupon
any dwelling or other building for a sum exceeding three
thousand dollars .
§ 10. All property insured by said associationshall be
liableas herein provided untilalloutstanding lossesshall
have been paid , and until the owner thereof shall have
withdrawn hisinsuranceby a writtenapplicationto the
board of directors, when he shall be entitled to a certifi-
cate of discharge,signed by the presidentand counter-
signed by the secretary,which shallshow that allexisting
demands againsthim have been paid : Provided,however,
That alltransferof such propertyshalloperateas a re-
leaseof the same under the provisionsof thisact,or to

all subsequent liabilities, unless the purchaser thereof
shall make application to the board of directors for a
continuance of said insurance within ten days from the
date of saidtransfer,in which event such purchaser shall
be substituted to all the rights of his vendor under this
act,and the said propertyheld liableas hereinprovided;
and the provisionsof thissectionshallapply as well to
personal representativesand guardians as to purchasers
of such property .

CHAPTER 1435 .
AN ACT to amend an act,entitled " An act to renew and amend the charter
of the Camdenvilleand Van BurenTurnpikeRoad ," approvedApril220,
1880, changing the titleof saidaet,and legalizingthe subscriptionof the
Anderson countycourtto thecapitalstockofthe company therebyand
bereby created .
WHEREAS , At the present session of this Legislature
there was passed an act approved April 22d, 1880,en-
titled " An act to renew and amend the charter of the
Camdenvilleand Van Buren Turnpike Road ," when itwas
intended to amend the charter of the Lawrenceburg and
CamdenvilleTurnpike Road Company ,number two ,which
was to enable citizensof Anderson county to form a com-
pany and build and complete a turnpikeroad from the
present constructed terminus of the Lawrenceburg and
Camdenville Turnpike Road , number two , or near that
point,to Van Buren ; and whereas,theAnderson county

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 989
court at itsregular April term , 1880,subscribed stock to
the extent of one thousand dollarsper mile for the build-
ing and completion of said road to Van Buren ,when said
act had not been approved ; and whereas , doubts may
arise as to the legalityof said subscription; for remedy
thereof ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the titleof said act ishereby changed to an
act to amend the charter of the Lawrenceburg and Cam-
denville Turnpike Road Company , number two ; and the
said subscription of the Anderson county court made at
itsApril term , 1880,is hereby legalizedand made binding
on said court and county ,and said subscriptionisdeclared
according to its terms to inure to and to have been made
tothe company created by thisand the act to which this
is an amendment ,and is made legal in all respect as
though it had been subscribed after the passage of this
act; and allordersof the Anderson county courtrelative
to said turnpike road company styled the Lawrenceburg
and Camdenville Turnpike Road ,number two , or the
originalcompany ,as well as the levy made pertainingto
this company , are legalizedand made valid.
§2 . That the powers ,duties,and privilegesconferred
on said Lawrenceburg and Camdenville Turnpike Road
Company ,number two ,or the originalcompany by legis-
lative act, or by the orders of the Anderson county court
under the provision of chapter 103 of Revised Statutes of
Kentucky , are hereby conferred on the company created
by this and the act to which thisisan amendment .
§3. That thisactbe in forcefrom itspassage.
Approved May 5, 1880..
CHAPTER 1436 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Edward Castelloand James W. Ingels, superin-
tendent of poor-house,Bourbon county.
THAT WHEREAS , Margaret Castello and America Ken-
ney ,who are of unsound mind , and were committed to

990 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
theEasternLunaticAsylum at Lexington,Kentucky ; and
whereas, they were, by order of the authoritiesof said
asylum,on July 12, 1878,returnedtothe county of Bour-
bon ,from whence they had been sent to the asylum ,to be
cared forby said county as harmless lunatics,under and
by virtueof the act of the Legislatureof date March 20,
1876, and the amendment to said law of date April 5,
1878 ; and whereas ,Margaret Castellowas committed to
the care of Edward Castello,and America Kenney to the
care of James W. Ingels, superintendentof the poor-
house of Bourbon county,and they have kept,supported,
and cared forthem ever since; and whereas ,the Auditor
paid forsaid keeping up to January 12,1879,and under
a constructionof the law , refusesto pay any more until
a new inquest is held; and whereas , nothing can be
allowed by the court except from the time of the inquest;
and whereas ,said Edward Castello and James W. Ingels
will lose for the keeping of said lunatics from January
12, 1879 ; now , therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be directed
to draw hiswarrant upon the publicTreasury,in favor
of Edward Castello,for one hundred and sixteen dollars
and sixty-six cents,for supporting,nursing,keeping,and
caringforMargaret Castello,a lunatic,from January 12,
1879, to March 12, 1880, being at rate of one hundred
dollars per year ; and he isdirectedto draw his warrant
upon the public Treasury,in favor of James W. Ingels,
superintendentof the poor-house of Bourbon county,for
one hundred and sixteendollars and sixty-six cents, for
supporting, nursing, keeping, and caring for America
Kenney ,from January 12, 1879, to March 12, 1880.
§2 . This to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 5, 1880.

CHAPTER 1457 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Lenoxburgh and Pribble'sCross roads Turnpike
Company , in Pendleton county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Charles F. Bonar ,J. B. Pribble, Wm . Bar-
nard,Darius Jones,T. W. Fossett,C. R. Drake ,and J. С.
Carnes , and theirsuccessors and associates,be , and they
are hereby,constituteda body-corporateand politic,for
the purpose of building and carrying on the business of
a turnpike road company ,under the name and styleof
the Lenoxburgh and Pribble'sCross-road Turnpike Com-
pany ; and by that name and styleshallhave perpetual
succession,and the right to contract and be contracted
with , sue and be sued ; to have a common seal ; and shall
have all other rights, privileges, and powers conferred
upon similarcorporationsby chapterfifty-sixof the Gen-
eralStatutes,so far as the same are applicable thereto.
§2 . That the said company shallhave the power to
constructa turnpike road from Lenoxburgh ,in Bracken
county ,via Pribble'sCross-roads, to the Campbell coun-
ty line, and intersectingthe Alexandria and Persimmon
Grove Road at or near Wesley Chapel .
§3 . That the capitalstock of the company shallnot
exceed thirtythousand dollars.

1026 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 4. That said company shall not subject itselfto a
greater liabilitythan fifteenthousand dollars.
§5 . That theprivatepropertyof stockholdersshallbe
exempt from corporate debts.
§6 . That the affairsof the company shall be managed
by a president and four directors, one of whom shall act
as secretary and treasurer,who shall be annually elected
by , and from among ,the stockholders, on the firstSatur-
day in April of each year ,and shallhold officeuntiltheir
successors shall have been duly elected; and they shall
have power to fillall vacancies that may occur among
themselves until the succeeding annual election .
§7. That the stockholdersof this company shall meet
and organize at pleasure afterthe passage of thisact.
§ 8. That twenty -fivedollarsshallconstitutea share of
stock in thiscompany , and in the electionof officersand
voting on all important measures ,each share shallcon-
stitutea vote,and can be cast by the holder or by writ-
ten proxy .
§ 9. The company shallmeet at a point most conven-
ientto a majority of the stockholders.
§ 10. That upon the passage of this act any two or
more of the above incorporatorsmay open books for sub-
scriptionstothe capitalstockof saidcompany ,and,after
the organization of the company ,the said president and
directorsshallnot callfor more than thirtyper cent,of
said stock at any one time ,nor shallthey calloftener than
once in sixtydays .
§ 11.That thepresidentand directorsshallhave power
to make such by -laws for the management of the affairs
of saidroad as they may deem necessary: Provided, That
the same are not inconsistent with this act or with the
laws of this State .
§12 . That so soon as two and one half miles of con-
tinuous road shallhave been completed and accepted by
the county commissioners ,the said president and directors
may erect a toll-gate and collecttollsnot to exceed the
amount allowed by law .
§ 13. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 5, 1880.

CHAPTER 1460 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Sharpsburg and Henry's Mill Turnpike Road
Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
§1 . That Jacob Henry , Dr. E. Roe , B. F. Haggard ,
Waller Sharp, and William Weathers , and theirassoci-
atesand successors,be,and they are hereby,constituted
a body -potiticand corporate, under the name and style
of the Sharpsburg and Henry's MillTurnpike Road Com-
pany; with power to sue and be sued,contractand be
contractedwith,plead and be impleaded. Said company
tohave a capitalstocknot to exceed threethousand dol-
lars,divided into shares of fiftydollarseach .
§2 . The saidcompany shallhave power to constructa
turnpikeroad from or near Sharpsburg,in Bath county,
to Jacob Henry's mill, a distance of about three miles.
§3 .The incorporatorsnamed above shallopen books
of subscription,and afterhalf the capitalstockshallhave
been subscribed,the company shallorganize by selecting
from theirnumber a president and treasurerand a board
of five directors; ten days ' previous notice of the time
and place of the meeting having been given by posting

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1033
notices,or by publishingthe same in the county paper.
Each stockholder shall have one vote for each share of
stock owned by him ,and may vote in person or by proxy .
§4 They may have power to acquire the land over
which the road is to be built by purchase , donation ,or
writ of ad quod damnum ,as well as for the residence of
gate-keepers. They shallhave access to timber, rock
quarries, gravel pits, & c.,necessary to the construction
of said road on the same conditions .
§5 . The grade of said road , as also the width and the
depth of gravel,shallbe under the controlof the board
of directors. They may erect a toll-gate at or near B. F.
Haggard's house ,and charge tollsboth ways ; the rate of
tollnot to exceed the rate charged by similar roads.
§6 . The board of directorsmay require the treasurer,
before entering upon the discharge of his duties, to exe-
cute bond to said company , in such sums as they may
require, conditioned on the faithfulperformance of his
duties .
§ 7. The board of directorsmay establishsuch by -laws
as are necessary for the management of said road , pro-
vided they shall not be in conflictwith the Constitution
and laws of the State .
§ 8. Beforethecounty courtof Bath county shallmake
an appropriationto saidroad,as prescribedby the turn-
pike laws of Bath county,thequestionshallbe submitted
to the qualifiedvoters of said county at any regular elec-
tion, and if a majority of the votersof said county voting
at said electionvote in favor of said appropriation,the
county court shallproceed to make saidappropriations,
and make the necessarylevyforthe payment of same .
§ 9. The officersfirstelected shall hold their officefor
one year after their election, and until their successors
are duly elected and qualified.
§ 10. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved Mау 5, 1880.

CHAPTER 1466 .
AN ACT for the benefitof school districtNo. 24 , in Lincoln county .
WHEREAS , The trusteesof school districtNo 24 ,in Lin-
coln county,were not able to employ a teacherforfive
months during the school year 1879-80 , for the public
money apportioned to it, which was only seventy-eight
dollars and eight cents; and whereas ,they did employ a
teacher for four months , who well and faithfullytaught
the schoolforthatlengthof time; now ,therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That school districtNo. 24 ,in Lincoln county ,shall
be ,and ishereby , entitledto the amount of public school
money apportioned to itfor the school year ending June
30 , 1880, for the four months ' term , the same as if said
school had been taught fivemonths ; which sum shallbe
paid upon the draft of the commissioner of Lincoln coun-
ty as other public school money ispaid .
§ 2. This act shall take effect and be in force from its
passage .
Approved May 5, 1880 .

CHAPTER 1478 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Joseph T. Benton , of Ohio county.
WHEREAS ,Executions issued from the officeof the clerk
of the Franklin circuit court in favor of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky against Q. C. Shanks ,late sheriffof
Ohio county ,and hissureties,forthe revenue due from said
county for the years 1858,1859, and 1860 ; and whereas ,
saidShanks made largepayments to the Auditor of Pub-
licAccounts,which payments were not indorsedon said
executions which were afterwards issued; and whereas ,
the house and lot of Joseph T. Benton ,in Hartford , was
levied upon and sold under one of said executions, and
was bought by the agent of the State; said Benton being
one of the sureties of said Shanks as sheriffas aforesaid ;
and whereas ,it appears that the debt and interestdue
from said Shanks to the Commonwealth , exclusive of
damages ,had been paid prior to the sale of said house
and lot; and whereas ,itappears that the property of said
Benton was soldfordamages exclusively; now ,therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That all right,title, and interest of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky in and to the house and lotof Joseph
T. Benton ,situated and being in Hartford and adjacent

1058 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
thereto,which lot contains about six acres, acquired by
virtue of the levy of said execution and the sale there-
under ,be ,and the same ishereby , released.
§2 . This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 5,1880.

eight years and twelve days; and it furtherappear-

of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 1519 .
AN ACT to change the boundary line of the town of Beaver Dam , Ohio
county .

of Kentucky :
§1 . That the southern boundary of the town of Beaver
Dam , in Ohio county ,be so changed as to run with the
present lineof the Elizabethtown and Paducah Railroad ,
and that the northern boundary line be so changed as to
run with the Ceralvo road to where it intersects with the
Hartford and Cromwell road; thence across said road , on
the line between a lot known as the William Austin lot,
and the school -house lot ; thence with the line between
lands of J. P. Barnard and Samuel Sanders , to the north
and south line of said town of Deaver Dam ; said change
in the northern boundary of said town excluding only the
William Austin lot and the lands of Samuel Sanders .
§2 . This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved May 5, 1880 .
CHAPTER 1520 .
AN ACT to authorize the Simpson county court to levy a tax for bridge
purposes .
WHEREAS ,The recent rains have caused the high waters
in Red river, in Simpson county, to destroyand wash
away the bridge over said river at a point known as
Price's mill on said river; and whereas ,the ford at said
point isdifficultto crossin time of high water,and the
businessinterestof the county, and especiallyin that
locality,demand a bridge over said river:
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
ofKentucky :
§1 . That the Simpson county court,a majorityof the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1087
justicesbeing present and concurring therein,are em-
powered to levy an additionaltax of twenty-fivecentson
each tithablefortheyears1880and 1881,to be appliedto
the erection of the bridge aforesaid.
§ 2. This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage.
Approved May 5 , 1880.

CHAPTER 1527 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof Richmond and LancasterTurnpike Com-
pany .
WHEREAS , Under the twenty -seventh section of an act,
entitled"An act to incorporatethe Paris, Winchester,
and Kentucky River Turnpike Road Company and the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1095
North Middletown Turnpike Road Company , and for
other purposes," approved February 25, 1848,a compa-
ny was organized ,and sufficientstock taken ,and money
paid in, to construct a macadamized road from the town
of Richmond to Lancaster ,that part of the road situated
in Madison county being under the control and manage-
ment of the presidentand directorsresidentin Madison
county , and the extent of thisbranch of said road being
from Richmond to Paint Lick creek; now ,therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That in addition to the powers ,privileges,rights,
and immunities granted to the presidentand board of
directorsof the Richmond and Lancaster Turnpike Road
Company by thecharteraforesaid,thatsaidpresidentand
board of directorsshallhave the power to erectanother
gate on theirroad at or near the point where the Kirks-
villeand Goggin's Ferry Turnpike Road intersectsthe
same ,and to placethetoll-house erectedforthatpurpose
upon the land granted to them for a right of way for the
construction of their road , or so much of said land as
they may deem necessary,and to erect two poles across
their road ,one on each side of the entrance of the said
Kirksvilleand Goggin's Ferry Turnpike Road . At such
gate they shallbe permittedto collectthe same ratesof
tollcollected at the two other gates on the line of their
road , and to be regulated according to the rates now
authorized by the charter to which thisis an amendment .
§ 2. This act to be in force from itspassage .
Approved May 5, 1880.
CHAPTER 1528 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Dugansvilleand Forsythe'sMillTurnpike Road
Company , in Mercer county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1.That a company shallbe,and the same ishereby,
incorporated, to constructa road, on the macadamized
plan,from the Bloomfieldroad,near Cyrus Graham's,in

1096 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Washington county ,viaDugansville and Forsythe's Mill,
intersectingthe Harrodsburg and Frankfort pike near
Vance Wilson's,in Mercer county,on or near the survey
made by James H. Lapsley,under the name and styleof
Dugansville and Forsythe's Mill Turnpike Road Com-
pany ; and by that name and stylemay sue and be sued,
contract and be contracted with,plead and be impleaded ,
and use and have a common seal, and alteror amend the
same at pleasure .
§2 . The capital stock of said company shall be four
thousand five hundred dollars, to be increased at the
pleasure of the company ,to be divided into shares of fifty
dollars each .
§3 . That George M. Forsythe, Ben . Sanders , William
Brown , and J. W. Robinson are hereby appointed com-
missioners,whose duty itshallbe to open books forsub-
scriptionof stock at such places as said commissioners
may designate ; and as soon as four thousand five hun-
dred dollarsissubscribed,they shallgive ten days ' notice
in the most public places in the vicinityof the line of the
road of a meeting of the stockholdersfor the purpose of
electing president and five directorsof said company ,a
majority of whom shallbe competent to do business,and
to do allthings authorized by this act to be done by the
president and directorsof said company ; and the man-
agement of the fiscaland prudentialaffairsof saidcom-
pany shallbe confidedto said presidentand directors,
and theirsuccessorsin office,to be chosen annually at
such timesand placesas thesaidpresidentand directors,
from time to time,may appoint,and who shallcontinue
in officeforone year,and untiltheirsuccessorsare elected
and qualified .
§ 4. That no person shallbe eligibleas presidentor
director who is not the owner on his own name of one
or more shares of stock in said road .
§ 5. The presidentand directorsshallhave the power
of appointinga treasurer,gate-keeper,and allother offi-
cersor agentsdeemed necessaryto effectthe purposes of
thisact,and to remove the same at pleasure; they shall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1097
have power to require of the treasurer, and allother offi-
cersor agentsappointed by them, bond and securityin
such penalties as they may require,conditioned for the
faithfulperformance of the duties incumbent on them as
such .
§ 6. The commissioners herein appointed shallprocure
a book or books , and the subscribers to the stock of said
company shallenterintothe followingobligationin said
book , viz: We , whose names are hereto subscribed, do
promise to pay the president and directorsof the Dugans-
villeand Forsythe'sMill Turnpike Road Company the sum
of fiftydollars for each and every share of stock in said
company set opposite our names , in such manner and
proportion,and atsuch times,as shallbe by them required
under the law incorporating said company ,to be collected
as other debts. Witness our hands this, the day of
, 1880 .
§7 . That said road shall not be less than thirty-five feet
wide ; the width of the grade shallnot be lessthan eigh-
teen feet clear of ditches, and the metal not lessthan four-
teen feet wide , and shall be graded to an elevation not
exceeding three degrees in any part thereof. That when-
ever fivemilesof said road are completed,the company
may erect a toll-gate and charge tollsthereat, at the rate
of tollnot exceeding that now authorized by law on roads
made under the general turnpike laws of thisState.
§8 . Said company shallhave allthe rightsand priv-
ilegesconferredby the general laws of the State upon
similar companies for procuring and condemning the right
of way and materialsforsaidroad,the violationof their
chartered rights, and the safety and preservation of their
property .
§9 . That any one owning one or more shares in said
company shallbe exempted from working on public
roads ,and each stockholder shall be entitledto one vote
for each share held by him or her in the road .
§10 This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
LOC . L , VOL . II- 70
Approved May 5, 1880 .
