CHAPTER 1529 .
AN ACT to establisha citygovernment forthe town of Carlisle.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That so much of the county of Nicholas as is em-
braced within the following boundary , viz: Beginning at
a point where the railroadcrossesBrushy Fork creek,
nearly opposite the residence of W. L. Piper; thence in a
southeast direction to the southwest corner of Hamilton
Harris' lands; thence eastwardly so as to include the
lands of RobertRobinson,John P. Norvell,Thompson S.
Parks ,E. D. Baxter , and Thomas A. Dorsey , and those
to whom saidDorsey has latelysoldlots,to the eastern
boundary of saidDorsey'slands; thence northwith said
eastern boundary of Thos . Dorsey's land , including the
land latelybought of said Dorsey by W. P. Ross , to the
division fence between Chas . Mathers and Thomas Ken-
nedy ; thence with said fence to the northern boundary of
saidMathers'land; thence in a westerlydirection,includ-
ing the grounds and houses of Thos . P. Trueman , and
the lot of Lucien Mann and Joseph Trueman , leaving
Henryvilleto the northof said line,embracing the resi-
dence of Green Cheathem ; thence southwest, including
the cemetery and the lands of Jno . W. Barnett and Mrs.
Hukle ,to the beginning ; and the persons residing within
said boundaries are hereby created a body -politicand
corporate,by the name and styleof the cityof Carlisle;
and by that name may have perpetualsuccession, sue
and be sued in all courtsof law or equity; may make
contracts and purchase and hold such lands and tene-

use of said corporation, and may selland convey the

§ 1. All officersand agentsemployed by or by author-

§ 2. Itshallbe unlawful for any person or persons,by
agent or otherwise,to sellintoxicating,spirituous,vinous,
or malt liquorsbehind screens, frostedwindows ,or any
other device designated or intended to protect the seller

§3 . Itshallbe unlawful for any person or persons to
sell, lend, or give vinous, spirituous,or malt liquors,or
any mixture of either, to a person under twenty -one

§4 . All places where intoxicating liquors are sold in

taurants,drug stores, groceries, coffee-houses , cellars,or

ituous,or vinous liquors are sold in violationof thisact,
shallbe shut up and abated as public nuisances,upon

ished as herein provided .

recoveredinactionof debt in the mayor's court,orin the
court of a justice of the peace in said city.

property,ormeans ofsupport,by any intoxicatedperson,or
in consequence of the intoxication,habitual or otherwise,

intoxication,in whole or in part, of such person or per-

recovered,as a feme sole; and alldamages recovered by a

or his or her parent , guardian , or next friend, as the

der any leaseor contractofrent,upon thepremiseswhere

CHAPTER 1531 .
AN ACT to regulatethe allowance of claims against the coun'y of Law-
rence, and regulatingappeals from the allowance of the same .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That hereafterall claims against the county of
Lawrence ,to be allowed or passed on by the court of
levy and claims,shallbe made out by the claimant and
filedwith the clerkof thecounty courtat leastten days
before the commencement of the term of said court ; and

1140 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
no claim shallbe considered or allowed unless so filed;
but this shall not apply to the pay of the court for their
servicesduring the term .
§2 . That hereafterany claim presentedby any one,
where the amount is twenty dollarsor more ; and when
the amount of said claim is allowed , or any part of it is
allowed , the county of Lawrence shall have the right to
appeal to the circuitcourt,and thence to the Court of
Appeals , as provided in titlesixteen,chapter two , of the
Civil Code , to the circuitcourt and to the Court of Ap-
peals as provided by the Code of Practice, except no
bond shall be required of the county on any of its ap-
peals.
§3 . This act to be in force from itspassage .
Approved May 5 , 1880 .

CHAPTER 1535 .
AN ACT to empower the county court of Fayette county to purchase stock
in turnpike roads already completed, and make subscriptionto the capi-
tal stock in turnpike roads in Fayette county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be lawful for the county court of
Fayette county , a majority of the justicesof said county
concurring ,to purchase stock in turnpike roads already
completed, and make subscriptionof stock in turnpike
road companies for the construction of such roads within
said county : Provided, That said subscription shall not
exceed one thousand dollars per mile in each road to be
constructed .
§2 . That no money to be subscribedby said county
court shallbe paid over to any turnpike road company ,
or any person whatever ,untileach mile of the road sub-
scribed for shall have been completed ,or until the said
court shallbe fullysatisfied,in open court,thatthe com-
pany constructing the road are fullyable to complete it
with the aid of such subscription.
§3 . The said county court shallhave the power , a ma-
jorityof thejusticesconcurring,tolevyan ad valorem tax
upon allthe taxablepropertyin saidcounty,not exceed-
ing cents upon one hundred dollars,to raise a sum
sufficientto pay offand dischargethe said subscription

1142 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and purchase of stock; and said tax may be imposed
yearlyuntilthe debt isfullypaid.
§4 . The tax shall be collectedby the sheriffof said
county in the same manner ,and for the same compensa-
tion , as is now fixed for collecting the State revenue .
§5 . All acts and parts of acts in conflictwith this act
are hereby repealed .
§ 6. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 5 , 1880 .

CHAPTER 1537 .

of Kentucky ;

porate limits of the town of Petersburg,Boone county ,

judge ,town marshal , and fivetrusteesfor said town .

CHAPTER 1547 .
AN ACT to authorizethe county court of Washing on county to issuethe
bonds of said county to build turnpike roads and bridges in said
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That thecounty judge of Washington county shall
have power to issuethe bonds of said county ,in any sum
not exceeding fiftythousand dollars,forthe purpose of
building such turnpike roads and bridges in said county

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1155
as the county judge and magistrates,when all such are
present and a majority concurring, may deem right and
proper for the best interestof the county .
§2 . That said bonds shall be issued in sums not less
than one hundred dollars nor more than one thousand
dollars, with coupons attached, and shall bear interestat
the rate of six per cent.per annum ,payable semi -annu-
ally,and payable on the firstof January and July of each
year ; and if said coupons are not paid at maturity,then
saidcoupons shall bear six per cent.interestuntil paid ;
and said bonds and interestshallbe payable at the First
National Bank of Springfield, Kentucky , and shall fall
due in twenty years from the time they are issued; but
the county may redeem said bonds within five years ,or
any part of them , giving notice in some newspaper
twenty days of such bonds by their number as they
desire to redeem .
§3 . The levying court of Washington county is em-
powered and required to levy taxes on the property of
the county ,both real, personal, and mixed , sufficientto
pay the interest as it falisdue , and to create a sinking
fund to pay off said bonds as they falldue .
§ 4. None of said bonds shallbe sold below par,and
the money so raised from the sale of said bonds shall be
used for the purpose of building turnpike roads and
bridges, and for no other purpose .
§5 . Before said bonds shall be issued, or be of any
legalforce whatever ,said county judge shall,aftergiving
twenty days ' notice to the voters of said county , submit
the question to the qualifiedvoters of said county at a
regularelectionheld for said county ,and he shallcause a
column to be opened in the poll-books for and against
saidtax ; and if a majority of the legal voters voting at
said election shall vote in favor of said tax, then said
bonds may be issued,and not otherwise.
§6 . And for the expenses of printing and issuing said
bonds,and allotherincidentalexpenses connected with
theissuingof saidbonds,the county courtshallmake an
appropriated to pay for.

roads or bridges,as contemplated by this act, said con-
tractor shall execute bond or bonds before said county
court,with good and sufficientsureties,for the faithful
performance of such work as he may have contracted to
do .
§ 8. And the county court of Washington county may
issue a part of the bonds contemplated by this act,as
many as in theirjudgment may be needed in any one
year ,and cause a vote to be taken on such issue at any
general election; but in no event shall they ,in any one

sage .

CHAPTER 1557 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled"An act relatingto opening and re-
pairing roads in Boone county," approved January 29, 1874 .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act,entitled"An act relatingto opening
and repairingroads in Boone county ," approved January
29 , 1874 ,be ,and the same ishereby , amended by adding
thereto the following section: That hereafter the public
roads of Boone county , within the meaning of said act,
shallinclude the streetsof the towns in said county in all
cases where any such streetor streetsare a continuance
through the town of any public road or roads leading to
or through said town ; and the county court of said coun-
ty, afterthe passage of thisact,shall, as soon as conven

1164 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ient, at some regular or special term .by order of said
court,so far alterthe present road districtsof saidcounty
as to place said streetsin such road districtsas may be
most convenient ; and the supervisors of such districts
shallthen keep said streetsin order as other roads in
theirdistricts; and said streetsshall, to all intents and
purposes,be a part of the public roads of said county,
within the meaning of said road law , and be kept in re-
pair in the same way and with the same resourcesas
other public roads in said county. The county court
may ,without the presence of the justicesof the peace
of said county , make the alterationin the road districts
herein authorized, and no other streetsin any towns ,ex-
cept such streets as are herein embraced , and no alleys
in any of said towns , shall be considered or treated as
public roads,any law to the contrary notwithstanding .
§2 . That no street or streets in any town in said
county,covered by the charterof any incorporatedroad
company ,shall be entitledto the benefit of this act,or
consideredas embraced by the provisionshereof: Pro-
vided, Such company has organized and is acting under
its charter .
§3 . That section two of said act shall not be so con-
strued as to require the county court to make the levy
thereinprovided for annually; but said court,having
made the levy under said section,the same shallbe an-
nuallycollectedand accountedforaccordingly,as by law
required .
§4 . That the settlement of the surveyor of roads with
the county judge shall,in each case,cover all his acts
and doings as such surveyorfor the year precedinghis
settlement, and up to the date of his settlement, and of
allmatters not before settledby him ; and ifany surveyor
fail to come forward and make settlement at the time
fixedby law ,the county judge shallbring him to a settle-
ment by rule and attachment,or by attachment without
the rule,at the discretionof the county judge . This sec-
tion shallalso apply to any surveyorwhose time may
have expired before he makes his settlement, as well as
to such as may be in office,and also to any and allsur

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1165 .
veyors heretofore appointed under said law , who have
failed to make their settlement, and to those now in
office,and such as may hereafterbe appointed.
§5 . There shall be no distinctionmade between the
citizenand property of the town and county in levying
and collectingsaid taxes and levies in said law hereby
amended .
§ 6. This act shall be in force from itspassage , and its
provisionsshallbe immediately applied, so that said
towns may receive its benefits from the present levies
and taxes .
Approved May 6, 1880.
CHAPTER 1558 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Kentucky ContractCompany .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That there is hereby incorporatedthe Kentucky
Contract Company ,with power to sue and be sued,and
contract and be contracted with, and with allother pow-
ers incident to corporations. The stock of said company
shall be divided into shares of one hundred dollars each .
The capitalstockshallnot exceed one hundred thousand
dollars ; and said corporation may begin business when
fiftyshares of stock shall have been subscribed for. R.
S. Veech or E. F. Trabue are authorized to open books
forsubscriptionof stockat any placein Louisville. The
said subscribersmay ,by by -law ,prescribethe number of
directors. The officersshall be a president, secretary,
and treasurer. The board of directorsshallbe chosen by
the stockholders'meeting,which shallbe on thefirstMon-
day of April in each and every year .
§2 . Said corporation is empowered to lease, operate,
or contract for the construction of any railroads out of
the State of Kentucky .
§3 . This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage
Approved May 6 , 1880.

CHAPTER 1559 .
AN ACT to authorizeturnpike roads to make certain contractswith each
other .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That turnpike road companies whose roads form
continuous linesmay make with each other the following
contracts: They may consolidatetheirmanagement ,stock,
and profits,eitheror any . They may leasetheirroads,or
any part thereof,to and from each other. They may give
to the consolidated or leased roads a common name ; but
none of these contracts may be made ,except a majority
in number and interest of the stockholders of the con-
tracting roads assent thereto,at some stated or called
meeting of stockholders. Called meetings shallbe called
by the presidentand board of directorsof the company ,
and noticeof the time and place and the purpose of said
meeting shallbe given by postingat fivepublic places
along the line of the road ,for two weeks , written or
printed noticessetting forthsuch facts,and by inserting
saidnoticein threeissuesof some weekly paper published
in the county or counties of said roads; and ifthere be no
weekly paper ,then in any daily paper issued or circulated
therein. Said noticeshallbe signedby thepresidentand
secretary .
§2 . Roads separated only by the improved streetof a
town or village shall, for the purposes of this act, be
deemed continuous .
§3 . No contract made by virtueof thisact shallin any
way impair or affectthe charter or other rightsof thecon-
tractingcompanies ; and this act shallnot be construed to
enable turnpike roads running parallelwith each otherto
make the consolidation contemplated herein : Provided,
This actshallnot apply to roads in which theStateowns
stock .
§ 4. This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved May 6 , 1880.

CHAPTER 1589 .
AN ACT to extend the time for lodging in the clerk's officeof the Court of
Appeals a certifiedtranscriptof the case of the Commonwealth ofKen-
tucky vs.Sidney Greer , latelypending in the Floyd criminalcourt.
WHEREAS ,It is represented to this General Assembly
that before the record of the case of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky vs.Sidney Greer ,charged with the killingof
John Clark,latelypending in the Floyd criminal court,
could be copied and filedin the officeof the clerk of the
Court of Appeals , the time in which itcould be so filed
and an appeal prosecutedhad expired,and many good
citizensof said county believing and representing that
said Greer should not have been convicted upon said
charge , therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the time be extended until the firstday of
June ,1880,in which saiddefendantGreer may lodge in
the clerk'sofficeof the Court of Appeals a certifiedtran-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1207
scriptofsaidrecord; and iffiledwithinsaidtime,itshall
have the same effectas iffiledwithinsixtydays after
the judgment ,and the appeal shallbe docketed ,progress,
and be treated and triedas iffiledwithin said sixtydays .
§ 2. This act shall take effectand be in force from and
after itspassage .
Approved May 6 ,1880 .
CHAPTER 1591 .
AN ACT to amend the severalacts in relationto the town of Carlisle,
Nicholas county , approved March 16 , 1876 .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That an act, entitled"An act to furtheramend the
several acts in relationto the cityof Carlisle,in Nicholas
county," approved March 16, 1876, be, and the same is
hereby ,amended as follows,viz: It shall be the duty of
the common council of said cityof Carlisle, on the fourth
Saturday in May ,1880,to submit to the qualifiedvoters
within the corporate limits of said city the question
whether or not the common council of said city shall
grant coffee-house licenseor issue license to vend spirit-
uous ,vinous , or malt liquors within said city. A poll-
book shallbe opened by the judges and clerksof said
electionto be held,and the question,for licenseor against
license,shallbe propounded to each voterwho may vote
thereat; and the vote, as cast,shall be kept by the clerk
at saidelectionin appropriatecolumns . The vote upon
the question so submitted to the voters shallbe compared
and certifiedas other electionsin said city are required
to be compared and certified; and if at said election a
majorityof the qualifiedvotersvoting at said election
shallvote in favor of granting license as aforesaid,then
the common council of said cityare authorized and em-
powered with fullpower and authorityto grant license
to any person or persons to keep a coffee-house or vend ,
by the small, spirituous, vinous, or malt liquorswithin

1208 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
thecorporatelimitsof saidtown : Provided,however, That
said person or persons applying for licenseshall have
good moral character,and shallpresent to saidcommon
councila petition,askingforlicense,signedby a majority
of the citizensresidentor doing business upon the street
where he or they propose to locate and conduct said cof-
fee-house , and who shall take the oath and give bond
similar to the oath and bond required of tavern -keepers.
and none others .
§2 . That said councilmen shall not grant license to
any person or personsfora lesssum than fiftydollarsnor
more than two hundred dollarsfor each year , and shall
exercise their discretionin fixingthe amount of license
withinthe aforesaidlimits,which amount must be paid
before the licenseshall be granted ,and under no circum-
stances shalllicensebe granted fora longer time than one
year .
§3 . That said applicant under thisact shall be liable
to allthe finesand penalties and forfeituresthat tavern-
keepers are under existinglaws ,for violationof the bond
or oath, which penalty shall be recovered in the same
manner , and applied to the same purposes ,that finesand
forfeituresagainst tavern-keepers are recovered and ap-
plied.
§ 4. That all money paid for licenseunder the provis-
ions of thisact,shallbe applied to the liquidationof the
debts of saidcity,and the improvement of the streets,& c.
§5 . If the common council of Carlisleviolate any of
the provisionsof thisact,upon conviction,said council
shallbe fined in the sum of not lessthan fiftydollarsnor
more than one hundred dollars for each offense, to be re-
covered by warrant before any court having jurisdiction;
and said money , should there be any from fines, shallbe
applied to the same purpose as in section four.
§ 6. All acts or parts of acts in conflictwith the pro-
visions of this act are hereby repealed; but nothing in
the foregoing act shall be so construed as to repeal any
section,or any partof a section,of an act,entitled" An
act to establisha citygovernment forthe town of Car

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1209
lisle;" but said charter shall remain in full force and
effect .
§7 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 6,1880 .
CHAPTER 1593 .
AN ACT to amend an act,entitled"An act authorizingthecountyjudge
of Rockcastle county to issuebonds and fund the county debt at a less
rateper cent.," known as the court-house bonds of saidcounty.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . The county judge of Rockcastlecounty shall, at
the next annual meeting of the court of claims for said
county,report,in writing, to said court what amount of
bonds he has sold,to whom sold,the rate per cent,that
each bond bears,and what dispositionhe has made of
the old bonds redeemed ; also, what length of time the
bonds issuedrun, and when sold,and at what pricesold
for, under and by authority of said act.
§ 2. If itshallappear from said report that the county
judge has not fullycomplied with the provisionsof said
act,then it shall be the duty of said court of claims to
elect two of their body ,who shall, in conjunction with
said judge ,proceed to sellthe bonds of said county ,and
redeem the outstanding bonds as provided for in said act,
and make report to the next annual meeting of the court
of claims of their acts .
§3 . All actsand parts of actsin conflictwith thisare
hereby repealed.
§ 4. This act shall be in force and effect from and after
itspassage .
Approved May 6,1880.

1880 .

of Kentucky :

equally between and among the districtsin which said

missioners to levy an ad valorem tax on allof the prop-

ner,and with likegood surety, as the bond of the treas-
urer of saidboard; and saidsheriffand hissuretiesshall

section(11) eleven of said act be so amended that said

sage .
